### M8 Obito Kamui Special Jutsu 1 Runtime Slice

- Reconnected the first real Obito Mangekyo Kamui skill entry:
  - Added the client `specialjutsu1` key mapping on `R`, preserving the old key translation id and sending press/release state through `SpecialJutsuKeyMessage`.
  - `SpecialJutsuKeyMessage` now accepts keys `1..3`; key 1 is offered to `ObitoKamuiHandler` before falling back to the existing Biju special jutsu 2/3 path.
  - Added server-side `ObitoKamuiHandler` with no client imports, keyed to `ModItems.MANGEKYOSHARINGANOBITOHELMET`.
- Restored a first-pass old `ProcedureKamuiJikukanIdo` behavior surface:
  - Plain `R` starts/stops `kamui_intangible`, keeps `noPhysics` and `noClipFlag` active while held, cancels incoming attacks through `LivingAttackEvent`, purges harmful effects on activation, and drains the old `1 chakra/tick` upkeep.
  - Shift+`R` starts `kamui_teleport`, drains the old `8 chakra/tick` upkeep, emits `PORTAL_SPIRAL` particles and `kamuisfx`, and on release toggles the looked-at entity into/out of Kamui through the existing `KamuiDimension.toggleEntity(...)` bridge; no entity hit falls back to self travel.
  - State uses the legacy tag names `kamui_intangible`, `kamui_teleport`, and `kamui_timer` through the current synced player variable path so debug commands and future render/HUD hooks can observe it.
- Added M8 diagnostics:
  - `/narutoport m8_world obito_ready` equips an owned Obito Mangekyo helmet, fills battle/chakra resources, ensures the Kamui platform, and spawns a named target armor stand.
  - `/narutoport m8_world obito_state` reports helmet presence, intangible/teleport/timer state, `noPhysics`, `noClipFlag`, chakra, dimension, and Kamui return tag.
  - `/narutoport m8_world obito_intangible_press`, `/narutoport m8_world obito_teleport_press`, and `/narutoport m8_world obito_key1_release` exercise the server handler without requiring a client key press.
- Scope note:
  - This is a runnable Obito Kamui bridge, not full 1.12 parity. Remaining old details include sharingan blindness gates, owner-vs-non-owner chakra multipliers, helmet durability damage, FOV overlay, in-Kamui grab behavior, self-target/block exact targeting, and partial-teleport health damage math.
  - Current teleport targeting sends entities or the player through the existing Kamui dimension bridge; raw block transfer remains available via `transfer_smoke` but is not yet wired into key-release targeting.

### M8 Obito Mangekyo Helmet Semantics Slice

- Replaced the plain Obito Mangekyo helmet registration with a dedicated `ObitoMangekyoHelmetItem`:
  - Added a small Obito-specific armor material preserving the old helmet durability factor (`13 * 1024` for the helmet slot), defense value `2`, enchantment value `0`, and toughness `5.0`.
  - Restored the old red display name styling, Kamui/Susanoo tooltip lines, and Obito helmet armor texture path.
  - Creative owner assignment now runs from `inventoryTick` when the stack has no `player_id`, matching the old `ItemDojutsu.Base` creative-owner fallback.
- Restored old Dojutsu/Obito item-layer behavior:
  - Foreign-owner Obito helmets apply the old 60-second Blindness penalty once per owner id and write `lastWornForeignDojutsu` on the player.
  - Wearing the helmet updates `mostRecentlyHadAnyDojutsu` each armor tick.
  - Wearing the helmet in Kamui grants `mayfly`; leaving Kamui or removing the helmet revokes only flight granted by this item and leaves creative/spectator flight alone.
  - Active Kamui teleport or future `susanoo_activated` state damages the helmet every 2 ticks for the owner and every tick for non-owners.
- Tightened Obito Kamui handler integration:
  - Chakra costs now come from `ObitoMangekyoHelmetItem.getIntangibleChakraUsage(...)` and `getTeleportChakraUsage(...)`, restoring the owner/non-owner multiplier (`1/8 chakra per tick` for owner, doubled for non-owner).
  - `sharingan_blinded` on the head stack now gates Kamui use and clears active Kamui state.
  - Removing the helmet clears Kamui state and revokes item-granted Kamui flight.
- Expanded diagnostics:
  - `/narutoport m8_world obito_state` now reports owner status, blindness, `mayfly`, head durability, and computed intangible/teleport costs.
  - Added `/narutoport m8_world obito_blind` and `/narutoport m8_world obito_unblind` to exercise the blindness gate without manual NBT editing.
- Scope note:
  - This closes the obvious Obito helmet item-behavior gap for owner checks, blindness, durability cadence, and Kamui flight. Remaining old behavior is now concentrated in the precise Kamui release flow: FOV overlay, in-Kamui grab path, self/block target disambiguation, raw block targeting, and partial entity teleport damage math.
  - Eternal Mangekyo key-3 Kamui reuse still needs a separate handler path; the cost helpers already recognize the Eternal helmet for that future slice.

### M8 Eternal Mangekyo Kamui Key 3 Slice

- Replaced the plain Eternal Mangekyo helmet registration with a dedicated `EternalMangekyoHelmetItem`:
  - Added an Eternal-specific armor material preserving the old non-damageable item behavior with zero slot durability, defense value `2`, enchantment value `0`, and toughness `5.0`.
  - Restored the old red display name styling, key1 Amaterasu/key2 Susanoo/key3 Kamui tooltip lines, and Eternal helmet armor texture path.
  - Creative owner assignment now runs from `inventoryTick` when the stack has no `player_id`, matching the old Dojutsu creative-owner fallback.
- Restored old Eternal item-layer behavior needed by Kamui:
  - Foreign-owner Eternal helmets apply the same 60-second Blindness penalty path and update the current Dojutsu wear timestamp.
  - Wearing the helmet grants the old short Speed III tick effect and reuses the Kamui-dimension flight grant/revoke behavior.
- Wired Eternal Mangekyo special jutsu 3 into the existing Kamui runtime:
  - `ObitoKamuiHandler` now accepts Obito key1 and Eternal key3, while debug entry points accept either Kamui-capable helmet.
  - Repeated key-press packets are idempotent for active intangible/teleport state, which keeps held key3 packets from resetting the Kamui timer.
  - The shared server tick now keeps/revokes active Kamui state based on either Obito or Eternal helmet presence.
- Expanded diagnostics:
  - Added `/narutoport m8_world eternal_kamui_ready` to equip an owned Eternal Mangekyo helmet, fill battle/chakra resources, ensure the Kamui platform, and spawn a named target armor stand.
  - `/narutoport m8_world obito_state` now reports combined Obito/Eternal Kamui helmet state, including which helmet is currently equipped.
  - `/narutoport m8_world obito_blind` and `obito_unblind` now work with either Obito or Eternal Kamui helmets.
- Scope note:
  - This closes the old Eternal key3 Kamui routing gap and the main Eternal helmet item-behavior gap for owner fallback, foreign-owner blindness, speed tick, texture, tooltip, and Kamui flight.
  - Key1 Amaterasu and key2 Susanoo behavior on Eternal/Obito remain separate follow-up work; this slice only routes Eternal key3 into Kamui.
  - Remaining precise Kamui behavior is still the same as the Obito slice: FOV overlay, self-hit detection, block transfer targeting, in-Kamui grab path, and partial entity teleport damage math.

### M8 Kamui Chunk Generator Slice

- Replaced the temporary flat/empty Kamui dimension generator with a registered 1.20.1 `ChunkGenerator`:
  - Added `KamuiChunkGenerator` and registered `narutomod:kamui_chunk_generator` in the vanilla `Registries.CHUNK_GENERATOR` registry.
  - Updated `data/narutomod/dimension/kamui_dimension.json` to use `narutomod:kamui_chunk_generator` instead of `minecraft:flat`.
  - The generator keeps the old fixed `THE_VOID` biome source, no carvers, no vanilla mob spawning, sea level `63`, min Y `0`, and spawn height aligned to the Kamui platform path.
- Ported the old 1.12 Kamui terrain that was actually enabled at runtime:
  - The old density terrain block in `setBlocksInChunk` and biome surface replacement had been commented out in the 1.12 source, so this slice does not revive that disabled code.
  - The active old `populate` path is restored as deterministic Kamui block island columns: simplex island-height gate, chunk random gate, one or two island centers at Y `55..70`, radius `10..14`, each extending down to Y `1`.
  - Generation writes only the current chunk's 16x16 slice and considers neighboring planned source chunks so island columns can cross chunk borders without unsafe worldgen writes.
- Expanded diagnostics:
  - Added `/narutoport m8_world kamui_terrain_probe`, which scans nearby planned Kamui island chunks, forces a capped number of them, and reports planned chunks plus generated Kamui block counts in Y `1..70`.
- Scope note:
  - This closes the plan's flat-generator placeholder for Kamui terrain. It does not yet add custom client `DimensionSpecialEffects` for black fog/no celestial visuals.
  - The entry platform is still explicitly ensured by `KamuiDimension.ensureEntryPlatform(...)`, which is deliberate because old player-entry logic also placed Kamui blocks around the arrival position.

### M8 Kamui Dimension Visual Effects Slice

- Added a Kamui-specific client `DimensionSpecialEffects` implementation:
  - `KamuiDimensionEffects` returns pure black fog color, no sunrise color, no sky rendering (`SkyType.NONE`), no ground/cloud layer, and constant ambient-light behavior.
  - This maps the old 1.12 `WorldProviderMod#getFogColor(...) -> (0,0,0)`, `isSurfaceWorld=false`, and no celestial presentation onto the 1.20.1 client rendering hook.
- Registered the effects on the Forge client mod bus:
  - `ClientModEvents` now handles `RegisterDimensionSpecialEffectsEvent` and registers `narutomod:kamui_dimension`.
  - `data/narutomod/dimension_type/kamui_dimension.json` now points its `effects` field at `narutomod:kamui_dimension` instead of borrowing `minecraft:the_end`.
- Scope note:
  - The server-side dimension type already carried the old no-rain/no-sleep/no-respawn/fixed ambient light settings; this slice closes the remaining client visual registration path.
  - This still needs an integrated-client visual pass to confirm the in-game sky/fog feel, because the current automated gates are compile/resource/server-load checks only.

### M8 Kamui Precise Release Slice

- Tightened Obito/Eternal Kamui teleport release toward the old `ProcedureKamuiJikukanIdo` behavior:
  - Release now distinguishes self hits, entity hits, block hits, and misses instead of treating every non-entity result as self travel.
  - Entity targets use the old charge-progress formula shape: `timer / (distance * average target edge * ninja-level factor)`.
  - Undercharged living targets now take partial Kamui damage based on max health; sufficiently charged targets still transfer through `KamuiDimension.toggleEntity(...)`.
  - Misses now report no target instead of silently toggling the player.
- Wired block targeting into the existing transfer bridge:
  - A block hit outside Kamui now calls `KamuiDimension.transferBlockDropsToKamui(...)`, preserving the existing loot-table drop path and removing the source block.
  - Unbreakable/air blocks are rejected before transfer.
  - In-Kamui reverse block return is left explicit as not restored yet, because the current bridge only defines source-world-to-Kamui drop transfer semantics.
- Expanded diagnostics:
  - Added `/narutoport m8_world obito_block_ready`, which equips an owned Obito helmet, fills resources, places a stone target along the player's current look vector, ensures the matching Kamui platform, and reports the pre-transfer Kamui item count.
  - `/narutoport m8_world obito_state` now reports nearby Kamui item count so the block-release path can be checked after `obito_teleport_press` + `obito_key1_release`.
- Scope note:
  - This closes the main precise release gaps for entity partial damage, block target transfer, miss handling, and the special self-hit branch.
  - Remaining old Obito-in-Kamui non-sneak grab behavior (`ProcedureGrabEntity`/`ProcedurePullAndHold`), FOV overlay, integrated-client visual/play validation, and reverse block return from Kamui remain separate follow-up work.

  - Run `/narutoport m8_world kamui_ready`, `kamui_toggle`, `kamui_terrain_probe`, and an integrated-client screenshot pass to verify black fog/no sky plus generated island columns.

### M8 Obito In-Kamui Grab Slice

- Restored the old Obito-only Kamui-dimension non-sneak key-1 branch:
  - `ObitoKamuiHandler` now routes Obito key 1 to grab mode when the wearer is already inside Kamui and is not sneaking, matching the old `ProcedureSpecialJutsu1OnKeyPressed -> ProcedureGrabEntity` split.
  - Eternal Mangekyo key 3 keeps using the existing Kamui/intangible path, matching the old Eternal route through `ProcedureKamuiJikukanIdo`.
- Ported the old `ProcedureGrabEntity`/`ProcedurePullAndHold` runtime as a common-side server state:
  - Pressing grab locks the looked-at entity within 4 blocks and stores it by UUID.
  - The server tick maintains the hold because the 1.20 client key packet is edge-triggered for key 1 rather than sent every tick.
  - Item entities and XP orbs are pulled to the player; ordinary entities are held at the 3-block look target; `EarthBlocksEntity` gets the old 5-block/pull-style handling.
  - Releasing the key, losing the helmet, blindness, leaving Kamui, or target invalidation clears the grab and restores the target's original no-gravity flag.
- Expanded diagnostics:
  - Added `/narutoport m8_world obito_grab_ready`, which equips an owned Obito helmet, fills resources, enters Kamui if needed, and spawns a named grab target in front of the player.
  - Added `/narutoport m8_world obito_grab_press`; release reuses `/narutoport m8_world obito_key1_release`.
  - `/narutoport m8_world obito_state` now reports `grab` and the current grabbed entity name/missing state.
- Scope note:
  - This closes the Obito in-Kamui non-sneak grab behavior called out after the precise release slice.
  - Remaining Kamui precision work is FOV overlay, integrated-client play/visual validation, and explicit reverse block return rules from inside Kamui.

### M8 Mud Lake Worldgen Slice

- Restored the old `BlockMud.generateWorld(...)` behavior through 1.20.1 data-driven worldgen:
  - Added `data/narutomod/worldgen/configured_feature/mud_lake.json` using the vanilla `minecraft:lake` feature with `narutomod:mud` as the lake fill block.
  - Set the lake barrier to `minecraft:air` so the feature does not add a new shell block around mud, matching the old non-water/non-lava `WorldGenLakes(block)` path more closely.
  - Added `data/narutomod/worldgen/placed_feature/mud_lake.json` with `rarity_filter` chance `10`, `in_square`, uniform Y `0..255`, and biome filtering.
  - Added `data/narutomod/forge/biome_modifier/add_mud_lake.json` to inject the placed feature into `minecraft:swamp` at the `lakes` generation step, preserving the old `BiomeSwamp` + `chance = 0.1f` scope.
- Expanded diagnostics:
  - Added `/narutoport m8_world mud_lake_probe`, which reports whether the configured and placed mud lake features are present in the server registry.
  - `/narutoport m8_world state` now includes `mud_lake_configured` and `mud_lake_placed` alongside Kamui dimension state.
- Scope note:
  - Natural generation still needs a new swamp chunk to be observed in an integrated client or long-running test world; the automated gate validates datapack loading and registry wiring, not visual lake placement.

### M8 Kamui Reverse Block Return Slice

- Restored the reverse block-drop path for Obito/Eternal Kamui release from inside Kamui:
  - `ObitoKamuiHandler.releaseBlockTarget(...)` now handles block hits in Kamui instead of reporting the old "not restored yet" diagnostic.
  - `KamuiDimension.transferBlockDropsToReturn(...)` extracts the targeted Kamui-side block drops, removes the source block, and spawns the drops in the player's remembered return dimension at the same block coordinates.
  - The existing source-world-to-Kamui block transfer now shares the same drop extraction helper, keeping forward and reverse semantics aligned with the old `ProcedureKamuiTeleportEntity.eBlock(...)` drop-transfer model.
  - Unbreakable/air checks remain in the release path, so Kamui platform blocks and portal-like blocks are not removed by this path.
- Expanded diagnostics:
  - Added `/narutoport m8_world obito_reverse_block_ready`, which equips an owned Obito helmet, fills resources, enters Kamui if needed, places a stone block in front of the player, and reports the remembered return target.
  - The actual release still uses the real path: `/narutoport m8_world obito_teleport_press` followed by `/narutoport m8_world obito_key1_release` while aiming at the prepared Kamui-side block.
- Scope note:
  - This restores the explicit reverse block return rule. Integrated-client validation is still needed for the full aiming/feedback loop and visible item placement in the return dimension.

### M8 Kamui FOV Overlay Slice

- Restored the old Obito/Eternal Kamui teleport FOV behavior on the 1.20.1 client render path:
  - Added `KamuiFovEvents`, a client-only Forge event subscriber for `ViewportEvent.ComputeFov`.
  - The handler reads the synced legacy `kamui_teleport` player variable and only acts while the local player is actively charging Kamui teleport.
  - It locally recomputes the current looked-at target within the old 100 block range and applies the old formula shape, `70 - log(distance) * 15`, clamped to a sane render range.
  - The event only overrides frames where `usedConfiguredFov()` is true, matching the plan's `ViewportEvent.ComputeFov` migration note and the old behavior of adjusting the configured FOV path.
  - Because this is now a per-frame event override rather than a direct game-option mutation, releasing/canceling Kamui naturally restores the player's configured FOV.
- Scope note:
  - This restores the Kamui FOV overlay path called out in the M8 precision backlog.
  - Integrated-client validation is still needed to visually confirm the zoom curve while holding shift+key over near and far entity/block targets.

### M8 SpecialEvent Delayed Spawn Scheduler Slice

- Restored the first piece of the old `SpecialEvent.Save` runtime on 1.20.1:
  - Added a common-side `SpecialEvent` scheduler backed by `NarutomodSavedData.specialEvents(server)`.
  - The queue keeps the old top-level list key `SpecialEvents` and legacy field names such as `Type`, `ID`, `World`, `Start`, `Tick`, and `x0/y0/z0`.
  - Time is keyed off `server.overworld().getGameTime()`, matching the plan's global `SavedData` and world-tick recommendation.
  - Unknown event types are retained in the queue for later ports of the other 7 legacy event classes instead of being discarded early.
- Restored legacy event type 3, `DELAYED_SPAWN`:
  - `setDelayedSpawnEvent(...)` serializes an unspawned entity with its 1.20 `EntityType` registry id and full entity NBT under `EntityTag`.
  - Due events are loaded with `EntityType.loadEntityRecursive(...)`, moved to the stored target position, and spawned into the stored dimension.
  - Invalid due delayed-spawn events are cleared after their due tick, matching the old one-shot clear behavior and avoiding a permanent bad queue entry.
- Expanded diagnostics:
  - Added `/narutoport m8_world special_events_state` to report pending, delayed, unsupported, due, current time, and next start tick.
  - Added `/narutoport m8_world special_events_tick` to force one immediate scheduler pass for due events.
  - Added `/narutoport m8_world delayed_spawn_ready`, which schedules a named armor stand 20 ticks ahead without adding it to the world immediately.
  - `/narutoport saveddata summary` now reports both the raw special-event SavedData key count and `specialevent_pending`.
- Scope note:
  - This slice restores the scheduler foundation plus `DELAYED_SPAWN` only. Cylindrical/spherical explosions, village siege, meteor shower, set blocks, vanilla explosion, and delayed callback still need their own behavior ports.

### M8 SpecialEvent Vanilla Explosion Slice

- Restored legacy SpecialEvent type 7, `VANILLA_EXPLOSION`, on the existing 1.20.1 SavedData scheduler:
  - Added `SpecialEvent.setVanillaExplosionEvent(...)` overloads that write legacy fields `Type=7`, `EntityUUID`, `Start`, `x0/y0/z0`, `strength`, `flaming`, and `damagesTerrain`.
  - Due events resolve the stored dimension, follow the tracked entity UUID when it is still loaded/alive, and call the 1.20.1 `level.explode(...)` path once.
  - Terrain damage uses the stored `damagesTerrain` flag; the old-style public overload seeds it from the server `mobGriefing` game rule.
  - `special_events_state` now reports `vanilla_explosion` separately instead of counting type 7 as unsupported.
- Restored Shibuki sword's old delayed explosion timing:
  - `BasicMeleeWeaponItem` no longer explodes immediately on Shibuki hit.
  - On a valid Shibuki hit, it schedules a type 7 event against the struck target for `gameTime + 20`, matching the 1.12.2 `EventVanillaExplosion(entity.world, entity, 0, 0, 0, 5, worldTime + 20, false)` behavior.
- Expanded diagnostics:
  - Added `/narutoport m8_world vanilla_explosion_ready`, which schedules a no-fire, no-terrain-damage explosion 20 ticks ahead at the player's look direction.
- Scope note:
  - This slice restores the one-shot vanilla explosion event. The remaining SpecialEvent behavior still pending is cylindrical explosion, spherical explosion, village siege, meteor shower, set blocks, and delayed callback.

### M8 SpecialEvent Set Blocks Slice

- Restored legacy SpecialEvent type 6, `SET_BLOCKS`, on the 1.20.1 SavedData scheduler:
  - Added `SpecialEvent.setBlocksEvent(...)` overloads that accept a `Map<BlockPos, BlockState>` and write legacy fields `Type=6`, `Start`, `Tick`, `x0/y0/z0`, `lifespan`, `addIndex`, `removeIndex`, `Sound`, `Particles`, and `blocksList`.
  - Each block list entry keeps the old `x/y/z/blockstate` shape and also stores a stable 1.20 block-state compound through `NbtUtils.writeBlockState(...)`.
  - Due events process at most 64 entries per server tick, matching the old incremental add/remove budget.
  - `lifespan=0` clears the event after placement; positive lifespans keep the event until `Start + lifespan`, then remove the placed positions in batches.
  - `special_events_state` now reports `set_blocks` separately instead of counting type 6 as unsupported.
- Expanded diagnostics:
  - Added `/narutoport m8_world set_blocks_ready`, which schedules a 3x3 yellow wool test panel 20 ticks ahead and removes it after 80 ticks.
- Scope note:
  - This restores the generic scheduler behavior for type 6. Existing 1.20 entity-local temporary block systems remain local for now; old jutsu call sites can be reconnected to `setBlocksEvent(...)` as each item/entity parity pass reaches them.

### M8 SpecialEvent Spherical Explosion Slice

- Restored legacy SpecialEvent type 2, `SPHERICAL_EXPLOSION`, on the 1.20.1 SavedData scheduler:
  - Added `SpecialEvent.setSphericalExplosionEvent(...)` overloads that write legacy fields `Type=2`, `EntityUUID`, `Start`, `Tick`, `x0/y0/z0`, `radius`, `tx`, `ty`, `tz`, `tr`, `mobGriefing`, `useBlockExplosionResistance`, `fireChance`, `Sound`, and `Particles`.
  - Due events preserve the old ring-scan progress model and process up to 1024 non-air block touches per server tick, keeping large blasts incremental instead of single-tick world edits.
  - Block breaking honors the stored `mobGriefing` and resistance flags, includes the old low block-drop chance shape, and sends explosion/smoke particles from the server path.
  - Sounds follow the old cadence: a vanilla generic explosion on the first event tick, then the mod explosion sound every 40 ticks at the old offset.
  - Positive `fireChance` is handled as a best-effort post-blast ignition pass after the staged scan completes.
  - `special_events_state` now reports `spherical_explosion` separately instead of counting type 2 as unsupported.
- Expanded diagnostics:
  - Added `/narutoport m8_world spherical_explosion_ready`, which builds a small stone target in front of the player and schedules a radius 4 staged blast 20 ticks ahead.

  - The old client camera-shake packet is not restored in this slice.

### M8 SpecialEvent Meteor Shower Slice

- Restored legacy SpecialEvent type 5, `METEOR_SHOWER`, on the 1.20.1 SavedData scheduler:
  - Added `SpecialEvent.setMeteorShowerEvent(...)` overloads for fixed center and entity-following showers, plus `setMeteorShowerForAllPlayersEvent(...)` for the old all-player targeting mode.
  - Events write legacy fields `Type=5`, `EntityUUID`, `Start`, `Tick`, `x0/y0/z0`, `radius`, `strikeInterval`, `duration`, and `allPlayers`.
  - Due events preserve the old duration gate and randomized strike cadence shape, `strikeInterval + random(0..2) - 1`, with a 1 tick lower bound to avoid zero-modulo crashes on tiny debug intervals.
  - Strike placement sets `narutomod:meteor` at Y 250, clamped to the current dimension build height.
  - `special_events_state` now reports `meteor_shower` separately instead of counting type 5 as unsupported.
- Restored the minimum meteor block runtime needed by the event:
  - `ModBlocks.METEOR` now uses a dedicated `MeteorBlock` extending `FallingBlock` instead of a plain static block.
  - On landing or fall break, the meteor schedules the restored radius 12 spherical explosion event with `fireChance=0.33`, damages and knocks back nearby living entities, then leaves `narutomod:meteorite` at the impact position.
  - The block keeps old-style no-loot/unbreakable/high-resistance behavior and emits light while falling/placed.
- Expanded diagnostics:
  - Added `/narutoport m8_world meteor_shower_ready`, which schedules a short radius 6 shower 20 ticks ahead near the player's look direction.
- Scope note:
  - This restores the scheduler plus the core falling meteor/impact loop. Client-side firework spark decoration and the full old oversized meteor hitbox/model parity remain visual follow-up work.
  - Remaining SpecialEvent behavior still pending: village siege and delayed callback.

### M8 SpecialEvent Village Siege Slice

- Restored legacy SpecialEvent type 4, `VILLAGE_SIEGE`, on the 1.20.1 SavedData scheduler:
  - Added `SpecialEvent.setVillageSiegeEvent(...)` overloads that preserve the old public signature shape: center position, start time, radius, optional mob entity, and spawn interval.
  - Events write legacy fields `Type=4`, `Start`, `Tick`, `x0/y0/z0`, `radius`, `spawnInterval`, and optional `EntityType` for custom mob spawns.
  - The first due tick broadcasts the old translated alert key, `chattext.specialevent.villagesiege`.
  - Daytime ends the event, matching the old `world.isDaytime()` clear behavior.
  - Night ticks use the old random radius/angle shape and spawn cadence to create either the saved custom mob type or a random zombie/skeleton.
  - Spawn placement scans down from the top of the build height and spawns on the first non-air surface at the selected x/z position.
  - `special_events_state` now reports `village_siege` separately instead of counting type 4 as unsupported.
- Expanded diagnostics:
  - Added `/narutoport m8_world village_siege_ready`, which sets the level time to night, schedules a radius 18 siege at the player position, and uses a short debug spawn interval.

### M8 SpecialEvent Delayed Callback Slice

- Restored legacy SpecialEvent type 8, `DELAYED_CALLBACK`, on the 1.20.1 SavedData scheduler:
  - Added `SpecialEvent.setDelayedCallbackEvent(...)` overloads that write legacy fields `Type=8`, `Start`, `Tick`, `x0/y0/z0`, and `callbackID`.
  - Added `SpecialEvent.setSmokeBombCallbackEvents(...)`, which recreates the old smoke bomb pattern of scheduling callback ID `681` for consecutive ticks.
  - Due events dispatch callback ID `681` to the smoke callback and clear the event after one execution, matching the old one-shot delayed callback behavior.
  - Unknown callback IDs are cleared when due and reported as not executed, preventing unsupported stale callback entries from staying in the queue forever.
  - `special_events_state` now reports `delayed_callback` separately instead of counting type 8 as unsupported.
- Restored the only callback used by the old source:
  - Callback ID `681` now sends `SMOKE_COLORED` particles at the stored position with the old dark smoke color and 40-60 tick particle lifetime range.
  - Existing 1.20 smoke bomb projectiles keep their local 60 tick smoke implementation, so this event path is available for old SavedData/API parity without doubling smoke in the current projectile path.
- Expanded diagnostics:
  - Added `/narutoport m8_world delayed_callback_ready`, which schedules 60 consecutive smoke callbacks at the player's look position.
- Scope note:
  - This closes the generic SpecialEvent scheduler coverage for all old event types 1-8. Remaining M8 work is now call-site reconnection, Might Guy village quest wrapping, world-spawn/POI parity, and integrated-client visual/play validation.

### M8 Might Guy Village Defense Quest Reconnect Slice

- Reconnected the old Might Guy village-defense quest to the restored 1.20.1 `SpecialEvent` village siege scheduler:
  - Right-clicking Might Guy now looks for a village context using 1.20 POIs (`PoiTypes.MEETING`) plus nearby living villagers instead of the removed 1.12 `Village` object.
  - Valid interaction schedules `SpecialEvent.setVillageSiegeEvent(...)` at the detected village center and keeps the old night-start shape: if already night, the siege begins shortly; otherwise it waits until the next night window.
  - Might Guy persists quest state on the entity NBT: customer UUID/name, start tick, center/radius, tracking state, starting villager count, player kills, and Might Guy kills.
  - The old `siege_kills` sidebar objective is restored as a dummy scoreboard and updated from server-side `LivingDeathEvent` tracking for both the player and Might Guy.
  - At daybreak or customer failure, the quest compares villager losses, player kills, Might Guy kills, and customer survival to decide success/failure.
  - Success broadcasts the old success chat, gives the player `EIGHTGATES`, grants `narutomod:openedgates`, and adds +3 to a 1.20 player-persistent Might Guy village reputation tag.
  - Failure broadcasts the old failure chat and subtracts 3 from that reputation tag; negative reputation blocks taking the quest again until reset by future gameplay/admin tooling.
- Expanded diagnostics:
  - Added `/narutoport m8_world might_guy_siege_ready`, which creates a small bell/villager test scene, spawns Might Guy, sets night time, resets the player's Might Guy village reputation to 0, and instructs the player to right-click Might Guy to schedule the real quest path.
- Scope note:
  - This restores the old quest wrapper and reward loop without resurrecting the removed 1.12 `Village` reputation API.
  - Natural Might Guy village spawn placement and broader world-spawn parity are still pending.

### M8 Summoning Delayed Spawn Reconnect Slice

- Reconnected the current 1.20.1 summoning entry points to restored `SpecialEvent` type 3, `DELAYED_SPAWN`:
  - Toad, snake, slug, Gamabunta, and Manda summon creation no longer add the entity to the level immediately.
  - Summons still configure owner, scale, and summon-specific state first, then persist the entity through the SpecialEvent queue and appear 20 ticks later.
  - Added `AbstractSummonAnimalEntity.scheduleDelayedSpawn(...)` so the shared summon path uses one delayed-spawn implementation.
  - Queued summons are marked visible and AI-ready at the actual spawn moment to avoid stacking the old SpecialEvent delay with the newer local summon intro delay.
- Expanded diagnostics:
  - `/narutoport m5_summoning state` now includes `special_events_pending`.

  - Old slug split/clone delayed-spawn behavior remains separate if the 1.20 slug behavior later restores splitting.

### M8 Natural Mob Spawn BiomeModifier Slice

- Reconnected the old 1.12 natural-spawn declarations to 1.20.1 data-driven biome modifiers for the eight legacy entities that had explicit `EntityRegistry.addSpawn(...)` calls:
  - Ambient-style NPC spawns: Iruka Sensei, Sakura Haruno, Tenten, and Might Guy.
  - Monster spawns: Itachi, Kisame Hoshigaki, Zabuza Momochi, and White Zetsu.
  - Preserved old weights and group sizes: Iruka/Sakura/Tenten `1,1,1`, Might Guy `20,1,1`, Itachi/Kisame/Zabuza `1,1,1`, White Zetsu `10,1,1`.
- Added 1.20 biome data:
  - Added custom biome tags under `data/narutomod/tags/worldgen/biome/legacy_*_spawns.json` to map the old 1.12 biome lists onto current 1.20 biome IDs.
  - Added eight `forge:add_spawns` biome modifiers under `data/narutomod/forge/biome_modifier/add_spawn_*.json`.
  - The four old ambient NPCs now use `MobCategory.AMBIENT` in their entity type builders instead of the temporary `MONSTER` category.
- Added spawn-placement registration:
  - `ModSpawnPlacements.register()` now runs during common setup.
  - Ambient NPCs use `Mob.checkMobSpawnRules` on ground.
  - Monster entries use `Monster.checkMonsterSpawnRules` on ground.
- Expanded diagnostics:
  - `/narutoport m8_world state` now reports `natural_spawn_biome_entries`.
  - Added `/narutoport m8_world natural_spawns_probe`, which scans the loaded biome registry and reports whether all eight target entities were actually injected into biome spawn lists.
- Scope note:
  - This slice covers old natural mob spawning, not dungeon-spawn weights from `DungeonHooks.addDungeonMob`; those need a separate loot/spawner compatibility decision.
  - The old all-biomes helper seen in a few NPC source files had no active `EntityRegistry.addSpawn(...)` call and was not treated as a spawn declaration.
  - Natural village placement constraints for Might Guy are still a follow-up; this restores his old ambient biome spawning and leaves the village quest gate on interaction.

### M8 God Tree Structure Placement Slice

- Restored the old `ProcedureSpawnGodTree` structure-placement behavior as a 1.20.1 common-side procedure:
  - Added `ProcedureSpawnGodTree.place(ServerLevel, BlockPos)`.
  - Preserved the old placement order and offsets for the God Tree structure stack.
  - Preserved the old duplicate `world_tree_15` placement at `(x + 27, y + 60, z)`, so the 1.20 path matches the 1.12 procedure rather than silently deduplicating it.
  - Uses 1.20.1 `StructureTemplateManager` + `StructureTemplate.placeInWorld(...)` with `Rotation.NONE`, `Mirror.NONE`, and entity placement enabled.
  - Performs a preflight pass before placing blocks: all templates must load, and the computed top must fit under the dimension build height.
- Expanded diagnostics:
  - Added `/narutoport m8_world god_tree_place_probe`, which places the full 21-template God Tree stack 18 blocks in front of the player and reports placed count, origin, missing templates, or height failure.
- Scope note:
  - This reconnects the God Tree structure runtime primitive, not the final player-facing trigger. The item/jutsu/procedure call site that should invoke God Tree still needs to be restored when that gameplay path is reconnected.
  - Live visual parity still needs an integrated-client placement test because the old NBT files were migrated as resources, not resaved through a 1.20.1 structure block/DFU workflow.
  - The `meteor` structure runtime remains separate because old `ProcedureMeteorStrike` belongs to the Rinnegan Tengaishinsei path, which is not yet restored as a current 1.20 player skill entry.

### M8 Meteor Structure Runtime Primitive Slice

- Restored the old `ProcedureMeteorStrike` meteor-template runtime primitive as a 1.20.1 common-side procedure:
  - Added `ProcedureMeteorStrike.strike(ServerLevel, LivingEntity, BlockPos)`.
  - Preserved the old `meteor` template placement offset from the strike target: `(x - 10, y + 90, z - 10)`.
  - Preserved the old 20x20x20 post-placement capture volume used by `ProcedureUtils.getNonAirBlocks(...)`.
  - Placed the migrated `data/narutomod/structures/meteor.nbt` through `StructureTemplate.placeInWorld(...)` with `Rotation.NONE`, `Mirror.NONE`, and entity placement enabled.
  - Converts captured non-air, breakable blocks into the existing 1.20 `ChibakuSatelliteEntity` block-cluster stage, then removes the placed template blocks so the falling satellite owns the visible meteor body.
  - Restored the old nearby-satellite reuse path: if a `ChibakuSatelliteEntity` is already above/near the caster, the procedure plays `tengaishinsei`, disables no-gravity, and gives it horizontal velocity toward the new target instead of spawning another template.
  - Keeps the old owner invulnerability marker (`InvulnerableTime=300`) on a newly spawned meteor satellite.
- Expanded diagnostics:
  - Added `/narutoport m8_world meteor_structure_probe`, which targets the point 18 blocks in front of the player, runs the new procedure, and reports target, template origin, whether a satellite was reused or spawned, satellite id, captured block count, and nearby satellite count before/after.
- Scope note:
  - This reconnects the server-side meteor structure primitive, not the final player-facing Rinnegan Tengaishinsei key path.
  - Chakra cost, Rinnegan/Tenseigan ownership gates, cooldowns, and special-jutsu-2 routing remain for a later Rinnegan item/procedure integration slice.
  - Live visual parity still needs an integrated-client placement/fall test because the old NBT file has been migrated as a resource, not visually resaved through a 1.20.1 structure block workflow.

### M5/M8 Rinnegan Deva Path Special Jutsu 2 Reconnect Slice

- Reconnected the first player-facing Rinnegan/Tenseigan special-jutsu-2 path to restored 1.20.1 runtime primitives:
  - Added `RinneganSpecialJutsuHandler` as a common-side key handler for Rinnegan-like head items.
  - The special-jutsu key network route now falls through in this order: Obito Kamui, Rinnegan/Tenseigan Deva path, then Biju handling.
  - Preserved the old Deva path `which_path == 0` split: key-2 release casts Chibaku Tensei, while sneaking key-2 release casts Tengaishinsei/Meteor Strike.
  - Restored old resource costs for this path: Chibaku Tensei consumes 5000 chakra and sets the old `chibakutenseicd` 6000-tick cooldown; Tengaishinsei consumes 5000 chakra, or 20% cost when reusing an existing nearby `ChibakuSatelliteEntity`.
  - Tengaishinsei now calls the restored `ProcedureMeteorStrike` primitive, so the gameplay key path can spawn or redirect the migrated `meteor` template satellite.
- Expanded diagnostics:
  - Added `/narutoport m5_jutsu rinnegan_deva_ready` to equip a Rinnegan head, select Deva path 0, clear Chibaku cooldown, and fill the debug resource pool.
  - Added `/narutoport m5_jutsu rinnegan_deva_chibaku_release` to fire the Deva path Chibaku release through the new handler and report ball count, cooldown, chakra, and satellite state.
  - Added `/narutoport m5_jutsu rinnegan_deva_meteor_release` to fire the Deva path meteor release through the new handler and report satellite reuse/spawn deltas and chakra.
  - `/narutoport m5_jutsu state` now includes Rinnegan head/path, Chibaku cooldown ticks, nearby Chibaku balls, and nearby Chibaku satellites.
- Scope note:
  - This restores only Deva path 0 for special-jutsu-2. Rinnegan path cycling from the powerup key, the other paths, armor model/tick/passive semantics, ownership parity, and full integrated-client keyplay remain separate slices.
  - The debug release commands require an equipped Rinnegan/Tenseigan head with `which_path=0`, so they do not bypass the real key path's item/path gate.
  - Live visual parity still needs integrated-client validation for Chibaku launch/capture, meteor template conversion, satellite fall, and explosions.

### M5 Rinnegan PowerIncrease Path Cycling Slice

### M5 Rinnegan Animal/Preta/Naraka Path Reconnect Slice

- Reconnected three more old Rinnegan/Tenseigan special-jutsu-2 paths through `RinneganSpecialJutsuHandler`:
  - Path 2 / Animal Path now toggles the restored `GiantDog2hEntity` summon, stores the old `SummonedAnimal_id` state on the head item, consumes the old 200 chakra on summon, and dismisses the stored summon with poof feedback on the next release.
  - Path 3 / Preta Path now consumes the old 10 chakra and calls `PretaShieldEntity.spawnFrom(...)`, including the existing remount behavior when an owned shield is already active.
  - Path 4 / Naraka Path now toggles `KingOfHellEntity`, consumes the old 100 chakra on summon, stores the old `KoH_id` UUID on the head item, and dismisses the stored King of Hell through its retreat animation on the next release.
  - Path 1 / Asura Path and path 5 / Outer Path still report as not reconnected from this key path. Outer Path already exists through the Six Path Senjutsu item path; wiring the Rinnegan helmet shortcut should be a separate parity pass because it touches the global Gedo/Ten Tails constraints.
- Small runtime support changes:
  - Added `KingOfHellEntity.dismiss()` so Naraka Path can use the existing retreat animation instead of abruptly removing the entity.
  - Added `GiantDog2hEntity.dismissWithPoof()` so Animal Path dismissal reuses the entity's own poof cleanup.
  - Updated `ProcedureUtils.removeUniqueIdTag(...)` to remove both modern `CompoundTag.putUUID(key, ...)` storage and the old `keyMost/keyLeast` compatibility pair.
- Expanded diagnostics:
  - Added `/narutoport m5_jutsu rinnegan_animal_ready`, `rinnegan_preta_ready`, and `rinnegan_naraka_ready`.
  - Added `/narutoport m5_jutsu rinnegan_path_release`, which triggers the currently selected Rinnegan/Tenseigan path through the real handler and reports Preta shield, Giant Dog, King of Hell, tag state, and chakra deltas.
  - `/narutoport m5_jutsu state` now reports `SummonedAnimal_id`, `KoH_id` presence, nearby Preta shields, nearby Giant Dogs, and nearby King of Hell entities.
- Scope note:
  - This restores the service-side activation/toggle path for Animal, Preta, and Naraka. It does not claim final visual/play parity until the integrated client commands are run in-world.
  - Exact dojutsu ownership doubling, Rinnegan armor/passive semantics, Asura Path armor/cannon auto-equip, and Rinnegan-key Outer Path shortcut remain separate work.

### M5 Rinnegan Asura Path Passive Reconnect Slice

- Reconnected the old Rinnegan/Tenseigan Asura Path passive through `RinneganSpecialJutsuHandler`:
  - Path 1 / Asura Path now arms itself on the server player tick when a Rinnegan-like head has `which_path=1` and the player is not holding `tenseigan_chakra_mode` in the main hand.
  - The handler auto-equips `asurapatharmorbody` to the chest slot and `asuracanon` to the offhand through the existing `ProcedureUtils.swapItemToSlot(...)` path.
  - Leaving Asura Path while still wearing a Rinnegan/Tenseigan head clears the old temporary Asura body/cannon items, matching the old helmet tick cleanup behavior.
  - Wearing the Asura body without a Rinnegan/Tenseigan head removes the equipped body, matching the old body tick safety gate, while avoiding broad inventory cleanup for standalone Asura Cannon tests.
  - The Asura body now tracks the old `ticks_used` tag and reapplies the old 40-tick cadence buffs: Strength 24, Speed 16, Haste 5, Jump Boost 5, and Saturation 0 for 41 ticks.
- Expanded diagnostics:
  - Added `/narutoport m5_jutsu rinnegan_asura_ready`, which equips a Rinnegan head with `which_path=1`, runs the real Asura maintenance path once, and reports chest/offhand/tick/effect state.
  - Added `/narutoport m5_jutsu rinnegan_asura_maintain`, which re-runs the real maintenance path against the current player state.
  - `/narutoport m5_jutsu state` now reports Asura body equipped state, offhand cannon state, chest/offhand item descriptions, `ticks_used`, and the Strength effect probe.
- Scope note:
  - This restores the server-side passive equipment and buff loop. Exact custom armor model parity and integrated-client play validation remain separate visual/client passes.
  - Path 5 / Outer Path still needs the Rinnegan-key shortcut wiring; the existing Six Path Senjutsu item route remains the current player-facing path for Gedo/Ten Tails work.

### M5 PowerIncrease Generic Jutsu Item Switching Slice

- Reconnected the highest-priority old `ProcedurePowerIncreaseOnKeyPressed` branch:
  - Added `PowerIncreaseKeyHandler` as the shared server-side PowerIncrease dispatcher.
  - `PowerIncreaseKeyMessage` now calls the shared dispatcher instead of going straight to the Rinnegan handler.

  - Main/offhand `JutsuItem` branches intentionally consume both press and release packets, but only release performs the switch, matching the old `if (!is_pressed)` behavior.

  - `/narutoport m5_jutsu state` now reports the main-hand and offhand PowerIncrease jutsu item current selection.

### M5 PowerIncrease Susanoo Upgrade Slice

- Reconnected the old Sharingan + mounted Susanoo PowerIncrease upgrade branch:
  - Added `SusanooPowerIncreaseHandler` as the server-side Susanoo upgrade bridge.
  - `PowerIncreaseKeyHandler` now follows the restored old branch order for completed branches: main-hand `JutsuItem`, offhand `JutsuItem`, Sharingan + mounted Susanoo, Rinnegan/Tenseigan path cycling, then Biju Cloak full-set handling.
  - The Susanoo branch requires a Sharingan-family head item and an owned mounted Susanoo entity.
  - Matching the old `if (!is_pressed)` behavior, the branch consumes both press and release packets while only release attempts the upgrade.
  - The upgrade chain now maps the old thresholds onto the current 1.20 entities: Skeleton -> Clothed half at 6,000 battle XP, Clothed half -> Clothed full at 12,000 battle XP, and Clothed full -> Winged at 24,000 battle XP, with the old 500 chakra cost per step.
  - Successful replacement continues to mount the player, stores the old `summonedSusanooID` persistent tag, and marks the synced `susanoo_activated` flag for existing Mangekyo durability logic.
- Expanded diagnostics:
  - Added `/narutoport m5_jutsu powerincrease_susanoo_ready`, which prepares an Eternal Mangekyo head, clears main/offhand PowerIncrease preemption, fills battle XP/chakra, and spawns a mounted Susanoo Skeleton.
  - Added `/narutoport m5_jutsu powerincrease_susanoo_upgrade`, which simulates PowerIncrease release through the real shared dispatcher and reports before/after Susanoo stage, chakra spent, held-item preemption state, and nearby Susanoo counts.
  - `/narutoport m5_jutsu state` now reports the Sharingan-head gate, current Susanoo PowerIncrease vehicle stage, and nearby Skeleton/Clothed/Winged Susanoo counts.
- Scope note:

### M5 PowerIncrease Byakugan FOV Slice

- Reconnected the old Byakugan activation and PowerIncrease FOV branch:
  - Added `ByakuganHandler` as the server-side Byakugan state bridge for `byakugan_activated` and `byakugan_fov`.
  - `SpecialJutsuKeyMessage` now routes Special Jutsu 1 through Byakugan after Obito Kamui and before Rinnegan/Biju fallbacks.
  - Pressing Special Jutsu 1 with a Byakugan head and enough chakra activates Byakugan, sets FOV to `110`, plays the old `byakugan` sound, applies night vision while worn, and drains the old `10` chakra per half-second, doubled for non-owner helmets.
  - Releasing Special Jutsu 1 deactivates Byakugan and sends a client reset.
  - `PowerIncreaseKeyHandler` now follows the restored old branch order: main-hand `JutsuItem`, offhand `JutsuItem`, active Byakugan FOV reduction, Sharingan + mounted Susanoo, Rinnegan/Tenseigan path cycling, then Biju Cloak full-set handling.
  - While Byakugan is active, the PowerIncrease press packet reduces `byakugan_fov` by 1 and syncs it to the client; the release packet is consumed but does not change FOV, matching the old branch.
- Restored a first 1.20 Byakugan client visual bridge:
  - Added `ByakuganViewSyncMessage` for server-to-client active/FOV sync.
  - Added `ClientByakuganViewState` and `ByakuganViewEvents`.
  - `ViewportEvent.ComputeFov` applies the synced Byakugan FOV only on configured-FOV frames.
  - `RenderGuiOverlayEvent.Post` draws the old white crosshair and a full-screen inverted color overlay while local Byakugan state is active and the player is still wearing a Byakugan head.
  - `ModItems.BYAKUGANHELMET` now instantiates `ByakuganHelmetItem`, preserving Byakugan texture/tooltips and creative owner binding instead of remaining a plain leather helmet placeholder.
- Expanded diagnostics:
  - Added `/narutoport m5_jutsu powerincrease_byakugan_ready`, which equips an owned Byakugan, clears main/offhand PowerIncrease preemption, fills battle XP/chakra, and activates Byakugan through the real Special Jutsu 1 route.
  - Added `/narutoport m5_jutsu powerincrease_byakugan_press`, which simulates a real PowerIncrease press and reports FOV before/after.
  - Added `/narutoport m5_jutsu powerincrease_byakugan_release`, which simulates Special Jutsu 1 release and reports active/FOV reset state.
  - `/narutoport m5_jutsu state` now reports Byakugan head, active state, and current FOV.
- Scope note:
  - This closes the known old `ProcedurePowerIncreaseOnKeyPressed` branch list on the server path. ### M5 Byakugan Special Jutsu 2/3 Reconnect Slice

- Reconnected the ordinary Byakugan Special Jutsu 2 and 3 server routes:

  - Special Jutsu 3 press/release now restores the ordinary Hakkesho Kaiten branch: player level 30 gate, original-owner gate, per-tick chakra check via `HakkeshoKeitenEntity`, press-to-start/ride, release-to-discard, old `HakkeshoKaitenCD` cooldown calculation from active ticks, and old food drain of `ticks / 60 + 1`.
  - `HakkeshoKeitenEntity` now exposes active lookup/release helpers so the key release path can cleanly stop an owned Kaiten and calculate cooldown without duplicating entity internals.

### M5 Tenseigan Evolution Runtime Slice

- Restored the old Byakugan-to-Tenseigan evolution runtime:
  - `ByakuganHelmetItem` now initializes old owner-bound Byakugan stacks with `ByakuganCount=1` when the tag is missing, matching the legacy `setOwner` side effect.
  - Owner-bound Byakugan stacks with `TenseiganEvolvedTime` now tick server-side and subtract 20 ticks once per second.
  - The timer path is guarded against double subtraction when both inventory and armor callbacks run in the same game tick.
  - When the timer reaches zero, the current Byakugan is replaced in the player stack location with `tenseiganhelmet`, the original owner and `ByakuganCount` are copied to the Tenseigan, a stripped copy of the old Byakugan is returned to the player, and `narutomod:tenseigan_achieved` is granted.
- Tightened related item-state helpers:
  - Medical Scroll Byakugan progress now treats a missing `ByakuganCount` on an owned Byakugan primary as the old baseline `1.0` instead of starting from zero.
  - Debug-created Byakugan eye samples now carry the same baseline count.
  - Chakra Fruit now writes `TenseiganChakraModeItem.BYAKUGAN_COUNT_TAG` instead of a duplicate literal key.
- Expanded diagnostics:
  - Added `/narutoport m5_medical_scroll tenseigan_evolution_ready`, which equips an owned Byakugan with `ByakuganCount=5` and a 20-tick evolution timer.
  - Added `/narutoport m5_medical_scroll tenseigan_evolution_finish`, which forces the equipped Byakugan through the production replacement helper and reports the new head item, returned Byakugan, and advancement state.
  - `/narutoport m5_medical_scroll state` now reports the head slot and `tenseigan_achieved` status.
- Scope note:
  - This restores the server/item-state evolution loop. Integrated-client validation is still needed for the natural one-second timer path, advancement toast, inventory placement feel, and Medical Scroll GUI-driven timer/count progression.

### M5 Byakugan Camera/Glow Visual Slice

- Restored the old client-side Byakugan camera/glow loop:
  - `ByakuganViewSyncMessage` now carries the server-calculated ninja level alongside active/FOV state so the client camera distance can use the old formula without depending on capability sync ordering.
  - `ByakuganHandler` exposes the shared old camera formulas: target render distance `clamp((int)(ninjaLevel / 3) * 11 / 16, 16, 32)` and camera offset `((110 - fov) * min(ninjaLevel / 3, 70) / 10) + 1`.
  - While Byakugan view is active, `ByakuganViewEvents` creates a local invisible `AltCamViewEntity`, switches the camera to it, moves it along the player's look vector each client tick, and restores the player camera when Byakugan deactivates or the helmet is removed.
  - The client temporarily raises render distance to the old Byakugan target value and restores the previous option value on deactivation.
  - Nearby living entities within `renderDistance * 8` are given client-side glowing while Byakugan is active; only entities newly marked by the Byakugan view are cleared on exit, preserving pre-existing glow state.
  - `AltCamViewEntity` now has an explicit no-op renderer instead of the previous temporary Chidori renderer registration.
- Expanded diagnostics:
  - Added `/narutoport m5_jutsu byakugan_visual_ready`, which equips and activates an owned Byakugan, runs five real PowerIncrease presses, spawns a living armor-stand target, and reports projected camera distance plus target render distance.
  - `/narutoport m5_jutsu state` now reports `byakugan_projected_camera_distance` and `byakugan_target_render_distance`.
- Scope note:
  - This restores the old local camera, render-distance, and glow mechanics at code level. It still needs integrated-client screenshot/play validation for first-person camera placement, render-distance option restoration, glow clearing, and interaction with user-changed video settings while active.

  - Added `ProcedureSpawnGodTree.executeProcedure(Map<String, Object>)`.

- Expanded diagnostics:

### M8 Village POI Spawn Constraint Slice

- Replaced the remaining loose village checks with a shared 1.20.1 POI helper:
  - Added `VillagePoiHelper` for common village context lookup, villager counting, savanna/taiga biome checks, and safe surface spawn selection.
  - Quest village lookup keeps the restored Might Guy behavior of accepting either a meeting POI or nearby villagers.
  - Natural Might Guy village lookup uses the old stricter scale intent: 20 village POIs plus 10 villagers before a spawn is allowed.
- Tightened SpecialEvent village siege spawning:
  - `SpecialEvent` type 4 now resolves a live POI/villager village context before each spawn attempt.
  - Siege mobs now use POI-constrained surface positions near the detected village context rather than any random solid block around the saved center.
  - If no village context exists, the event stays queued until daytime but does not spawn mobs into non-village terrain.
- Restored the old `ProcedureCheckVillageSize` gameplay path in 1.20.1 form:
  - `MightGuyEntity` now checks players every 400 overworld ticks.
  - The natural path requires the old `narutomod:ninjaachievement`, savanna/taiga biome, a large POI/villager village, no nearby Might Guy within 96 blocks, and a non-water surface spawn.
  - The spawned Might Guy is persistent and finalized as an event spawn.
- Expanded diagnostics:
  - `/narutoport m8_world village_siege_ready` now builds a small POI village scene before scheduling the siege and reports detected meeting/village POI counts.
  - Added `/narutoport m8_world might_guy_natural_ready`, which builds a 20-POI/10-villager test scene, grants `ninjaachievement`, force-runs the natural Might Guy spawn helper, and reports the exact reason if it does not spawn.

### M8 Akatsuki Dungeon Spawn Replacement Decision Slice

- Audited the old MCreator spawn settings for the three remaining dungeon-spawn questions:
  - Itachi: `spawnThisMob=true`, `spawnInDungeons=true`, monster spawn weight `1`, group `1-1`.
  - Kisame Hoshigaki: `spawnThisMob=true`, `spawnInDungeons=true`, monster spawn weight `1`, group `1-1`.
  - Zabuza Momochi: `spawnThisMob=true`, `spawnInDungeons=true`, monster spawn weight `20`, group `4-4`.
- Landed the 1.20.1 replacement decision:
  - Do not reintroduce direct dungeon spawner injection. The old MCreator `spawnInDungeons` hook has no direct stable data-pack equivalent in the current Forge 1.20.1 path.
  - Keep these encounters on natural monster biome modifiers, with the old biome spread already represented by `legacy_itachi_spawns`, `legacy_kisame_spawns`, and `legacy_zabuza_spawns`.
  - Added `audit/world_spawn_replacement_decisions.csv` to document the old dungeon flag and the chosen replacement for Itachi, Kisame, and Zabuza.
- Fixed a parity gap in the current biome modifier data:
  - `add_spawn_zabuza_momochi.json` now matches old Zabuza natural spawn settings: weight `20`, min count `4`, max count `4`.
  - Itachi and Kisame already matched the old `1/1-1` natural settings.
- Strengthened diagnostics:
  - `/narutoport m8_world natural_spawns_probe` now checks configured spawn weight and min/max group size, not just whether the entity appears in any loaded biome spawn list.
  - The probe reports `mismatched=...` for parameter drift and includes `dungeon_replacement=natural_monster_biome_modifiers`.
- Verification after the Akatsuki dungeon spawn replacement decision slice:
  - `.\gradlew.bat --no-daemon compileJava`: passes.

### M8 World Validation Suite Slice

- Added a consolidated M8 server-side validation command:
  - `/narutoport m8_world validation_suite` now checks structure template loading, mud lake configured/placed feature registration, runtime natural spawn entries and spawn parameter drift, Kamui dimension/platform readiness, POI village context detection, and the Might Guy natural-spawn route.
  - The suite intentionally does not run destructive God Tree or meteor structure placement. It reports `destructive_structure_placement=not_run` so those large in-world probes remain explicit operator actions.
  - The POI portion builds a local test village in front of the player, grants `narutomod:ninjaachievement`, and exercises `MightGuyEntity.tryNaturalVillageSpawn(..., true)`. Repeat runs treat the duplicate `nearby_might_guy` guard as a valid route result so the command stays useful after the first successful spawn.
  - The command reports all M8 drift details in one line: missing structures, missing/mismatched natural spawn targets, Kamui platform state, POI/villager counts, natural/siege village context booleans, Might Guy spawn result, and pending SpecialEvent count before/after.

### M5 Byakurin Rinne Sharingan Item Runtime Slice

- Promoted the standalone `narutomod:byakurinnesharinganhelmet` item out of plain armor placeholder behavior:
  - `ModItems.BYAKURINNESHARINGANHELMET` now instantiates `ByakuganHelmetItem` as an implicit Rinne Sharingan variant.
  - `ByakuganHelmetItem` now recognizes both the ordinary `byakuganhelmet` and standalone `byakurinnesharinganhelmet` as Byakugan-family heads.
  - The standalone item automatically carries the `RinneSharinganActivated` tag on default/debug/server-tick paths, while the old tagged ordinary Byakugan path remains supported.
  - Rinne Sharingan-active Byakugan-family heads now restore the old `+380` max-health attribute modifier on the head slot.
  - Tooltip/texture branching now uses the shared Rinne Sharingan check: Special Jutsu 2 shows `tooltip.byakurinnesharingan.jutsu2` for the active/fake Rinne Sharingan path, and the armor texture resolves to `byakurinnesharingan_helmet.png`.
  - `ByakuganHandler.isByakuganHead(...)` now accepts the standalone fake Rinne Sharingan head, so Special Jutsu 1/2/3 and PowerIncrease Byakugan routing can operate on that item instead of only on a manually tagged `byakuganhelmet`.
- Tightened diagnostics:
  - The Byakurin Yomotsu Hirasaka and shockwave debug preparations now equip the real `byakurinnesharinganhelmet` item rather than a tagged ordinary Byakugan stack, directly exercising the restored standalone item path.
  - The shared `ownedEye(...)` debug helper initializes implicit Rinne Sharingan tags for standalone fake Rinne Sharingan heads.
- Scope note:
  - This does not change the stricter Six Path Senjutsu gate, which currently requires Rinnegan/Tenseigan heads with `RinneSharinganActivated=true`.
  - Integrated-client validation is still needed for the item tooltip, armor texture, max-health modifier display, and live Special Jutsu key behavior.

### M5 Rinnegan/Tenseigan Helmet Legacy Tick Runtime Slice

- Promoted `narutomod:rinneganhelmet` and `narutomod:tenseiganhelmet` from plain leather armor placeholders to `RinneganHelmetItem`.
- Restored the old `ProcedureRinneganHelmetTickEvent` passive head-slot behavior on the server tick path:
  - Wearing either head cancels fall distance, refreshes the latest-worn-dojutsu timestamp, applies Speed/Night Vision, and ensures Black Receiver is present.
  - Active `RinneSharinganActivated` heads purge harmful effects, grant Flight/Saturation/Strength, heal by 1 when below max health, equip Rinnegan body/legs, and ensure Six Path Senjutsu is present.
  - Active Tenseigan heads also ensure Tenseigan Chakra Mode is present.
  - Inactive/base heads enable Ninjutsu Limbo Clone, apply Resistance, preserve the Ten Tails jinchuriki + level 180 Rinne Sharingan awakening gate, preserve the Tenseigan ByakuganCount>=5 Chakra Mode grant, and delegate Asura Path passive gear maintenance to the existing Rinnegan handler.
  - `chibakutensei_active` still extends Flight for the old Chibaku Tensei lift behavior.
  - A per-stack game-time guard prevents duplicate work when Forge invokes both inventory and armor tick paths in the same tick.
- Strengthened diagnostics:
  - `/narutoport m5_six_path rinnegan_helmet_tick_ready` equips an active Rinnegan head, injects a harmful poison effect, runs the legacy tick immediately, and reports purge/effect/gift/armor state.
  - `/narutoport m5_six_path tenseigan_helmet_tick_ready` equips a ByakuganCount=5 Tenseigan head, disables Limbo Clone first, runs the legacy tick, and reports Limbo/Tenseigan Chakra Mode/passive effect state.
  - `/narutoport m5_six_path state` now includes `legacy_tick=...` details for head tag, Rinnegan armor, Six Path, Black Receiver, Tenseigan Chakra Mode, Limbo Clone, and relevant effects.

### M5 Senjutsu Inherited Jutsu Runtime Slice

- Restored the old `ItemSenjutsu` inherited-jutsu gate behavior on the server inventory tick path:
  - `SAGE_RASENGAN` is enabled when the player is creative or has an owned learned ordinary Rasengan route.
  - `SAGE_RASENSHURIKEN` is enabled when the player is creative or has an owned usable Futon Rasenshuriken route.
  - `WOOD_BUDDHA` remains deliberately disabled here because the 1.20.1 Buddha 1000 entity still lacks the old rideable controller/chakra-burn/arm-volley runtime. This avoids exposing a jutsu slot that would still fall into incomplete behavior.
- Added actual `SAGE_RASENSHURIKEN` use/release behavior to `SenjutsuItem`:
  - Requires active Sage Mode, a usable Futon Rasenshuriken prerequisite, enabled Senjutsu gate, sufficient Senjutsu XP, cooldown clearance, and enough chakra.
  - Uses the restored 1.20.1 `RasenshurikenEntity.spawnFrom(...)` runtime with Senjutsu item XP progression.
  - Keeps the old charge/release interaction shape instead of falling through to the parent Ninjutsu "not ported" branch.
- Restored the old Toad Sage Mode self-hit redirect:
  - Left-clicking self while active Toad Sage Mode is present now attacks the looked-at entity within the player's reach cone, matching the old `ItemSenjutsu.onLeftClickEntity` behavior.
- Strengthened diagnostics:
  - Added `/narutoport m5_jutsu senjutsu_rasenshuriken_ready` to prepare Futon Rasenshuriken, active Senjutsu, full Senjutsu XP, and the correct current jutsu.
  - `/narutoport m5_jutsu state` and the shared Senjutsu state summary now report Sage Rasengan, Sage Rasenshuriken, Wood Buddha, and Snake 8 Heads gates individually.

### M5 Senjutsu Wood Buddha Runtime Slice

- Promoted `Buddha1000Entity` from a static display shell to a server-owned rideable runtime entity:
  - Tracks owner, sitting/standing state, ticks alive, chakra burn, and growth progress through synchronized entity data.
  - Spawns from `SenjutsuItem.WOOD_BUDDHA`, immediately mounts the owner, plays the legacy Shinsu Senju sound, and keeps the summon alive only while the owner is alive, riding, and in active Sage Mode.
  - Burns chakra once per second using the old `WOOD_BUDDHA.chakraUsage * 0.02 * xpModifier` shape and grants feather-falling/poof cleanup on discard or kill.
  - Restores basic rider movement control, sitting-to-standing transition, large growth particle/sound presentation, and owner-only interaction/remount behavior.
- Restored Wood Buddha arm attacks on the server runtime:
  - Sitting Buddha fires a 100-arm volley from randomized large-body offsets with 100 damage and grow visuals.
  - Standing Buddha fires one high-damage directed arm from the forward body edge toward the rider look target.
  - `BuddhaArmEntity.spawnFrom(...)` now has a positioned/directed overload so Buddha 1000 can spawn arms without relying on the player's eye position.
- Enabled the `WOOD_BUDDHA` inherited Senjutsu gate when the player is creative or owns a usable Mokuton Wood Golem route.
- Added `SenjutsuItem.WOOD_BUDDHA` use behavior:
  - Requires owned active Senjutsu, active Sage Mode, usable Mokuton Wood Golem prerequisite, enabled gate, sufficient Senjutsu XP, cooldown clearance, and enough chakra for the summon burn.
  - Right-clicking while riding an owned sitting Buddha switches it to standing mode and awards Senjutsu XP.
- Strengthened diagnostics:
  - `/narutoport m5_jutsu senjutsu_wood_buddha_ready` prepares active Senjutsu Wood Buddha and the Mokuton Wood Golem prerequisite.
  - `/narutoport m5_jutsu senjutsu_wood_buddha_shoot` triggers the current ridden Buddha's arm attack and reports spawned arm count.
  - `/narutoport m5_jutsu state` now reports Wood Buddha gate state, current Buddha vehicle state, nearby Buddha 1000 count, and nearby Buddha arm count.
- Scope note:
  - The old empty-left-click packet route for arm attacks is still not reintroduced in this slice. The server runtime is available and testable through the debug command; normal key/network wiring can be handled in a later input packet slice.

### M5 Wood Buddha Empty Left-Click Input Slice

- Reintroduced the old Buddha 1000 rider empty-left-click attack route in 1.20.1 form:
  - Added `ClientWoodBuddhaInput`, a client-only Forge input listener that watches the attack interaction key.
  - The client sends the packet only when the local player is riding `Buddha1000Entity`, no GUI is open, and the current hit result is empty/miss, matching the old `PlayerInteractEvent.LeftClickEmpty` intent.
  - Added `WoodBuddhaAttackMessage` as a zero-payload client-to-server packet.
  - The server does not trust a client-sent entity id. It validates the sender's current vehicle, requires it to be an owned `Buddha1000Entity`, then calls `shootArms()`.
  - Registered the packet through the existing `NetworkHandler` `SimpleChannel`.
- Scope note:
  - This restores the normal input/network path for the already-restored server runtime. Integrated client testing is still required to confirm the attack key event timing, hand swing feel, and empty-hit behavior while mounted.

### M5 Snake 8 Heads Empty Left-Click Input Slice

- Reintroduced the old Snake 8 Heads rider empty-left-click head attack route in 1.20.1 form:
  - Added `ClientSnake8HeadsInput`, a client-only Forge input listener that watches the attack interaction key.
  - The client sends the packet only when the local player is riding `Snake8HeadsEntity`, no GUI is open, and the current hit result is empty/miss, matching the old `PlayerInteractEvent.LeftClickEmpty` trigger shape.
  - Added `Snake8HeadsAttackMessage` as a zero-payload client-to-server packet.
  - The server validates the sender's current vehicle and ownership, then reruns the target search server-side with the old wide empty-click tolerance (`64` block range, `2.0` bounding-box inflation) before spawning a snake head. No client target id is trusted.
  - `Snake8HeadsEntity` now exposes `shootLookTarget(Player, double)` for the packet and debug command paths. Its ordinary tick-based swing route keeps the narrower `1.0` tolerance.
- Strengthened diagnostics:
  - Added `/narutoport m5_jutsu senjutsu_snake_8_heads_ready` as an M5 alias for the existing Snake 8 Heads prepared scene.

  - `/narutoport m5_jutsu state` now reports the current Snake 8 Heads vehicle ownership/tick state.

### M5 Rasenshuriken SpecialEvent Terrain Reconnect Slice

- Reconnected the Rasenshuriken impact terrain path to the restored SpecialEvent spherical explosion scheduler:
  - `RasenshurikenEntity.affectTerrain(...)` no longer performs a local immediate block scan and direct break loop.
  - Each server impact tick now schedules `SpecialEvent.SPHERICAL_EXPLOSION_TYPE` at the impact point, matching the old 1.12.2 `EventSphericalExplosion` call-site shape.
  - The scheduled event keeps the old no-fire, particles-on, sounds-off behavior and respects the server `mobGriefing` game rule.
  - Entity damage remains in the Rasenshuriken runtime itself through `damageImpactTargets(...)`, preserving the already-restored impact damage cadence.
- Strengthened diagnostics:
  - `/narutoport m5_jutsu state` now reports `special_events_pending` and `spherical_explosion_events` near the Rasenshuriken counters.
  - `/narutoport m5_six_path state` reports the same counters so Truth-Seeking Rasenshuriken impacts can be checked from the Six Path debug route.
- Scope note:
  - This is a server-verifiable call-site reconnection. Integrated client testing is still needed to compare terrain deletion shape, particle density, and sound cadence against 1.12.2 during live Rasenshuriken and Truth-Seeking Rasenshuriken impacts.

### M3 MobAppearance Itachi Fallback Parity Slice

- Restored the old `mob_appearance` fallback semantics now that `narutomod:itachi` has a real 1.20.1 entity type and renderer:
  - `MobAppearanceParticle` now treats no particle argument or legacy entity id `117` as the old Itachi illusion path and creates a client-side `ModEntityTypes.ITACHI` render entity.
  - Non-Itachi ids still resolve as live client entities when present, preserving the existing player-id particle route used by Genjutsu and M3 particle smoke tests.
  - Missing live entity ids now remain unresolved and retry on later ticks instead of immediately falling back to the generic `PORTING_DUMMY`, matching the old "wait for player entity" behavior more closely.
  - This supersedes the earlier M6 compatibility cleanup that temporarily changed MobAppearance's missing-entity fallback to `PORTING_DUMMY` while named ninja ids were still being promoted.
- Strengthened diagnostics:
  - Added `/narutoport m3_smoke mob_appearance_itachi`, which emits a single MobAppearance particle with legacy id `117` in front of the player.
  - The existing `/narutoport m3_smoke particles` route still passes the player id for its MobAppearance sample, so both old paths are now separately checkable.
- Scope note:

### M5 Snake 8 Heads Legacy Model Geometry Slice

- Replaced the first-pass 1.20.1 Snake 8 Heads client model with the generated legacy `ModelSnake8h` layer:
  - `Snake8HeadsModel.createBodyLayer()` now returns `LegacyModelLayerDefinitions.EntitySnake8Heads_ModelSnake8h_341()`, preserving the old 64x64 texture layout, trunk, eight tails, eight head meshes, horns, jaws, and 289-box geometry instead of the simplified 128x128 temporary model.
  - The model constructor now traverses the generated `tails -> neck_i_0 -> ... -> neck_i_9 -> head{i}` hierarchy, so renamed/missing generated parts fail immediately at bake time.
- Restored the old neck animation shape:
  - Ported the old 8x10 `neckRotation` angle table into the 1.20.1 model.

### M6 Susanoo Skeleton Legacy Model Geometry Slice

- Replaced the first-pass hand-written Susanoo Skeleton client model with the generated legacy `ModelSusanooSkeleton` layer:

  - Restored the old `setRotationAngles(...)` shape: `limbSwing * 2 / entity.height`, head/hat Y offset `-8`, body Y offset `-8`, arm Y offset `-7`, arm Z offset `-1`, arm X offsets `-12/+12`, hidden legs, and hidden `HornStyle1`.
- Reintroduced the old gas/flame overlay render pass in 1.20.1 form:
  - `SusanooSkeletonRenderer` now renders the skeleton fullbright, then adds a second `gas256.png` layer using the entity's synchronized flame color and old `0.5 * min(age / 60, 1)` alpha ramp.
  - The gas pass hides body, headwear, legs, and `HornStyle1`, matching the old `renderFlame` branch that rendered only head/arms with the moving gas texture.
  - The gas texture uses the shared bounded-key `NarutoRenderTypes.scrollingTranslucent(..., 0, 0.01)` path instead of per-frame RenderType allocation.

### M6 Nine Tails Legacy Model Geometry Slice

- Added a dedicated Nine Tails client model and renderer instead of continuing to route `ModEntityTypes.NINE_TAILS` through the first-pass shared `TailedBeastRenderer`:
  - `NineTailsModel.createBodyLayer()` now delegates to `LegacyModelLayerDefinitions.EntityNineTails_ModelNineTails_255()`, preserving the old 64x64 `ModelNineTails` geometry, eye overlay, humanoid-derived body, clawed limbs, and full 9x8 segmented tail hierarchy.
  - The model constructor traverses the generated legacy tree (`body -> body/right_arm/left_arm`, `body -> right_leg/left_leg`, `body -> tails -> Tail_i_j`, plus `hat -> eyes`) so missing or renamed generated parts fail at bake time.
  - The old per-segment tail wave was restored with the legacy `sin/cos((age - j) * 0.2)` formula and the old `j < 4`, `j == 4`, `j > 4` Z-offset branches. The randomized sway factors are now deterministic for repeatable screenshot comparison.
- Restored the old Nine Tails runtime animation shape:
  - Ported the old `ModelBiped`-style `limbSwing * 2 / entity.height` arm/leg animation pass, head yaw/pitch, old head/arm/leg rotation points, and idle arm wobble.
  - Restored the old airborne vs grounded custom leg poses for `upperLegRight/Left`, `midLegRight/Left`, and both feet.
  - Restored the old shooting mouth pose: snout lowers to `-0.2618F`, jaw opens to `1.0472F`, otherwise both reset to closed.
  - The eye overlay now renders fullbright after copying the animated head transform, matching the old post-body `bipedHeadwear` render branch.
- Reintroduced the old Nine Tails render framing:
  - `NineTailsRenderer` applies the old `MODELSCALE=10` translate/scale path, including the forward `0.375 * scale` Z offset and shadow radius `scale * 0.5`.
  - `NINE_TAILS` now registers `NineTailsRenderer`; the other tailed beasts still use the existing shared `TailedBeastRenderer` until their own legacy slices are ported.
  - KCM rendering keeps the old KCM texture path and now renders fullbright. The old 0.8 alpha blend is still pending an integrated renderer pass decision because the vanilla `MobRenderer` opaque path does not expose that alpha cleanly.
- Scope note:
  - This closes the first-pass Nine Tails geometry/animation mismatch at code level. Integrated client comparison is still needed for live KCM alpha, rider offset/camera placement, Tail Beast Ball mouth timing, boss-combat movement, and screenshot similarity against the 1.12 reference.

### M6 Eight Tails Legacy Model Geometry Slice

- Added a dedicated Eight Tails client model and renderer, separating `ModEntityTypes.EIGHT_TAILS` from the first-pass shared `TailedBeastRenderer`:
  - `EightTailsModel.createBodyLayer()` now delegates to `LegacyModelLayerDefinitions.EntityEightTails_ModelEightTails_209()`, preserving the old 64x64 `ModelEightTails` geometry, horn/snout/jaw hierarchy, chest/hump, arms, eye plane, and 8x8 segmented tail set.
  - The model extends `HumanoidModel<TailedBeastEntity>` because the generated old layer uses standard root `head/hat/body/right_arm/left_arm/right_leg/left_leg` parts, while the eight root-level tails are traversed and rendered manually.

  - Ported the old `ModelBiped`-style call with `limbSwing * 2 / entity.height`, then reapplied the old post-biped head/hat Y offset and right/left arm X/Z offsets.
  - Hid both legs at render time, matching the old `bipedRightLeg.showModel = false` and `bipedLeftLeg.showModel = false` branch.
  - Restored the old per-segment tail wave: `0.2618F + sin((age - j) * 0.08) * swayX`, plus Z and Y sway with the old `sqrt(j)` randomization shape. The random sway factors are deterministic for repeatable screenshot comparison.
  - Restored the old shooting pose: head and hat pitch down by `-0.5236F`, jaw opens to `1.0472F`, otherwise jaw resets closed.
  - The hat/eye plane is rendered fullbright after the main body pass, matching the old post-body `bipedHeadwear` render branch.
- Reintroduced the old Eight Tails render framing:
  - `EightTailsRenderer` applies the old `MODELSCALE=10` translate/scale path, including the forward `0.375 * scale` Z offset and shadow radius `scale * 0.5`.
  - Other tailed beasts remain on the shared first-pass `TailedBeastRenderer` until their own legacy layers and animation slices are ported.
- Scope note:
  - This closes the first-pass Eight Tails geometry/animation mismatch at code level. Integrated client comparison is still needed for live rider posture, mount camera placement, Tail Beast Ball mouth/origin timing, arm placement under long-running animation, and screenshot similarity against the 1.12 reference.

  - `OneTailModel.createBodyLayer()` now delegates to `LegacyModelLayerDefinitions.EntityOneTail_ModelOneTail_224()`, preserving the old 64x64 `ModelOneTail` geometry, head/jaw hierarchy, rounded body, limb details, fullbright headwear marker, and 10-segment single tail chain.

  - Tail traversal now fails at bake time if any generated `Tail_j` segment is missing, matching the stricter generated-model slices used for the other tailed beasts.
- Restored the old One Tail runtime animation shape:
  - Ported the old `ModelBiped`-style call with `limbSwing * 2 / entity.height`, then reapplied the old post-biped head/hat Y offset, arm X/Z offsets, and leg Y offsets.
  - Restored the old per-segment tail wave for `Tail_1..9`: base pitch `0.2618F`, with the old `sin/cos((age - j) * 0.05)` X/Z sway and `sin((age - j) * 0.1)` Y sway. The random sway factors are deterministic for repeatable screenshot comparison.
  - Restored the old shooting pose: head and hat pitch down by `-0.5236F`, jaw opens to `0.7854F`, otherwise jaw resets closed.
  - The hat/headwear marker is rendered fullbright after the main body pass, matching the old post-body `bipedHeadwear` render branch. The tail is not manually re-rendered because it is a child of `body` in the generated old layer.
- Reintroduced the old One Tail render framing:
  - `OneTailRenderer` applies the old `MODELSCALE=20` translate/scale path without the commented-out forward Z offset, and keeps shadow radius at `scale * 0.5`.
  - Remaining Two/Five/Seven Tails stay on the shared first-pass `TailedBeastRenderer` until their legacy layer gaps are resolved.
- Scope note:
  - This closes the first-pass One Tail geometry/animation mismatch at code level. Integrated client comparison is still needed for live rider posture, mount camera placement, Tail Beast Ball mouth/origin timing, body/tail framing, and screenshot similarity against the 1.12 reference.
