### M6 Seven Tails Legacy Model Geometry Slice

- Promoted the old `ModelSevenTails` generated snippet into compiled generated source:
  - `tools/generate_layer_definition_snippets.py` now allowlists `EntitySevenTails.ModelSevenTails` for dynamic pose normalization. The extractor captured runtime tail-root yaw expressions inside the static layer; these are now zeroed at bake time and restored in `SevenTailsModel.setupAnim(...)`.
  - Regenerated `LegacyModelLayerDefinitions`; `audit/generated_layer_snippets_summary.json` now reports 68 compiled source methods, up from 67.
  - The generated `EntitySevenTails_ModelSevenTails_343()` layer preserves the old 64x64 geometry, head/hair/mandible hierarchy, fullbright headwear marker, chest/stomach chain, six fan tails, central 10-segment tail, and multi-arm limb details.
- Added a dedicated Seven Tails client model and renderer, separating `ModEntityTypes.SEVEN_TAILS` from the first-pass shared `TailedBeastRenderer`:
  - `SevenTailsModel.createBodyLayer()` now delegates to the generated old layer and traverses the deep body tail parent (`stomach -> ... -> bone17`) so missing or renamed tail parts fail at bake time.
  - The model extends `HumanoidModel<TailedBeastEntity>` because the generated old layer uses standard `head/hat/body/right_arm/left_arm` parts, while the legs are default biped placeholders and are hidden like the old model.
- Restored the old Seven Tails runtime animation shape:
  - Ported the old `ModelBiped`-style call with `limbSwing * 2 / entity.height`, then reapplied the old post-biped head/hat Y offset and arm X/Z offsets.
  - Hid both legs at render time, matching the old `bipedLeftLeg.showModel = false` and `bipedRightLeg.showModel = false` branch.
  - Restored the old six fan-tail yaw animation using the `sin(age)` / `cos(age)` formulas and the original base yaw offsets.
  - Restored the old central tail segment wave for `tail6_1..9`: base pitch `0.1745F`, with the old `sin/cos((age - i) * 0.05)` X/Y/Z sway. The random sway factors are deterministic for repeatable screenshot comparison.
  - The hat/headwear marker is rendered fullbright after the main body pass, matching the old post-body `bipedHeadwear` render branch.
- Reintroduced the old Seven Tails render framing:
  - `SevenTailsRenderer` applies the old `MODELSCALE=14` translate/scale path without the commented-out forward Z offset, and keeps shadow radius at `scale * 0.5`.
  - Remaining Two/Five Tails stay on the shared first-pass `TailedBeastRenderer` until their quadruped legacy layers are ported.
- Scope note:
  - This closes the first-pass Seven Tails geometry/animation mismatch at code level. Integrated client comparison is still needed for flying/body posture, live rider placement, fan-tail orientation, Tail Beast Ball origin/timing, and screenshot similarity against the 1.12 reference.

### M6 Five Tails Legacy Model Geometry Slice

- Closed the old quadruped extraction gap before porting Five Tails:
  - `tools/extract_legacy_models.py` now recognizes legacy model classes extending `ModelQuadruped` in addition to `ModelBase` and `ModelBiped`.
  - Regenerated the legacy manifest and layer definitions. `audit/legacy_model_manifest.csv` now includes `EntityFiveTails.ModelFiveTails` and `EntityTwoTails.ModelTwoTails`; `audit/generated_layer_snippets_summary.json` now reports 70 compiled generated layer methods, up from 68.
  - This keeps the remaining Two Tails work on the generated-model path instead of hand-copying 1.12 geometry.
- Added a dedicated Five Tails client model and renderer, separating `ModEntityTypes.FIVE_TAILS` from the first-pass shared `TailedBeastRenderer`:
  - `FiveTailsModel.createBodyLayer()` delegates to `LegacyModelLayerDefinitions.EntityFiveTails_ModelFiveTails_222()`, preserving the old 64x64 `ModelFiveTails` geometry, head/jaw hierarchy, root-level fullbright `Eyes` layer, body, four quadruped legs, and 5x8 segmented tails under the body.
  - The model extends `EntityModel<TailedBeastEntity>` because the generated old layer is a true quadruped root, not a humanoid root.
  - Tail traversal now fails at bake time if any generated `Tail_i_j` segment is missing, matching the stricter generated-model slices used for the other legacy tailed beasts.
- Restored the old Five Tails runtime animation shape:
  - Recreated the old `ModelQuadruped` head and four-leg walk animation with `limbSwing * 2 / entity.height`, including the original `0.6662F` swing rate and `1.4F` stride amplitude.
  - Preserved the old final body orientation. The 1.12 path set quadruped body pitch to `PI/2` and immediately subtracted `PI/2`, so the 1.20 model leaves the generated body pitch at its baked zero pose.
  - Restored the old per-segment tail wave for `Tail_i_1..7` with `sin/cos((age - j) * 0.2)` randomized X/Z motion. The random sway factors are deterministic for repeatable screenshot comparison.
  - Restored the old shooting pose: head pitches down by `-0.1745F`, jaw opens to `0.7854F`, otherwise jaw resets closed.
  - The root-level `Eyes` layer copies only head rotation, not position, then renders fullbright after the main body pass. This matches the old `copyModelAngles(head, Eyes)` behavior without overwriting the eyes' separate baked root offset.
- Reintroduced the old Five Tails render framing:
  - `FiveTailsRenderer` applies the old `MODELSCALE=20` translate/scale path without the commented-out forward Z offset, and keeps shadow radius at `scale * 0.5`.
  - Remaining Two Tails stays on the shared first-pass `TailedBeastRenderer` until its quadruped/flame legacy slice is ported.
- Scope note:
  - This closes the first-pass Five Tails geometry/animation mismatch at code level. Integrated client comparison is still needed for live rider posture, mount camera placement, Tail Beast Ball mouth/origin timing, quadruped stride similarity, and screenshot similarity against the 1.12 reference.

  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0.
  - `python tools\validate_dedicated_server_safety.py`: 247 non-client Java files scanned, 0 client-reference issues.

### M6 Two Tails Legacy Model Geometry Slice

- Added a dedicated Two Tails client model and renderer, separating `ModEntityTypes.TWO_TAILS` from the first-pass shared `TailedBeastRenderer`:
  - `TwoTailsModel.createBodyLayer()` delegates to `LegacyModelLayerDefinitions.EntityTwoTails_ModelTwoTails_298()`, preserving the old 128x128 `ModelTwoTails` geometry, head/jaw hierarchy, root-level fullbright `eyes` layer, body, four quadruped legs, 2x8 segmented tails, and the parallel `headFlamed/bodyFlamed/legFlamed/TailFlamed` overlay hierarchy.
  - The model extends `EntityModel<TailedBeastEntity>` because the generated old layer is a true quadruped root with an additional flame overlay, not a humanoid root.
  - Base and flamed tail traversal now fails at bake time if any generated `Tail_i_j` or `TailFlamed_i_j` segment is missing.
- Restored the old Two Tails runtime animation shape:
  - Recreated the old `ModelQuadruped` head and four-leg walk animation with `limbSwing * 2 / entity.height`, including the original `0.6662F` swing rate and `1.4F` stride amplitude.
  - Preserved the old final body orientation. As with Five Tails, the 1.12 quadruped path set body pitch to `PI/2` and then subtracted `PI/2`, so the 1.20 model leaves the generated body pitch at its baked zero pose.
  - Restored the old per-segment tail wave for `Tail_i_1..7` and `TailFlamed_i_1..7` with `sin/cos((age - j) * 0.1)` randomized X/Y/Z motion. The random sway factors are deterministic for repeatable screenshot comparison.
  - Restored the old shooting pose: head pitches down by `-0.1745F`, base and flamed jaws open to `0.7854F`, otherwise both jaws reset closed.
  - The root-level `eyes` layer copies only head rotation, not position, then renders fullbright after the main body pass. This matches the old post-body `copyModelAngles(head, eyes)` branch without overwriting the eyes' separate baked root offset.
- Reintroduced the old Two Tails render framing and flame layer:
  - `TwoTailsRenderer` applies the old `MODELSCALE=10` translate/scale path without the commented-out forward Z offset, and keeps shadow radius at `scale * 0.5`.
  - The main model pass hides all flamed parts and renders the eyes fullbright after the base model.
  - A dedicated render layer draws only the flamed hierarchy with `textures/twotailsflames.png`, alpha `0.9`, fullbright light, and vertical UV scroll speed `0.01F`. It uses the shared scrolling render type where the cache key is only `(texture, speed constants)` and the current offset is computed inside the texturing state.
  - All tailed beast entity renderers now point at dedicated legacy model slices; the first-pass shared `TailedBeastRenderer` remains in source but is no longer registered for these entities.
- Scope note:
  - This closes the first-pass Two Tails geometry/animation/flame-overlay mismatch at code level. Integrated client comparison is still needed for live rider posture, mount camera placement, Tail Beast Ball mouth/origin timing, flame scrolling appearance, quadruped stride similarity, and screenshot similarity against the 1.12 reference.

### M6 Biju Cloak Legacy Visual Layer Slice

- Ported the old `ModelBijuCloak` visual path onto a custom player/mob render layer instead of the vanilla armor render path:
  - Added `BijuCloakModel`, backed by `LegacyModelLayerDefinitions.ItemBijuCloak_ModelBijuCloak_413()`, preserving the old 128x64 cloak geometry: inflated humanoid shell, six-segment ears, nine tails with eight segments each, and separate wear overlay parts for body/arms/legs.
  - Added `BijuCloakLayer`, attached to both player renderers and `JinchurikiCloneRenderer`, so the cloak renders once as a complete visual layer when the head/body/legs set matches the same tail count.
  - Added a Biju Cloak client item extension that hides the default vanilla armor model for cloak pieces, preventing the temporary diamond armor material from drawing under the custom layer.
- Restored the old Biju Cloak visual behavior at code level:
  - Recreated `tailShowMap` tail bitmask visibility for tail counts 0-9.
  - Recreated the old ear sway and tail segment sway formulas with deterministic random sway tables for repeatable screenshot comparison.
  - Restored old texture selection: one-tail sand cloak, level 1 cloak, level 2 cloak, and Kurama texture when tails are 9, cloak level is 2, and XP is at least 800.
  - Restored old fade-in alpha from `WearingBijuCloakTicks / 80`.
  - Restored old split-pass behavior: base body uses vertical UV scroll speed `0.01F`, Kurama body shine uses fullbright light, and the overlay/headwear pass renders fullbright without UV scroll.
  - Added `NarutoRenderTypes.scrollingEntityTranslucent(...)` so Biju Cloak can use scrolling UVs with the normal translucent entity shader instead of forcing all non-Kurama bodies through the emissive scrolling type.
- Scope note:
  - This closes the explicit old `ModelBijuCloak` geometry/animation/texture-switching mismatch at code level. Integrated client screenshot comparison is still needed for player sneaking posture, item/armor overlap with other visible gear, alpha fade appearance under vanilla transparency sorting, clone skin parity, and exact similarity against the 1.12 reference.

### M6 Ten Tails Biju Cloak Transformation Slice

- Wired the tail-10 Biju Cloak level-3 transformation path into the existing Ten Tails runtime:
  - `BijuManager.increaseCloakLevel(...)` now spawns and mounts `TenTailsEntity` for Ten Tails jinchuriki at cloak level 3, applies the same `cloakXp[2] * 5 + 200` lifespan formula used by ordinary biju, saves the spawned Ten Tails snapshot, and removes the cloak armor set.
  - Removed the old placeholder action-bar message that said Ten Tails cloak transformation was not wired into the generic cloak path.
  - The transformation reuses the current `TenTailsEntity` owner, singleton, SavedData, and rider logic. Non-creative Ten Tails riders still need the existing active Rinne Sharingan gate enforced by the entity runtime.
- Completed the mounted Tail Beast Ball path for Ten Tails:
  - Special Jutsu 3 release at cloak level 3 now accepts a Ten Tails mount when the player is assigned tail `10`, and delegates to `TenTailsEntity.performRangedAttack(...)`.
  - The existing one-tail through nine-tails mounted firing path is unchanged.
- Expanded debug output for this path:
  - `/narutoport saveddata jinchuriki increase_cloak` now reports nearby and loaded Ten Tails counts.
  - `/narutoport saveddata jinchuriki powerincrease_cloak` now reports nearby and loaded Ten Tails counts after the PowerIncrease route runs.
- Scope note:
  - This closes the explicit code-level Ten Tails level-3 cloak routing gap. Integrated client/play testing is still needed for the full Ten Tails jinchuriki flow: active Rinne Sharingan state, mounted control immediately after transformation, Tail Beast Ball release timing, SavedData restore after restart, and player logout/dimension edge cases.

  - Preserved the old visual vocabulary from `EntityWoodBurial`: multiple branches, old 2.0 segment render scale, 5-degree per-segment twist, target-width based branch count, and an approximately 50 tick growth window.
  - Exposed `WoodBurialEntity#getTargetForRender()` as a read-only render helper.
  - Switched `ModEntityTypes.WOOD_BURIAL` from the blank `ChidoriRenderer` path to `WoodBurialRenderer`; the existing shared `WoodSegmentModel.LAYER_LOCATION` remains the baked model layer.
  - Wood Burial target hold, in-wall damage, placed log/leaves root blocks, particles, sound, lifetime, save-data, and cleanup semantics were left unchanged.
- Scope note:
  - Old 1.12 Wood Burial spawned many `WoodSegment` entities with random yaw/pitch chains and late leaves. The current 1.20 runtime is one control entity plus placed root blocks, so this slice reconstructs the old visible segment branches client-side from the synced target instead of restoring the full multi-entity branch graph. Integrated client screenshot comparison is still needed for exact branch count, random branch curvature, growth timing, leaf/block overlap, target wrap/framing, and zero-depth bark plane culling against 1.12.

### M6 Futon Great Breakthrough Legacy Smoke Parameter Slice

- Corrected Futon Great Breakthrough smoke argument order against the old `EntityFutonGreatBreakthrough.EC.AirPunch` evidence:
  - Restored the old `SMOKE` argument mapping for the 50 wind particles emitted per execution: color `0x80FFFFFF`, scale `80 + random(20)`, lifetime `(int)(16 / (random * 0.8 + 0.2))`, default brightness, default viewer id, and default upward float.
  - The previous 1.20 code had the legacy scale and lifetime values reversed, producing smaller, longer-lived wind smoke than the old effect.
  - Confirmed Futon Vacuum already matches the old default-smoke lifetime path because it passes scale `10` and maxAge `0`, which the current `SmokeAtlasParticle` resolves through the legacy random lifetime formula.
  - Confirmed Futton Mist and Poison Mist intentionally keep scale `80-99` with maxAge `0`, matching their old `SMOKE` calls.
  - Great Breakthrough push, block break chance, range/far-radius, wind sound, duration, owner tracking, save-data, and lifecycle semantics were left unchanged.
- Scope note:
  - This is a particle parameter fidelity slice. Integrated client testing is still needed for smoke sheet size, wind cone density, block break visuals, entity push feel, and overlap with Vacuum Sphere/Futton/Poison mist effects.

### M6 Chidori Legacy Hand-Anchor Sync Slice

- Restored the old Chidori renderer-to-server hand anchor path while keeping the 1.20 server authoritative for particles, lightning entities, and damage:
  - Added `ChidoriHandPositionMessage`, mirroring the existing Rasengan hand-position sync pattern.
  - `ChidoriRenderer` now computes the local player's main-hand point from the humanoid arm model once per rendered tick for active `ChidoriEntity` and `ChidoriSpearEntity`, then sends it to the server.
  - The hand transform preserves the old Chidori local offsets: empty-hand palm point `(0, -0.7, 0)`, weapon spark line from `(0, -0.6875, 0.2)` to `(0, -0.6875, 1.6)`, main-arm side offset `0.0586 * +/-6`, crouch lowering, and body-yaw rotation.
  - `ChidoriEntity` accepts only owner-sent, finite, nearby hand positions and uses the fresh hand point for smoke and random near-hand lightning feedback; if no fresh point exists, it falls back to the previous eye/look approximation.
  - `ChidoriSpearEntity` accepts the same owner-validated hand point and uses it as the non-Ryu spear lightning start position; Ryu body-area arcs remain unchanged.
  - `CHIDORI` and `CHIDORI_SPEAR` still use the shared blank renderer for geometry; this slice adds hand-position sync behavior to that renderer without changing unrelated placeholder users.
- Scope note:
  - This fixes the biggest server-approximation gap for third-person/observer Chidori feedback. Full integrated client testing is still needed for first-person hand feel, offhand weapon sparks, left-handed players, pose timing under latency, and Chidori Spear damage-line alignment.

### M6 Suiton Hiding in Mist Legacy Fog Density Slice

- Restored the old Hiding in Mist player fog-density path against the `ItemSuiton.EntityMist` and `ProcedureRenderView` evidence:
  - Added `SuitonMistFogMessage` and registered it on the main network channel so the server can push per-player mist density to the client, matching the old `ProcedureRenderView.setFogDensity(entity, den)` packet model.
  - `SuitonMistEntity` now sends `phase / max(distanceOutsideRadius, 1)` to every affected `ServerPlayer` in the old `radius + 100` search box, including the caster when the caster is a player and preserving the old non-player-owner skip rule.
  - Removed the first-pass Blindness approximation from Hiding in Mist; player visibility is now driven by client fog instead of a potion effect.
  - Added `ClientSuitonMistFogState` with a short TTL and same-client-tick max-density merge so overlapping mist packets do not leave stuck fog but the strongest active packet wins for that render tick.
  - Added `SuitonMistFogEvents` on `ViewportEvent.RenderFog`, mapping the old exponential fog density into tightened 1.20 fog planes with near plane `0`, spherical shape, cancellation enabled, and far plane clamped from `0.85 / density` down to a 2-block minimum.
  - Mob follow-range reduction, mist build/dissipate timing, water-dependent idle duration, radius formula, particles, sound, save data, and cleanup behavior were left unchanged.
- Scope note:
  - This restores the system-level fog hook but still needs integrated client testing for exact perceived density, overlapping mist fields, caster/non-caster visibility, water/lava fog interaction, spectator/camera edge cases, and parity of old EXP fog versus the 1.20 near/far-plane approximation.

### M6 Suiton Hiding in Mist Legacy Target Glow Slice

- Restored the old Hiding in Mist caster-only target glow path against `ItemSuiton.EntityMist` and `ProcedureSync.SetGlowing` evidence:
  - Added `EntityGlowMessage` as a viewer-specific client packet, matching the old `ProcedureSync.SetGlowing.send(ownerPlayer, target, glow)` model.
  - Added `ClientEntityGlowState`, which applies `setGlowingTag(true)` on the receiving client, tracks the pre-existing glow flag, and clears expired entries with a short TTL so stale glow does not remain if a target leaves the mist area.

### M6 Earth Sandwich Legacy Falling-Block Motion Slice

- Restored the old Earth Sandwich wall-closing visual path against `EntityEarthSandwich.EC` and `EntityEarthBlocks.BlocksMoveHelper` evidence:
  - The old code converted each finished earth wall into a group of no-gravity `EntityFallingBlock` instances, moved both wall groups every tick at motion `0.15`, and released gravity through `BlocksMoveHelper#fall()` when the sandwich ended.
  - `EarthSandwichEntity` now converts the two completed `EarthWallEntity` block lists into no-gravity `FallingBlockEntity` instances via `FallingBlockEntity.fall(...)` instead of moving real world blocks by integer offsets.
  - The moving wall groups now receive the old per-tick direction motion from `snappedYaw +/- 90` with speed `0.15`, so the visible closure is continuous rather than one-block jumps every several ticks.
  - End-of-life cleanup now releases those falling blocks back to gravity instead of deleting or clearing the moved wall blocks.
  - Preserved the old wall build phase, paired wall centers, wall yaw, 100 tick move duration, obstacle breaking chance `0.1`, and collision-force-style entity damage scaling from motion speed times moving block count.
- Scope note:
  - This restores the main code-level visual semantics of the old `BlocksMoveHelper` path. Integrated client testing is still needed for exact FallingBlock placement/drop behavior after release, block collision resistance, very large wall packet load, multiplayer interpolation, and whether vanilla 1.20 FallingBlock collision matches the old helper's custom collision clipping.

### M6 Earth Sandwich FallingBlock Collision Filter Slice

- Tightened the new Earth Sandwich moving-wall path against the old `EntityEarthBlocks.BlocksMoveHelper#NOT_FALLINGBLOCK` evidence:
  - The old helper explicitly excluded `EntityFallingBlock` from its entity collision target predicate while moving a wall group.

### M6 Itachi Legacy Genjutsu AI Slice

- Restored the old Itachi look-triggered Genjutsu path against `EntityItachi.EntityCustom#updateAITasks` evidence:

### M6 Itachi Legacy Hit Dodge And Crow Escape Slice

### M6 Itachi Legacy Low-Health Susanoo Guard Slice

- Restored the old Itachi low-health Susanoo guard branch against `EntityItachi.EntityCustom#attackEntityFrom` evidence:
  - `ItachiEntity` now persists the old 1-in-5 "real Itachi" flag and `LastSusanooTime` cooldown state.
  - After the old dodge roll fails, a real Itachi now checks the old guard condition: still alive, incoming damage would leave health at or below one-third max health, not already riding, 600 ticks since the previous guard, and at least 300 legacy chakra.
  - Added a `SusanooClothedEntity.spawnFrom(LivingEntity, boolean, int, boolean)` overload so non-player owners can summon and mount a 600 tick half-body clothed Susanoo without changing the existing player-facing `spawnFrom(Player, boolean)` entry.
  - When the guard triggers, Itachi mounts the spawned Susanoo, consumes 300 legacy chakra, records the 600 tick cooldown, sets the attacker as target/revenge target, forwards the incoming hit to the Susanoo, and cancels damage to Itachi itself.
  - Existing Itachi Genjutsu, fall immunity, short dodge, crow escape, renderer, attributes, and generic player Susanoo behavior were left unchanged.
- Scope note:

### M6 Itachi Legacy Initial Loadout And Drop Slice

### M6 Itachi Legacy Ranged Tactical Combat Slice

- Restored the old Itachi tactical attack split against `EntityItachi.EntityCustom#initEntityAI` and `attackEntityWithRangedAttack` evidence:
  - `ItachiEntity` now implements the 1.20 `RangedAttackMob` interface and replaces the inherited always-melee ninja goals with the old Itachi distance gates: ranged at more than 4 blocks, melee at 4 blocks or less, and Susanoo reach when mounted.
  - Added the old vertical leap gate: Itachi only uses leap pursuit when not riding and the target is more than 3 blocks above him.
  - Restored the old 12-way ranged attack roll: 1/12 Amaterasu at distance factor > 0.3333 with 50 chakra, 2/12 Katon fireball at distance factor >= 0.5333 with 50 chakra, otherwise Kunai when not riding.
  - The Amaterasu branch uses the old `sharingansfx` sound and applies the 1.20 `AMATERASUFLAME` effect for 1200 ticks at amplifier 1.
  - The Katon branch launches the existing 1.20 `KatonFireballEntity` at legacy scale 5.0. `KatonFireballEntity` now has a direction-aware `configure(LivingEntity, Vec3, float)` overload while the existing owner-look overload remains unchanged for player use.
  - The Kunai fallback launches the existing `ENTITYBULLETKUNAI` projectile from Itachi's eye line at the old speed 1.0 and arrow-shoot pitch formula.
  - When Itachi is riding a clothed Susanoo, 5/12 ranged rolls now delegate to the existing 1.20 Yasaka Magatama launch at `MODEL_SCALE * 0.5`, matching the old `EntitySusanooClothed.EntityCustom#attackEntityWithRangedAttack` magatama handoff.
  - Melee attacks while mounted now route through the active `SusanooClothedEntity#doHurtTarget`, preserving the old "Susanoo swings and hits for Itachi" path.
- Scope note:
  - This restores the old tactical range split, ranged attack roll, vertical leap gate, and Susanoo attack handoff. The old Itachi boss bar tracking, peaceful target reset, invisible-target visibility override, team list, and spawn-density gate remain separate follow-up work.

### M6 Itachi Legacy Boss, Visibility, Team, And Spawn Gate Slice

- Restored the remaining Itachi-specific lifecycle and targeting tail behavior against `EntityItachi.EntityCustom#isOnSameTeam`, `updateAITasks`, `getCanSpawnHere`, `trackAttackedPlayers`, `onUpdate`, and `canSeeInvisible` evidence:
  - Added an Itachi `ServerBossEvent` with the old blue progress bar color. Player tracking hooks add/remove viewers, and the old `trackAttackedPlayers()` behavior is preserved by keeping the boss bar visible only for a player attacker or player target.
  - The boss bar health progress is refreshed every server tick with the current health ratio.
  - Restored the old Itachi team list: Itachi treats other Itachi entities and the registered `kisame_hoshigaki` entity type as allies, matching the old `TeamItachi = [Itachi, Kisame]` list.
  - Added the old invisible-target gate by overriding 1.20 `hasLineOfSight`: normal line of sight is still required, but invisible entities are only considered visible within the old 20 block / 400 square distance window.
  - Added an Itachi-local peaceful state counter. When Itachi has no live target and no live attacker, it increments peaceful ticks, regenerates 4% of the legacy 4000 chakra pool plus 1 health every 20 ticks, and clears its target after 200 peaceful ticks like the old `peacefulTicks > 200` branch.
  - Replaced the generic Itachi spawn placement with a dedicated spawn predicate in `ModSpawnPlacements`: the old monster spawn rule still applies, but natural Itachi spawns now also require no other Itachi within 128 blocks and pass the old 1-in-5 random gate.
- Scope note:
  - This closes the Itachi-specific items previously left after the ranged tactical combat slice. The old generic `ProcedureBasicNinjaSkills` path remains broader `NinjaMobEntity` parity work, not an Itachi-only tail item.

### M6 NinjaMob Legacy Basic Ninja Skills Slice

- Restored the old generic ninja-mob mobility path against `ProcedureBasicNinjaSkills` and `EntityNinjaMob.Base#onUpdate` evidence:
  - Added a 1.20 `ProcedureBasicNinjaSkills` helper under `net.narutomod.procedure`.
  - `NinjaMobEntity#tick()` now calls the helper on the server for every entity that inherits the common ninja mob base.
  - The helper restores the old 2 tick base buffs: Speed II, Haste II, and Jump Boost II, using the same hidden/non-ambient effect flags as the old procedure.
  - Restored the old water-standing behavior: a non-sneaking ninja mob in water gets vertical motion `0.01`, is marked on-ground, and receives an impulse update.
  - Restored the old wall-climb behavior: when airborne, looking upward, and raytracing a full collision block within 1 block, the mob gets vertical motion `0.3` in water/lava or `0.6 - (friction - 0.6) * 2` outside mud.
  - Restored the old fall-distance bleed: if fall distance is above 4, it is reduced by `0.75` each tick.
  - Restored the old common ninja feather-falling upkeep: every 200 ticks the mob receives 201 ticks of `FEATHER_FALLING` amplifier 1.
- Scope note:
  - This slice intentionally applies the old basic ninja skills to `NinjaMobEntity` descendants only. The old player post-tick call to `ProcedureBasicNinjaSkills` remains separate player-side parity work.

### M6 Player Legacy First Ninjutsu Affinity Grant Slice

- Restored the old one-time first ninjutsu grant path against `ProcedureOnPlayerPostTick` evidence:
  - Added `ProcedurePlayerLegacyNinjutsuGrant` as the 1.20 server-side helper for the old `FirstGotNinjutsu` branch.
  - `Chakra.ForgeEvents#onPlayerTick` now calls the helper inside the same effective gate as the old branch: ninja player with positive battle XP, current experience level >= 10, and server-side tick.
  - The helper preserves the old one-time `FirstGotNinjutsu` flag using the 1.20 player variables capability and syncs it after setting the flag.
  - If the player does not already have the base `ninjutsu` item, the helper grants it and writes the legacy affinity marker tag (`IsNatureAffinityKey`) onto the new stack.
  - If the player has none of the five base nature items, the helper grants the old initial nature affinity set from existing advancements:
    - Bakuton -> Doton + Raiton
    - Ranton -> Suiton + Raiton
    - Futton -> Suiton + Katon
    - Jiton -> Futon + Doton
    - Yooton -> Doton + Katon
    - Hyoton -> Futon + Suiton
    - Shakuton -> Katon + Futon
    - Kekkei Tota -> Katon + Doton + Futon
  - If none of those advancement gates are met, it preserves the old random single-nature fallback over Katon, Suiton, Raiton, Futon, and Doton.
- Scope note:
  - This slice restores the first ninjutsu and base nature affinity grant only. The old first-branch `ItemIryoJutsu` random/medical-genin advancement grant remains a separate player progression slice.

- Restored the old 20 tick Susanoo invalid-state cleanup path against `ProcedureOnPlayerPostTick`, `ProcedureSusanoo`, and `ProcedureSusanooSkeletonBodyTickEvent` evidence:

### M6 Player Legacy Deterministic Advanced Catch-Up Grant Slice

- Restored the deterministic part of the old 20 tick advanced/dojutsu catch-up branch from `ProcedureOnPlayerPostTick`:
  - `ProcedurePlayerLegacyDojutsuPostTick` now treats the old recent-dojutsu death penalty as the first `if` branch and only runs catch-up when that penalty branch is not active, preserving the old `if/else if` flow.
  - Added deterministic catch-up grants for players with battle XP >= 300, no dojutsu items, no advanced nature/Shikotsumyaku item exemptions, and no Jinchuriki state.
  - The restored advancement priority order matches the old branch: `byakuganopened`, `sharinganopened`, `kekkei_tota_awakened`, `shikotsumyaku_acquired`, `futton_acquired`, `ranton_acquired`, `yooton_acquired`, `shakuton_acquired`, `hyoton_acquired`, `jiton_acquired`, `bakuton_acquired`.
  - Byakugan/Sharingan catch-up stacks get the 1.20 owner marker and update `MOST_RECENT_WORN_DOJUTSU_TIME`, matching the old post-grant dojutsu owner/time block.
  - Advanced jutsu catch-up stacks receive the legacy affinity tag (`JutsuItem.AFFINITY_TAG`), matching the old `ItemJutsu.Base#setIsAffinity` post-grant block.
  - The Sharingan catch-up path restores old `GuiScrollGenjutsuGui.giveGenjutsu` semantics by ensuring an `inton` item exists and enabling `IntonItem.GENJUTSU` directly, rather than routing through scroll learning and adding unrelated learning side effects.
- Scope note:

### M6 Player Legacy Random Advanced Awakening Slice

- Restored the old 20 tick random awakening tail from `ProcedureOnPlayerPostTick` after deterministic catch-up fails:
  - The random path remains behind the old effective gates: no dojutsu item, no advanced nature/Shikotsumyaku item, no Jinchuriki state, battle XP >= 300, current XP level >= 10, no recent-dojutsu death penalty branch, and the old outer `0.001` roll.
  - Recreated the old `rngbase = 105 + (available ordinary Biju > 0 ? 5 : 0)` pool sizing.
  - Recreated the old sequential random roll chain for:
    - Byakugan -> grants `byakuganopened` and `byakuganhelmet`
    - Sharingan -> grants `sharinganopened`, enables Genjutsu on `inton`, and grants `sharinganhelmet`
    - Shikotsumyaku -> grants `shikotsumyaku_acquired` and `shikotsumyaku`
    - Yooton, Shakuton, Hyoton, Jiton, Bakuton, Ranton, Futton -> require either of their old base nature prerequisites, grant the matching advancement, backfill missing base nature affinity items, and grant the advanced item
    - Kekkei Tota -> requires Katon or Doton or Futon, grants `kekkei_tota_awakened`, backfills missing Katon/Doton/Futon affinity items, and grants `jinton`
  - Reused the existing 1.20 `BijuManager.availableTailedBeasts`, `getRandomAvailableTailedBeast`, and `setPlayerAsJinchuriki` APIs for the old final fallback when the Biju pool is available, including the legacy `chattext.biju.playerisjinchuriki` broadcast.
  - Random dojutsu grants flow through the same owner/time write as deterministic grants; random jutsu grants flow through the same legacy affinity tag write.
- Scope note:
  - This closes the random awakening item/advancement/tail assignment path in the old 20 tick branch. Broader post tick tail work such as inventory sync/debug hooks remains separate if still needed.

### M6 Player Legacy Inventory Body Render Sync Slice

- Audited the old `ProcedureSyncInventory` and `ProcedureDebug` tail from `ProcedureOnPlayerPostTick`:
  - `ProcedureDebug.executeProcedure` is empty in 1.12.2, so there is no runtime behavior to migrate.
  - `ProcedureSyncInventory` only calls `ItemOnBody.InventoryTracker.createOrSyncInventory` every 30 ticks for server players.
  - The old `ItemOnBody` path is visual inventory sync for rendering non-held items on player body parts; it is not gameplay inventory state.
- Restored the old body-mounted inventory item path in 1.20.1:
  - Added `ItemOnBody` with the old body parts and leg offsets, plus a server-side `InventoryTracker` that syncs changed tracked main-inventory slots every 30 ticks.
  - Added `InventoryTrackerSyncMessage` on the existing 1.20 network channel.
  - Added `ClientInventoryTrackerSync` as a client-only cache for other players' tracked inventory slots.
  - Added `InventoryItemLayer` to the player renderer, rendering local inventory directly and remote player inventory from the synced cache, while suppressing the selected hotbar slot like the old renderer.
  - Registered `InventoryItemLayer` alongside the existing Lava Chakra, Biju Cloak, and Sage Mode player layers.
  - Marked existing 1.20 equivalents of old `ItemOnBody` items: basic legacy swords, Hiramekarei, Kiba Blades, Kusanagi, Nuibari, puppet scrolls, and Summoning Contract default to torso; Kunai and Explosive Kunai use the old right-leg slot; Shuriken uses the old left-leg slot.
  - Kept `SenjutsuItem` out of this body item layer because its old skin-layer behavior is already covered by the dedicated 1.20 `SageModeHelmetLayer`.
- Scope note:
  - This restores player body-mounted item visibility and the old inventory sync hook. Ninja mob/clone body item rendering remains a separate renderer pass if those entities need the same visual treatment.

### M6 Clone Owner Inventory Body Render Slice

- Restored the old `EntityClone.ClientRLM.LayerInventoryItem` owner-inventory visual path for 1.20 humanoid clone renderers:
  - Added `BodyMountedItemRenderer` so player and clone layers share the same body-part attach, offset, rotation, scale, and item render transform.
  - Added `OwnerInventoryItemLayer`, which resolves a clone owner, requires the owner to be a player like the old `AbstractClientPlayer` check, reads the local owner's real inventory, and reads remote owners through the existing `ClientInventoryTrackerSync` cache.
  - Preserved the old clone duplicate suppression by skipping owner inventory stacks whose item matches the clone's main-hand stack, approximating 1.12 `isItemEqualIgnoreDurability` with 1.20 `ItemStack.isSameItem`.
  - Attached the layer to confirmed clone-family humanoid renderers: Kage Bunshin, Jinchuriki Clone, Explosive Clone, Limbo Clone, Bigger Me, and Mind Transfer Self.
  - Kept the existing player `InventoryItemLayer` behavior unchanged: local and remote players still skip the selected hotbar slot, while clone-owner rendering does not skip the owner's selected slot unless the clone itself is holding the same item.
- Scope note:
  - `TransformationJutsuRenderer` was not included in this slice because the old transformation entity does not flow through `EntityClone.ClientRLM.RenderClone`.
  - `NinjaMobEntity` was not included because the current 1.20 entity has no migrated equivalent of the old mob-owned `inventory` list that `EntityNinjaMob.LayerInventoryItem` rendered from.

### M6 Clone Owner Handedness Slice

- Restored the old clone handedness inheritance from `EntityClone._Base`:
  - Old 1.12 clone construction copied `summonerIn.getPrimaryHand() == EnumHandSide.LEFT` into the clone's `setLeftHanded` flag.
  - The 1.20 Mind Transfer Self body already copied owner handedness, but Kage Bunshin, Jinchuriki Clone, Explosive Clone, Limbo Clone, and Bigger Me were still using the default mob handedness.
  - Those five entities now call `setLeftHanded(owner.getMainArm() == HumanoidArm.LEFT)` during spawn/configuration, matching the old owner-primary-hand snapshot behavior.
  - This makes the clone active-item arm pose helper deterministic for left-handed owners and keeps copied main/off-hand equipment on the expected rendered arm.
- Scope note:
  - This restores spawn-time owner handedness inheritance at code level. Live client testing is still needed for left-handed player screenshots with blocking, bow-style jutsu charge, dual wield, and slim-arm skins.

### M6 Clone Owner Name Inheritance Slice

- Restored the old clone custom-name inheritance from `EntityClone._Base`:
  - Old 1.12 clone construction called `setCustomNameTag(summonerIn.getName())`, so clones carried the summoner's name.
  - Mind Transfer Self already copied `owner.getDisplayName()`, but Kage Bunshin, Jinchuriki Clone, Explosive Clone, Limbo Clone, and Bigger Me did not.
  - Those five entities now call `setCustomName(owner.getDisplayName())` during configuration, matching the existing Mind Transfer Self approach and the old owner-name snapshot behavior.
  - No `setCustomNameVisible(true)` was added, preserving the old behavior of assigning the name without forcing permanent nameplate visibility.
- Scope note:

### M6 Clone Kill XP Return Slice

- Restored the server-side XP return surface from old `EntityClone._Base` for the clone classes that directly descend from that old path:
  - Old 1.12 clones kept `collectedXPPoints`, manually produced `ModifiedXPOrb` instances on clone kills, accumulated XP when those orbs reached a clone, and finally called `summoner.addExperience(collectedXPPoints)` when the clone was removed.
  - Added `CloneExperienceRewards`, a common server-side helper that reads the killed `Mob#getExperienceReward()` and transfers accumulated points to the owner player with `giveExperiencePoints`.
  - Kage Bunshin, Jinchuriki Clone, Explosive Clone, and Limbo Clone now accumulate kill XP through `awardKillScore` and transfer it during their existing cleanup/removal paths.
  - The implementation does not alter normal mob loot drops, clone chakra refunds, camera restoration, Limbo visibility, or Explosive Clone fuse/explosion behavior.
- Scope note:
  - This restores the XP ownership outcome at code level. It intentionally does not recreate the old custom `ModifiedXPOrb` attraction visual yet; live gameplay testing is still needed for exact kill-credit coverage, especially ranged Tail Beast Ball and Explosive Clone explosion kills.
- Verification after the clone kill XP return slice:
  - `rg -n "collectedXPPoints|ModifiedXPOrb|onKillEntity|addExperience\(|CloneExperienceRewards|collectedExperience|awardKillScore|transferCollectedExperience|giveExperiencePoints|getExperienceReward\(\)" 1.12.2\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\EntityClone.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\CloneExperienceRewards.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\KageBunshinEntity.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\JinchurikiCloneEntity.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\ExplosiveCloneEntity.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\LimboCloneEntity.java`: reports the old XP collection/return path and the new shared helper plus four clone call sites.

### M6 Clone Melee Attack Helper Slice

### M6 Clone Owner Lifecycle Gate Slice

- Restored the old clone owner-lifecycle removal gate:
  - Old `EntityClone._Base#onUpdate` removed server-side clones when the summoner was missing, dead, sleeping, or disconnected.
  - Added `CloneOwnerState`, a shared server-side helper that treats `null`, dead, sleeping, and disconnected player owners as unavailable.
  - Kage Bunshin, Jinchuriki Clone, Explosive Clone, and Limbo Clone now use the shared gate instead of only checking `owner == null || !owner.isAlive()`.
  - Bigger Me and Mind Transfer Self are also owner-bound clone-family bodies, so they now use the same gate to avoid sleeping/disconnected owners leaving stale body entities behind.
- Scope note:
  - This restores the code-level cleanup gate. Live multiplayer testing is still needed for the exact tick timing when an owner sleeps, logs out, or disconnects while riding/controlling a body.

### M6 Clone Owner Follow Spacing Slice

- Restored the old clone follow-spacing behavior from `EntityClone.AIFollowSummoner`:
  - Old Kage Bunshin, Explosive Clone, and Limbo Clone used `AIFollowSummoner(..., stopRange=3.0F)`, which followed the owner outside 3 blocks and actively moved away when the clone got within half that stop range.
  - Added `CloneOwnerMovement`, a shared server-side helper for grounded owner-following clones. It keeps the existing far-owner teleport safety but now stops inside 3 blocks and asks path navigation to move away when the owner is within 1.5 blocks.
  - Kage Bunshin and Explosive Clone now use the old `0.6D` follow speed constant instead of the faster first-pass `0.9D/1.0D` values.
  - Limbo Clone keeps its custom no-physics flight movement, but now applies the same 3 block stop / 1.5 block push-away threshold instead of only damping movement near the owner.
- Scope note:
  - This restores the code-level follow spacing rule. Live combat testing is still needed for clone crowding around the owner, pathing in water/doors, and Limbo's no-clip spacing feel under movement.

### M6 Clone Hostile Targeting Hook Slice

- Restored the old clone targetability hook without reintroducing the 1.12 reflection-based AI patch:
  - Old `EntityClone.AITaskHook` watched spawned living entities and, when a target selector already had a player-targeting task, inserted an equivalent `EntityClone.Base` target task at a slightly higher priority.
  - Added `CloneTargetingEvents`, a server-side tick event that checks hostile `Enemy` mobs every 20 ticks and redirects them to nearby legacy targetable clones when they are idle or currently targeting that clone's owner.
  - The replacement is scoped to the old `EntityClone.Base` equivalents: Kage Bunshin, Explosive Clone, and Jinchuriki Clone. Limbo Clone remains excluded because old Limbo extended `_Base`, not `Base`, and has its own detection/invulnerability semantics.
  - The event respects hostile mob follow range, line of sight, `Mob#canAttack`, existing allied checks, and avoids stealing targets from unrelated combat.
- Scope note:
  - This restores the code-level "hostiles can pick clones like player targets" behavior in a Forge 1.20-safe way. Live combat testing is still needed for exact priority parity versus vanilla target goals and for modded hostile mobs with unusual targeting rules.
- Verification after the clone hostile targeting hook slice:
  - `rg -n "AITaskHook|addCloneToAITargetTasks|EntityAINearestAttackableTarget|EntityAIFindEntityNearestPlayer|EntityClone\\.Base|CloneTargetingEvents|findNearestTargetableClone|Enemy|canSwitchTarget" 1.12.2\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\EntityClone.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\CloneTargetingEvents.java`: reports the old reflection hook and the new 1.20 event replacement.

### M6 Jinchuriki Clone Idle Cloak Shutdown Slice

- Restored the old Jinchuriki Clone idle shutdown side effect:
  - Old `EntityJinchurikiClone.EntityCustom#updateAITasks` discarded the clone after `idleTime > 200` and then called `EntityBijuManager.toggleBijuCloak` on the summoner.
  - Current 1.20 clone already restored camera, game mode, health, invisibility tick, XP, and original jinchuriki tails on cleanup, but the idle path did not close the active Biju cloak.
  - The idle timeout branch now captures the `ServerPlayer` owner, performs normal clone cleanup, and then calls `BijuManager.toggleBijuCloak` only if the cloak level is still active.
  - The guard prevents accidental cloak activation if some other path already turned the cloak off before the idle cleanup ran.

  - This restores the code-level idle shutdown rule. Live testing is still needed for the full training loop: clone target loss, chest-piece `CloneID` refresh, cloak removal timing, and camera restoration in an integrated world.

### M6 Clone Same Owner Alliance Slice

- Restored the old same-summoner team rule from `EntityClone._Base`:
  - Old 1.12 `isOnSameTeam` treated the summoner and every `_Base` clone with the same summoner as allies, regardless of concrete clone subtype.
  - Added `CloneFamilyAlliances`, a shared server-side helper that recognizes owner-bound clone-family entities by owner: Kage Bunshin, Explosive Clone, Jinchuriki Clone, Limbo Clone, Bigger Me, and Mind Transfer Self.
  - Replaced the previous same-class-only `isAlliedTo` checks in those six entities with the shared same-owner check plus the vanilla team fallback.
  - This prevents same-owner clone-family entities from being picked as owner-defense targets or controlled-body attack targets, and keeps hostile target redirection from creating friendly-fire loops between clone subtypes.
- Scope note:
  - This restores the code-level same-owner alliance rule. It does not implement the larger Kage Bunshin controlled-clone state machine, clone auto-casting held jutsu, or live multiplayer combat validation.

### M6 White Zetsu Player Disguise State Slice

### M8 SpecialEvent Spherical Explosion Camera Shake Slice

- Restored the old client camera-shake side effect for large spherical explosions:
  - Old `EventSphericalExplosion` sent `ProcedureCameraShake.Message` every update while `radius > 20`, using range `32 * f + radius`, duration `80`, and scale `8 * f`, where `f = 1 - tr / radius`.
  - Added `CameraShakeMessage` to the 1.20.1 SimpleChannel and a client-only `ClientCameraShakeEvents` subscriber that applies the yaw/pitch/roll random offsets through `ViewportEvent.ComputeCameraAngles`.
  - Added `NetworkHandler.sendToPlayersNear(...)` using Forge `PacketDistributor.NEAR`, so `SpecialEvent` can broadcast by dimension and radius without referencing client classes from the server event path.
  - `tickSphericalExplosion(...)` now broadcasts the shake before the staged block scan, preserving the old current-ring falloff while keeping the existing incremental explosion scheduler.
- Scope note:
  - This restores the code-level camera shake path. Live integrated-client testing is still needed to tune perceived intensity because 1.20 camera events and frame cadence differ from the 1.12 `EntityViewRenderEvent.CameraSetup` path.
- Verification after the SpecialEvent spherical explosion camera shake slice:
  - `rg -n "ProcedureCameraShake|sendToClients|CameraShakeMessage|ClientCameraShakeEvents|sendSphericalExplosionCameraShake|ComputeCameraAngles|TargetPoint\.p|radius > 20|radius <= 20" 1.12.2\src\main\java\net\narutomod\procedure\ProcedureCameraShake.java 1.12.2\src\main\java\net\narutomod\event\EventSphericalExplosion.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\network\CameraShakeMessage.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\client\ClientCameraShakeEvents.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\network\NetworkHandler.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\event\SpecialEvent.java`: reports the old send point and the new message, client event, NEAR packet distributor, and spherical explosion call site.
  - `.\gradlew.bat '-Dnet.minecraftforge.gradle.check.certs=false' --no-daemon compileJava`: passes.

### M7 Chakra HUD Warning Overlay Slice

- Restored the old Chakra HUD warning path:
  - Old `Chakra.PathwayPlayer#warningDisplay` called `OverlayChakraDisplay.notEnoughChakraWarning`, which sent a 60 tick client warning and rendered the Chakra bar during the helmet overlay pass.
  - Added `ChakraWarningMessage` to the 1.20.1 SimpleChannel and a client-only `ClientChakraHudEvents` subscriber that renders the Chakra bar from synced player variables.
  - The HUD uses the old left anchor (`screenWidth / 2 - 200`), 80 pixel bar width, stacked over-capacity segments, yellow full segments, and red/cyan warning color toggle for the current segment and text.
  - `Chakra.PlayerPathway#warningDisplay` now sends the 60 tick warning packet instead of only printing the action-bar "Not enough chakra" text.
- Scope note:
  - This restores the base Chakra display and insufficient-Chakra warning overlay. The old Sage Mode green flame background (`ShowFlamesMessage`) remains a separate overlay slice because it is controlled by Senjutsu state transitions, not by Chakra consumption failure.
- Verification after the Chakra HUD warning overlay slice:

### M6 Haku Target-Driven Ice Senbon Loadout Slice

### M6 Haku Legacy Ice Spear Ranged AI Slice

- Restored the old Haku ranged Ice Spear combat surface:
  - Old `EntityHaku` implemented `IRangedAttackMob`, added `EntityNinjaMob.AIAttackRangedTactical(this, 1.25D, ICE_SPEARS_CD, 10.0F)`, and used `ICE_SPEARS_CD = 80`.
  - Old Haku consumed `ItemHyoton.ICESPEARS.chakraUsage` and called `EntityIceSpear.EC.Jutsu#createJutsu(this, target, 2f)`.
  - Added a dedicated `HakuEntity` and switched the `haku` registry entry from generic `NinjaMobEntity` to `EntityType<HakuEntity>`, preserving the current 0.525 x 1.75 dimensions and Haku attributes.
  - `HakuEntity` now implements `RangedAttackMob`, keeps local 4000 initial chakra with saved `HakuChakra`, consumes 20 chakra per Ice Spear volley, fires every 80 ticks at up to 10 blocks, and uses power `2.0F`.
  - `IceSpearEntity` now exposes `spawnAtTarget(...)`, matching the old targeted overload by spawning the volley near the user's eye position and aiming each spear at the target center.
  - Haku's old fire immunity and high-pitched voice are also restored at the entity level.
- Scope note:
  - This restores the code-level ranged Ice Spear combat loop. It does not yet restore Haku's Ice Dome support behavior, Zabuza leader follow/defense behavior, or a visible held-item layer for the custom model.

### M6 Haku Low-Health Ice Dome Support Slice

- Restored the old Haku low-health Ice Dome support path:
  - Old `EntityHaku` spawned an `EntityIceDome` at the current target position when Haku had an attack target, no active dome, `DOME_CD = 500` had elapsed, Haku had more than `ICEDOME.chakraUsage * 5`, and Haku health was below 50%.
  - Old Haku capped dome use at `DOME_SPEARS_CD + 180` ticks and called `shootSpears()` every `DOME_SPEARS_CD = 160` ticks, with the first trigger offset by 60 ticks after spawn.
  - `HakuEntity` now tracks `domeEntity`, `domeLastUsed`, and `domeSpearsLastUsed`, persists the two cooldown counters, spawns the dome at the target position, discards it after the old max usage window, and periodically triggers `shootSpears()`.
  - `IceDomeEntity` now has `spawnAt(owner, x, y, z)` / coordinate-based `configure(...)`, so NPC users can create the dome at a target location instead of only at the caster.
  - `IceDomeEntity` upkeep now recognizes `HakuEntity` owners and consumes Haku's local legacy chakra pool, preventing NPC-owned domes from instantly breaking because generic non-player `Chakra.pathway(...)` starts at zero.
- Scope note:
  - This restores Haku's self low-health dome behavior. The old Zabuza leader health trigger and `excludeEntity(leader)` behavior remain future work because leader following/defense is not restored yet.
- Verification after the Haku low-health Ice Dome support slice:
  - `rg -n "DOME_SPEARS_CD|DOME_CD|MAX_DOME_USAGE|ICE_DOME_CHAKRA|createJutsu\(this, target\.posX|shootSpears\(\)|spawnAt\(|configure\(LivingEntity owner, double|consumeLegacyChakra|tickLegacyIceDomeSupport|MAX_DOME_USAGE_TICKS|DOME_COOLDOWN_TICKS|HakuDomeLastUsed|HakuDomeSpearsLastUsed|owner instanceof HakuEntity" 1.12.2\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\EntityHaku.java 1.12.2\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\EntityIceDome.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\HakuEntity.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\IceDomeEntity.java`: reports the old Haku dome constants/spawn/spear trigger and the new Haku support loop plus targeted dome spawn/upkeep hooks.

### M6 Zabuza-Haku Legacy Pairing Slice

- Restored the old Zabuza/Haku pairing surface:
  - Old `EntityZabuzaMomochi#spawnHaku()` ran after the first 20 ticks, looked for the nearest Haku within a 128 x 32 x 128 box, spawned one at +/-3 blocks if none existed, and called `haku.setLeader(this)`.
  - `NinjaMobEntity` now applies that behavior only for `zabuza_momochi`, keeping a `legacyHakuPartner` field so the lookup/spawn happens once per Zabuza instance just like the old `haku == null` gate.
  - Old `EntityHaku` added `AIFollowLeader(this, 0.5d, 4f)`. `HakuEntity` now has a `FollowLeaderGoal` with the same base speed and stop range, and doubles follow speed while the leader has an active attack target.
  - Haku's low-health Ice Dome trigger now also recognizes the old leader-health condition: leader below 25% health can trigger the same dome support path when Haku has a target.
- Scope note:
  - The old `defendLeader()` method was commented out in 1.12.2, so this slice does not implement it or claim leader defense beyond following and the leader-health dome trigger.
  - Zabuza's own old water prison, water clone, water dragon ranged AI, call-for-help avoidance state, and boss bar remain separate future slices.

### M6 Zabuza Legacy Water Dragon Core Slice

- Restored the old Zabuza Water Dragon core combat effect:
  - Old `EntityZabuzaMomochi` used `WATERDRAGON_CD = 300`, `WATERDRAGON_CHAKRA = 200d`, `AIAttackRangedJutsu(this, WATERDRAGON_CD, 12.0F)`, and `attackEntityWithRangedAttack(...)` spawned `EntityWaterDragon` at power `1f`, consumed 200 chakra, then called `standStillFor(60)`.
  - `NinjaMobEntity` now applies this behavior only for `zabuza_momochi`: if the target is alive within 12 blocks and the 300 tick cooldown has elapsed, it consumes from a local 5000 legacy Zabuza chakra pool and calls `WaterDragonEntity.spawnFrom(this, 1.0F)`.
  - The local Zabuza chakra, Water Dragon cooldown, and stand-still counter are saved as `LegacyZabuzaChakra`, `LegacyZabuzaWaterDragonLastUsed`, and `LegacyStandStillTicks`.
  - The 60 tick stand-still side effect now stops navigation and clears horizontal motion while active, matching the old post-cast immobilization surface closely enough for the current 1.20 entity path.
- Scope note:
  - This restores the code-level Water Dragon cast/cost/cooldown/stall effect. It does not fully restore old `AIAttackRangedJutsu` repositioning/strafe behavior, water clone setup, clone water prison behavior, mist casting, or Zabuza boss bar.

### M6 Zabuza Legacy Hiding In Mist Slice

- Restored the old Zabuza Hiding in Mist cast path:
  - Old `EntityZabuzaMomochi` used `MIST_CD = 1200`, `MIST_CHAKRA = 100d`, initialized `mistLastUsed = -MIST_CD`, and when a live target existed called `ItemSuiton.EntityMist.Jutsu#createJutsu(...)`.
  - `NinjaMobEntity` now applies the same behavior only for `zabuza_momochi`: with a live target and the 1200 tick cooldown elapsed, it consumes 100 from the local legacy Zabuza chakra pool and calls `SuitonMistEntity.spawnFrom(this)`.
  - `LegacyZabuzaMistLastUsed` is saved to NBT and defaults to `-LEGACY_ZABUZA_MIST_COOLDOWN_TICKS`, preserving the old "can mist early once combat starts" behavior.
- Scope note:
  - This restores the cast/cost/cooldown surface. Exact mist visual density and AI perception effects are governed by the already-ported `SuitonMistEntity`; live client combat testing is still needed for parity.

### M6 Zabuza Legacy Boss Bar Tracking Slice

- Restored the old Zabuza boss bar tracking surface:
  - Old `EntityZabuzaMomochi` owned a blue progress `BossInfoServer`, added a tracking player only when that player was Zabuza's attacker or attack target, removed players on untracking, and each tick either added the attacked/targeted `EntityPlayerMP` or removed all players.

### M6 Zabuza Legacy Peaceful Recovery Slice

- Restored the old base ninja peaceful recovery behavior for the current Zabuza path:
  - Old `EntityNinjaMob.Base.PathwayNinjaMob#onUpdate()` incremented `peacefulTicks` while both attack target and attacking entity were absent or dead, restored `maxChakra * 0.04d` every 20 ticks, healed 1 health, and reset the counter when combat resumed.

### M6 Zabuza Legacy Low-Health Help/Avoid Slice

- Restored the old Zabuza low-health support loop:
  - Old `EntityZabuzaMomochi` added a player target goal and hurt-by target goal, used an `EntityAIAvoidEntity` keyed to `avoidTarget`, and when non-clone Zabuza was at or below 40% health with a revenge target and a 40 tick cooldown elapsed, it set `avoidTarget`, cleared its own attack target, called nearby Zabuza-team help, then resumed normal target selection after health rose above 60%.
  - `NinjaMobEntity` now adds a Zabuza-only nearest-player target goal, suppresses Zabuza target goals while an avoid target is active, and runs a Zabuza-only low-health support tick before Mist/Water Dragon casts.
  - The support tick sets the current attacker as `legacyZabuzaAvoidTarget`, clears Zabuza's active target, avoids that living entity within 10 blocks at speed 1.25, and assigns the attacker to nearby living Zabuza-team `NinjaMobEntity` instances within follow range x 8 vertical x follow range.
  - Avoid mode clears when the avoid target dies or Zabuza heals above 60%, after which the Zabuza-only player target and hurt target goals naturally become eligible again.
- Scope note:
  - This restores the non-clone help/avoid behavior against the current live Zabuza/Haku team path. Water clones, clone water prison, clone death water splash, and exact old target-task removal bookkeeping remain separate future work.

### M6 Zabuza Legacy Water Dragon Ranged AI Slice

- Restored the old Zabuza Water Dragon ranged-jutsu movement layer:
  - Old `EntityZabuzaMomochi` registered `EntityNinjaMob.AIAttackRangedJutsu(this, WATERDRAGON_CD, 12.0F)` above melee, and `AIAttackRangedJutsu` waited for its 300 tick cooldown, found a standable setup position 12 blocks away in the direction opposite the target, applied a launch velocity toward that position, then cast when the distance settled inside the old 10-14 block band.
  - `NinjaMobEntity` now owns a Zabuza-only `LegacyZabuzaWaterDragonGoal` at the ranged/avoid priority. The generic per-tick "cast if within 12 blocks" path was replaced by this goal-driven path.
  - The new goal preserves the old distance behavior: beyond 14 blocks it navigates toward the target at speed 1.0, below 10 blocks it stops navigation and strafes backward at 1.25, and between 10 and 14 blocks it attempts the existing Water Dragon spawn.
  - The actual cast still uses the already-restored core semantics: power `1.0F`, 200 legacy Zabuza chakra, 300 tick cooldown, and 60 tick stand-still.
- Scope note:
  - The setup-position standability check is mapped to 1.20 block support/collision APIs rather than the removed 1.12 `PathNavigateGround#canEntityStandOnPos` helper. Water clones and clone water prison remain separate future slices.

### M6 Zabuza Legacy Water Clone Core Slice

- Restored the old Zabuza water clone core lifecycle:
  - Old `EntityZabuzaMomochi` spawned at most one clone when non-clone Zabuza had a live target, at 100 tick cooldown, 500 chakra cost, and an initial `-100 + 40` cooldown offset.
  - `NinjaMobEntity` now mirrors that gate for `zabuza_momochi`: the original spawns one same-type Zabuza clone, records the cooldown only after a successful spawn, and plays the legacy `kagebunshin` sound.
  - The clone stores the original Zabuza UUID, resolves the original on server ticks, increments/decrements the original's runtime clone count on load/removal, and discards itself when the original dies or when the original has neither a live target nor a live avoid target.
  - The clone is configured to old combat scale: 10 max health, 1 attack damage, zero xp reward, and zero equipment drop chance despite using the same entity type and constructor loadout.
  - The clone is excluded from Haku partner spawning, Mist casting, low-health avoid/help, and Zabuza boss bar tracking; it can still use the restored ranged Water Dragon path, matching the old same-entity clone behavior.
  - Clone targeting follows the original's live target, then the original's avoid target, then recent hurt-by / hurt-mob targets within the existing 200 tick recency window.
- Scope note:
  - This intentionally does not restore clone water prison behavior or the old death-time temporary water/splash block effect. Those remain future slices so this change stays independently verifiable.

### M6 Zabuza Legacy Water Clone Prison Slice

- Restored the old Zabuza water-clone Water Prison trigger:
  - Old `EntityZabuzaMomochi` only let clones cast Water Prison. The clone checked that the target was not already trapped, target distance was at least 2 blocks, the 400 tick cooldown had elapsed, and the clone had 200 chakra; the old `EntityWaterPrison` creation itself only succeeded within 4 blocks and lasted 300 ticks for the Zabuza clone path.
  - `NinjaMobEntity` now gives same-type Zabuza clones their own `LegacyZabuzaWaterPrisonLastUsed` cooldown, uses the same 400 tick cooldown / 40 tick initial offset / 200 chakra / 300 tick duration constants, and only consumes chakra plus records cooldown after `WaterPrisonEntity.spawnFrom(this, target, 300)` succeeds.
  - The clone aims at the target with the old 90/30 look limits before attempting the trap.
  - While the clone is the active Water Prison trapper, it sets `NoAi`, stops navigation, and holds position; when the trap state clears, only the no-AI state that this hold path set is released.
  - This reuses the existing 1.20 `WaterPrisonEntity` implementation for block placement, target holding, 4-block maintain distance, FOV maintain gate, temporary floor water, splash release sound, trap state, and forced bow pose sync.
- Scope note:
  - This restores the Zabuza clone water-prison behavior without changing the shared `WaterPrisonEntity` mechanics. The old clone-specific death splash / one-block temporary water effect remains a separate future slice.

### M6 Zabuza Legacy Water Clone Death Water Slice

- Restored the old Zabuza water-clone death water effect:
  - Old `EntityZabuzaMomochi#onDeathUpdate()` handled clones specially: play the generic splash sound, schedule a flowing-water block at `new BlockPos(this).up()` for 10 ticks with no particles and no sounds, then remove the clone immediately.
  - `NinjaMobEntity` now hooks `die(DamageSource)` for `zabuza_momochi` clones, plays `SoundEvents.GENERIC_SPLASH`, schedules `Blocks.WATER` at `blockPosition().above()` through the existing 1.20 `SpecialEvent.setBlocksEvent(..., 10, false, false)`, runs the normal death path, then discards the clone.
  - Clone cleanup still goes through the existing `remove(...)` path, so the original Zabuza runtime clone count is decremented once.
  - Lifecycle removals that are not real deaths, such as original missing/dead or original disengaged, still use `discard()` and do not create the death water effect.
- Scope note:
  - This uses vanilla water rather than the old custom `Blocks.FLOWING_WATER` state because 1.20 no longer exposes the same 1.12 flowing-water block constant; the 10 tick `SpecialEvent` lifecycle preserves the old temporary-water behavior.

### M6 Zabuza Legacy Base Combat Slice

- Restored the old Zabuza base combat envelope:
  - Old `EntityZabuzaMomochi` constructed `EntityNinjaMob.Base(world, 80, 5000d)`, so the base 50 max health plus the legacy ninja-health modifier `0.005 * 80 * 80` produced 82 max health, and the entity experience/ninja level was 80.
  - `NarutomodMod` now registers Zabuza with `NinjaMobEntity.createLegacyZabuzaAttributes()`, preserving 82 max health, 100 armor, 0.5 movement speed, 10 attack damage, and 48 follow range.
  - `NinjaMobEntity` now gives original Zabuza an 80 xp reward while same-type water clones still override to 0 xp, 10 health, and 1 attack damage through the existing clone configuration path.
  - Restored the old Zabuza melee profile: Zabuza uses a dedicated melee goal at speed 1.5 and old reach formula `5.3 + target.width`, while non-Zabuza generic `NinjaMobEntity` instances keep the existing 1.2 speed melee goal.
  - Restored the old Zabuza leap target goal: on ground, with a live target at 3-12 blocks, 1-in-5 random chance, leap strength 1.0, and the same target-relative Y compensation as the 1.12 `AILeapAtTarget`.
  - Restored the old hit-reactive blocking window: when Zabuza is hurt, has a main-hand sword/item, is not no-AI, and is not already using an item, it starts using the main hand; after 20 ticks the use state is stopped. This maps to the existing 1.20 blocking behavior on `BasicMeleeWeaponItem` / Zabuza sword.
- Scope note:
  - This slice does not yet restore old side-inventory weapon hiding/swapping outside combat. Zabuza still visibly holds the sword in the current 1.20 path except where clone stats/drop behavior explicitly overrides it.
- Verification after the Zabuza legacy base combat slice:

  - `rg -n "createLegacyZabuzaAttributes|LEGACY_ZABUZA_NINJA_LEVEL|LEGACY_ZABUZA_MAX_HEALTH|LegacyZabuzaMeleeAttackGoal|LegacyZabuzaLeapGoal|LEGACY_ZABUZA_BLOCKING|startLegacyZabuzaBlock|tickLegacyZabuzaBlocking|createLegacyZabuzaAttributes\\(\\)\\.build" 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\NinjaMobEntity.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\NarutomodMod.java`: reports the new 82-health attribute registration, 80 xp value, Zabuza-only melee/ leap goals, and blocking tick window.

### M6 Zabuza Legacy Weapon Stow Slice

- Restored the old Zabuza side-inventory weapon stow behavior:
  - Old `EntityNinjaMob.Base` had a two-slot side inventory and swapped slot 0 with the main hand whenever a weapon existed and combat state changed. The combat flag was revenge target, active target, or a recent last-attacked entity within 100 ticks.
  - Old Zabuza spawned with the Zabuza sword in main hand, so the base swap hid it into side inventory while idle and pulled it back out when combat started.
  - `NinjaMobEntity` now has a Zabuza-only stored main-hand stack that mirrors this slot-0 behavior for the current Zabuza path.
  - Zabuza now hides its main-hand weapon while idle, redraws it when it has a live target, live attacker, or a recent live hurt target within 100 ticks, and skips swapping while actively using/blocking an item.
  - Water-clone Water Prison success explicitly hides the main-hand weapon, matching the old `swapWithInventory(EntityEquipmentSlot.MAINHAND, 0)` after prison creation.
  - The stored stack is saved under `LegacyZabuzaStoredMainHand` and restored through `ItemStack.of(...)`.
  - Non-clone Zabuza now drops the stored stack through `dropCustomDeathLoot(...)` if the sword is hidden at death; water clones still drop no stored/held equipment.
- Scope note:
  - This is a Zabuza-local restoration of the old side-inventory behavior. A general `NinjaMobEntity` side inventory for every old ninja NPC remains future work.
- Verification after the Zabuza legacy weapon stow slice:

### M6 NinjaMob Legacy Name And Water Movement Slice

- Restored two old `EntityNinjaMob.Base` defaults for the current 1.20 `NinjaMobEntity` path:

### M6 Haku Legacy Base Stats Slice

- Restored the old Haku `EntityNinjaMob.Base` stat envelope:
  - Old `EntityHaku.EntityCustom` called `super(worldIn, 60, 4000d)`, which set the ninja level / xp reward to 60.
  - Old `EntityNinjaMob.Base` used base max health 50 plus the `0.005 * level * level` ninja-health modifier. For Haku's level 60, this produces 68 max health.
  - `HakuEntity` now records the legacy level and derived max-health formula as constants, sets `xpReward` to 60 in the constructor, and registers attributes with 68 max health, 40 armor, 0.6 movement speed, 10 attack damage, and 48 follow range.

### M6 Zabuza Legacy Stored Item Render Slice

### M6 NinjaMob Legacy Hostile Sound Slice

- Restored the old hostile sound envelope for the shared `NinjaMobEntity` path:
  - Old `EntityNinjaMob.Base#getSoundCategory()` returned `HOSTILE` for `IMob` ninja mobs and `NEUTRAL` otherwise.

### M6 Zabuza Legacy Natural Spawn Gate Slice

- Restored Zabuza's old `getCanSpawnHere()` add-on rules in the 1.20 spawn-placement path:
  - Old `EntityZabuzaMomochi.EntityCustom#getCanSpawnHere()` required the base monster spawn check, no other Zabuza within a 128 block grown box, and `rand.nextInt(5) == 0`.
  - `ModSpawnPlacements` now registers `zabuza_momochi` with a dedicated predicate instead of the generic monster predicate.
  - The new predicate keeps `Monster.checkMonsterSpawnRules(...)`, adds the old 1-in-5 random gate, and rejects spawns when any existing `NinjaMobEntity` of the same `zabuza_momochi` type is within `new AABB(pos).inflate(128.0D)`.

  - This does not change the current biome modifier spawn weight/group data or reintroduce the old `DungeonHooks.addDungeonMob(...)` dungeon-spawner injection.
  - The old source line shows `EntityRegistry.addSpawn(..., 1, 1, 1, ...)`; the current data-layer replacement remains as previously audited, and this slice only restores the runtime per-spawn predicate.

### M6 Kisame Legacy Samehada Stow Slice

- Restored Kisame Hoshigaki's old Samehada side-inventory behavior on the current 1.20 `NinjaMobEntity` path:
  - Old `EntityKisameHoshigaki.EntityCustom#onInitialSpawn(...)` placed `ItemSamehada` into `EntityNinjaMob.Base` inventory slot 0 instead of the main hand.
  - Old `EntityNinjaMob.Base#updateAITasks()` swapped slot 0 with the main hand when the mob had a revenge target, an attack target, or a recently attacked entity within 100 ticks.
  - `NinjaMobEntity` now keeps a synced `LegacyKisameStoredMainHand` stack initialized to `ModItems.SAMEHADA` for `kisame_hoshigaki`.
  - The stored Samehada swaps into the main hand while Kisame is in combat and swaps back out when that combat flag clears, reusing the same target-driven timing as the restored Zabuza stow path.
  - Stored Samehada is saved to NBT, synced to clients, and dropped on death when it is stowed; the main-hand slot drop chance is set to 1.0 so an actively held Samehada also drops through the normal equipment path.
  - `KisameHoshigakiRenderer` now adds `KisameStoredItemLayer`, and `KisameHoshigakiModel` exposes `translateToBodyPart(...)` so the stowed Samehada renders on the torso through the shared `BodyMountedItemRenderer`.
- Scope note:
  - This slice does not restore Kisame's fused Samehada model, water dome, water clones, water prison AI, swimmer navigation swap, or fused-form attack effects. Those remain separate Kisame behavior slices.
  - This restores the old slot-0 Samehada stow/display/drop chain for the current named NPC entity only.

### M6 Kisame Legacy Blocking Slice

- Restored Kisame Hoshigaki's old hurt-triggered weapon blocking gate:
  - Old `EntityKisameHoshigaki.EntityCustom#attackEntityFrom(...)` started using the main hand when Kisame was hit by non-thorns entity damage, had a non-empty main hand, was not already blocking, and was past `lastBlockTime + BLOCKING_CD`.
  - Old Kisame used `BLOCKING_CD = 30` for re-trigger cooldown and reset the active hand after `lastBlockTime + 20`.
  - `NinjaMobEntity` now maps those conditions for `kisame_hoshigaki`: entity-sourced damage only, excludes `DamageTypes.THORNS`, requires main-hand Samehada/weapon already drawn, and starts `InteractionHand.MAIN_HAND`.
  - The new tick path stops Kisame's active item use after the old 20 tick blocking duration while preserving the 30 tick re-trigger cooldown.
- Scope note:

### M6 Kisame Legacy Player Target Slice

- Restored Kisame Hoshigaki's old player target selection gate:
  - Old `EntityKisameHoshigaki.EntityCustom#initEntityAI()` registered `EntityAINearestAttackableTarget(this, EntityPlayer.class, true, false)` at target priority 1.

### M6 Kisame Legacy Water Shark Slice

- Restored Kisame Hoshigaki's old water shark ranged-jutsu behavior:
  - Old Kisame registered `EntityNinjaMob.AIAttackRangedJutsu(this, WATERSHARK_CD, 15.0F)` at goal priority 1.
  - Old constants were `WATERSHARK_CD = 80` and `WATERSHARK_CHAKRA = 75d`.
  - Old ranged attack used power `1f + rand.nextFloat()` in normal form, consumed `75 * power` chakra, spawned `EntitySuitonShark`, and called `standStillFor(80)`.
  - `NinjaMobEntity` now gives `kisame_hoshigaki` a 10,000 chakra pool, 4% out-of-combat chakra recovery per second, and the old passive 1 health/sec out-of-combat heal.
  - `LegacyKisameWaterSharkGoal` restores the shared old ranged-jutsu rhythm: wait for the 80 tick cooldown, find a standable setup point about 15 blocks away from the target, leap to it, strafe around the 13-17 block band, then fire `WaterSharkEntity.spawnFrom(this, power)`.
  - Kisame chakra and peaceful recovery ticks are persisted in NBT through `LegacyKisameChakra` and `LegacyKisamePeacefulTicks`.
- Scope note:
  - This restores normal-form water shark behavior only. The old fused-form fixed power 5 branch remains future work because the fused Kisame/Samehada model and state are not restored yet.
  - Water clone, water prison, water dome, and fused Samehada counter/damage-reduction behavior remain separate Kisame slices.
  - Zabuza's existing water dragon, clone, prison, and support logic were not changed.

### M6 Kisame Legacy Water Clone Slice

- Restored Kisame Hoshigaki's old water clone lifecycle:
  - Old Kisame used `WATERCLONE_CD = 100`, `WATERCLONE_CHAKRA = 500d`, and `cloneLastUsed = -WATERCLONE_CD`.
  - Old original Kisame spawned clones only while not fused, not a clone, in combat, and below 2 active clones.
  - Old clone construction linked the clone back to its original, incremented the original clone count, copied position/rotation, set max health to 10, attack damage to 1, and XP to 0.
  - Old clone death decremented the original clone count and left a temporary water block above the clone.
  - `NinjaMobEntity` now tracks separate Kisame clone state (`LegacyKisameOriginal`, clone count, counted/cleanup guards, and `LegacyKisameCloneLastUsed`) so Zabuza and Kisame clone counters do not overlap.
  - Kisame clones now use the same entity type, keep the existing Kisame AI goals, get 10 health / 1 attack damage / no equipment drops / 0 XP, sync to the original's combat target, and disappear if the original dies or loses its target.
  - Clone death now reuses the temporary water-block path already used by Zabuza clones.
- Scope note:
  - This restores clone spawning, clone lifecycle, clone stats, clone count cleanup, NBT linkage, and death water only.
  - Old Kisame clone water prison behavior is intentionally left for the next slice.
  - Old fused-form suppression is not active yet because fused Kisame state/model is still a future slice.
- Verification after the Kisame legacy water clone slice:
  - `rg -n "WATERCLONE_CD|WATERCLONE_CHAKRA|clones < 2|cloneLastUsed|new EntityCustom\\(this\\)|this\\.original = cloneFrom|MAX_HEALTH\\)\\.setBaseValue\\(10D\\)|ATTACK_DAMAGE\\)\\.setBaseValue\\(1D\\)|experienceValue = 0|setDead\\(\\)|original\\.clones|originalUUID" 1.12.2\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\EntityKisameHoshigaki.java`: reports the old clone cooldown/cost/count gate, clone constructor stats, clone death cleanup, and NBT original UUID.
  - `rg -n "LEGACY_KISAME_WATER_CLONE|legacyKisameOriginal|legacyKisameClone|isLegacyKisameClone|tickLegacyKisameWaterClone|spawnLegacyKisameWaterClone|configureLegacyKisameClone|tickLegacyKisameCloneLifecycle|cleanupLegacyKisameCloneReference|LegacyKisameOriginal|LegacyKisameCloneLastUsed|spawnLegacyCloneDeathWater" 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\NinjaMobEntity.java`: reports the new Kisame clone constants, original linkage, spawn/lifecycle/cleanup paths, NBT keys, and death-water path.

### M6 Kisame Legacy Clone Water Prison Slice

- Restored Kisame Hoshigaki clone-only water prison behavior:

  - Old water prison fired only from clones, required a live target that was not already trapped, checked enough chakra, spawned a 300 tick water prison, hid the main-hand Samehada, consumed 200 chakra, and updated `prisonLastUsed`.
  - Old clone tick also set clone AI disabled while `EntityWaterPrison.isEntityTrapping(this)` was true.

### M6 Kisame Legacy Fused Water Dome Logic Slice

- Restored Kisame Hoshigaki's old fused-form server logic:
  - Old Kisame used `WATERSHOCK_CHAKRA = 600d`, created `EntityWaterShockwave` with power/radius `20f`, set `useAltModel` true, applied `FUSED_HEALTH` +100 max health, and healed to the new max when the non-clone original had a live target and dropped below one third health.
  - Old fused form shut down after `peacefulTicks > 200`, called `waterDome.setShouldDie()`, and set `useAltModel` false.
  - Old fused water shark used fixed `power = 5f` instead of `1f + rand.nextFloat()`.
  - Old original stopped creating water clones while fused, and existing clones died if their original entered fused form.
  - Old fused form was not pushed by water and used water slowdown `1.0f`; normal Kisame used `0.8f`.
  - `NinjaMobEntity` now has a synced `LegacyKisameFused` flag, applies/removes a transient +100 max-health modifier, starts/stops the existing 1.20 `WaterShockwaveEntity` dome at radius 20, restores fused water-shark power 5, suppresses clone spawning while fused, discards active Kisame clones when the original fuses, and maps the water slowdown/fluid-push behavior.
- Scope note:
  - This slice restores fused-form server behavior and synced state only. The fused model geometry/texture switch and hidden render layers remain a separate client-render slice.
  - The old commented Samehada fused counterdamage/damage-reduction branches remain future work because they were commented in the 1.12 source.

  - `rg -n "LEGACY_KISAME_FUSED|LEGACY_KISAME_WATER_SHOCKWAVE|LegacyKisameFused|isLegacyKisameFused|tickLegacyKisameFusedForm|ensureLegacyKisameWaterDome|deactivateLegacyKisameWaterDome|WaterShockwaveEntity\\.toggleFrom|isPushedByFluid|getWaterSlowDown|5\\.0F|original\\.isLegacyKisameFused" 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\NinjaMobEntity.java`: reports the new fused synced data, health modifier, NBT key, trigger/exit paths, water dome calls, fused water-shark power, clone suppression, and water movement mapping.

### M6 Kisame Legacy Fused Render Slice

  - Old renderer swapped to `ModelKisameFused`, whose extra geometry was the left/right arm fins.
  - Old renderer skipped normal render layers while fused, so the stowed Samehada/body layer was not drawn over the fused body.
