### M6 Kisame Legacy Boss Bar Slice

- Restored Kisame Hoshigaki's old boss-bar tracking:
  - Old Kisame owned a `BossInfoServer` with blue progress overlay.
  - Old non-clone Kisame tracked attacked/targeted players each tick, updated `bossInfo` with `health / maxHealth`, added tracking players when they were the attacker or target, and removed players when they stopped tracking.
  - `NinjaMobEntity` now has a dedicated `legacyKisameBossEvent`, updates it each server tick for non-clone `kisame_hoshigaki`, and excludes Kisame water clones from boss-bar ownership.
  - The progress calculation uses current `getMaxHealth()`, so the restored fused +100 max-health modifier is reflected in the boss bar.
- Scope note:

### M6 Kisame Legacy Samehada Block Counter Slice

- Restored Kisame Hoshigaki's old successful-block counterdamage:
  - Old Kisame overrode `blockUsingShield(...)`, called `super.blockUsingShield(attacker)`, and for non-clone Kisame reflected `2f + rand.nextFloat() * 8f` thorns damage to attackers that were not another Kisame entity.
  - `NinjaMobEntity` now maps that behavior through 1.20's `blockUsingShield(LivingEntity attacker)` hook.
  - The new path excludes Kisame water clones and excludes attackers whose entity type is `kisame_hoshigaki`, matching the old `!(attacker instanceof EntityCustom)` check without excluding unrelated shared `NinjaMobEntity` types.
  - The reflected damage uses `damageSources().thorns(this)`, matching the old `DamageSource.causeThornsDamage(this)` semantics.
- Scope note:
  - This restores only the active old block-counter path. The fused Samehada attack drain/counter block inside `attackEntityFrom` and `attackEntityAsMob` was commented out in the 1.12 source and remains out of scope.
- Verification after the Kisame legacy Samehada block counter slice:
  - `rg -n "blockUsingShield\\(EntityLivingBase attacker\\)|super\\.blockUsingShield\\(attacker\\)|!this\\.isClone\\(\\)|attacker instanceof EntityCustom|causeThornsDamage\\(this\\)|2f \\+ this\\.rand\\.nextFloat\\(\\) \\* 8f" 1.12.2\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\EntityKisameHoshigaki.java`: reports the old active block-counter override, non-clone gate, same-Kisame exclusion, thorns source, and 2-10 damage range.
  - `rg -n "blockUsingShield\\(LivingEntity attacker\\)|super\\.blockUsingShield\\(attacker\\)|damageSources\\(\\)\\.thorns\\(this\\)|2\\.0F \\+ getRandom\\(\\)\\.nextFloat\\(\\) \\* 8\\.0F|attacker\\.getType\\(\\) != ModEntityTypes\\.KISAME_HOSHIGAKI|!isLegacyKisameClone\\(\\)" 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\NinjaMobEntity.java`: reports the new 1.20 block hook, thorns source, damage range, same-type exclusion, and clone exclusion.

### M6 Itachi Legacy Kunai Knockback Slice

### M6 Legacy Merchant Natural Spawn Gate Slice

- Restored the shared old natural-spawn gate for Iruka Sensei, Sakura Haruno, and Tenten:
  - Old `EntityNinjaMerchant.Base#getCanSpawnHere()` required a nearby village within 32 blocks, fewer than two ninja merchants around the village center in a 96x10x96 box, and a `rand.nextInt(10) == 0` gate before falling back to normal mob spawn rules.
  - Old Iruka, Sakura, and Tenten then each added their own same-class 128x16x128 nearby exclusion.
  - `VillagePoiHelper` now exposes `findLegacyMerchantContext(...)`, mapping the old 32-block village lookup to current POI/villager evidence.
  - `ModSpawnPlacements` now registers Iruka, Sakura, and Tenten with a shared legacy merchant predicate: normal ambient ground spawn rules, 1-in-10 roll, current village context, fewer than two existing legacy merchant NPCs, and same-class 128x16x128 exclusion.
  - The merchant-count box includes current Iruka, Sakura, Tenten, and Might Guy because old Might Guy also extended `EntityNinjaMerchant.Base`.
- Scope note:
  - Might Guy's own stricter old natural-spawn gate is intentionally not changed here; his old path also required a large village and no existing Might Guy, and current Might Guy already has separate village-spawn work.

### M6 Legacy Merchant Reputation Tier Slice

- Restored old reputation-gated merchant trade tiers for Iruka Sensei, Sakura Haruno, and Tenten:
  - Old `EntityNinjaMerchant.Base#getRecipes(player)` selected `tradeList[clamp(getTradeLevel(player), 0, length - 1)]`.
  - Old Iruka/Sakura/Tenten each computed trade level as nearby village reputation divided by 3, and old `useRecipe(...)` incremented that reputation by 1 after a successful trade.
  - Added `LegacyMerchantOfferTiers`, which stores a player-scoped legacy merchant reputation (`NarutomodLegacyMerchantReputation`), returns tier 0 until reputation reaches 3, returns tier 1 afterward, increments reputation on successful trade, and persists tier-specific offer lists.
  - Iruka now exposes tier 0: golden apple, body replacement scroll; tier 1: Kage Bunshin scroll, enchanted golden apple.
  - Sakura now exposes tier 0: baked potato, military rations pills; tier 1: healing scroll, golden military rations pill.
  - Tenten now exposes tier 0: shuriken, kunai, explosive tags; tier 1: explosive kunai, chokuto.
- Scope note:
  - 1.20 no longer has the same direct per-village reputation object used by 1.12, so this slice maps the old reputation progression to player persistent data shared by the restored legacy ninja merchants.

### M6 Legacy Merchant Hostility Reputation Slice

- Restored old hostile-player handling for Iruka Sensei, Sakura Haruno, and Tenten:
  - Old `EntityNinjaMerchant.Base#setRevengeTarget(...)` added attacking players to an in-memory refusal list and reduced their village reputation by 1.
  - Old `processInteract(...)` refused to open the trade screen for players in that refusal list.
  - Old `onDeath(...)` reduced the attacking player's village reputation by 5.
  - `LegacyMerchantOfferTiers` now tracks an in-memory hostile player UUID set per merchant entity, blocks trade opening for those players, subtracts 1 legacy merchant reputation when they successfully hurt the merchant, and subtracts 5 when they kill the merchant.
  - The refusal set intentionally stays entity-local and non-persistent, matching the old unsaved `assholeList`.
- Scope note:
  - This maps the old per-village reputation penalties to the restored player-scoped legacy merchant reputation used by the reputation-tier slice.
- Verification after the legacy merchant hostility reputation slice:
  - `rg -n "assholeList|processInteract\\(EntityPlayer player|!this\\.assholeList\\.contains\\(player\\)|setRevengeTarget\\(@Nullable EntityLivingBase|modifyPlayerReputation\\(livingBase\\.getUniqueID\\(\\), -1\\)|onDeath\\(DamageSource cause\\)|modifyPlayerReputation\\(this\\.attackingPlayer\\.getUniqueID\\(\\), -5\\)" 1.12.2\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\EntityNinjaMerchant.java`: reports the old refusal list, trade gate, hurt penalty, and death penalty.
  - `rg -n "hostilePlayers|canTradeWith|markHostile|penalizeDeath|adjustReputation\\(player, -1\\)|adjustReputation\\(player, -5\\)|offerTiers\\.canTradeWith\\(player\\)|offerTiers\\.markHostile\\(player\\)|offerTiers\\.penalizeDeath\\(player\\)" 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\LegacyMerchantOfferTiers.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\IrukaSenseiEntity.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\SakuraHarunoEntity.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\TentenEntity.java`: reports the new refusal set, interaction gate, hurt penalty, and death penalty.

### M6 Legacy Merchant Trade Interaction Gate Slice

- Restored the old trade-screen opening conditions for Iruka Sensei, Sakura Haruno, and Tenten:
  - Old `EntityNinjaMerchant.Base#processInteract(...)` opened the villager trade screen only when the player's clicked hand was empty, the merchant was alive, the merchant was not already trading, and the player was not sneaking.
  - The current Iruka/Sakura/Tenten merchant ports now use `LegacyMerchantOfferTiers.canOpenTradeScreen(...)` to require the same empty-hand, non-sneaking, not-currently-trading, and not-hostile-player gates.

### M6 Legacy Merchant Trading Stop And Watch Slice

- Restored old in-trade stop-and-watch behavior for Iruka Sensei, Sakura Haruno, and Tenten:
  - Old `EntityNinjaMerchant.Base.AITradePlayer` only stayed active while the customer existed, was within 4 blocks, and still had a container open.
  - Old `AITradePlayer#startExecuting()` cleared merchant navigation, and old `AIWatchCustomer` made the merchant look at its current customer while trading.
  - `LegacyMerchantOfferTiers.tickTrading(...)` now validates the active trading player, rejects closed menus or players farther than 4 blocks, stops navigation, and points look control at the player.
  - Iruka, Sakura, and Tenten now tick this shared trading upkeep on the server and clear `tradingPlayer` once the old conditions no longer hold.

### M6 Legacy Merchant Trade Update Sound Slice

- Restored old trade-input feedback sounds for Iruka Sensei, Sakura Haruno, and Tenten:
  - Old `EntityNinjaMerchant.Base#verifySellingItem(...)` played villager no/yes sounds on the server depending on whether the current trade output stack was empty.

### M6 Legacy Merchant Restock Timer Slice

- Restored old out-of-stock trade restock timing for Iruka Sensei, Sakura Haruno, and Tenten:
  - Old `EntityNinjaMerchant.Base#useRecipe(...)` started a 6000-tick reset timer when a traded recipe became disabled.
  - Old `updateAITasks()` only decremented that timer while the merchant was not trading.
  - When the timer expired, old code added one extra max use to every disabled recipe in the active recipe list.
  - `LegacyMerchantOfferTiers` now starts a 6000-tick `recipeResetTime` when a `MerchantOffer` becomes out of stock, decrements it only outside active trading, and restocks generated tier offers by rebuilding out-of-stock offers from their own NBT with `maxUses + 1`.
- Scope note:
  - 1.20.1 exposes `MerchantOffer#getMaxUses()` and NBT construction, but not a public direct equivalent of 1.12's `increaseMaxTradeUses(1)`, so the port preserves the offer through `createTag()` and changes only `maxUses`.
- Verification after the legacy merchant restock timer slice:
  - `rg -n "recipeResetTime|isRecipeDisabled\\(\\)|increaseMaxTradeUses\\(1\\)|!this\\.isTrading\\(\\)|getRecipes\\(this\\.getCustomer\\(\\)\\)|useRecipe\\(MerchantRecipe recipe\\)" 1.12.2\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\EntityNinjaMerchant.java`: reports the old reset timer, out-of-stock trigger, non-trading countdown, and max-use increment.
  - `rg -n "RECIPE_RESET_TICKS|recipeResetTime|isOutOfStock\\(\\)|restockOutOfStockOffers|OFFER_MAX_USES_TAG|getMaxUses\\(\\) \\+ 1|new MerchantOffer\\(tag\\)|tickLegacyTrading|tickTrading\\(" 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\LegacyMerchantOfferTiers.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\IrukaSenseiEntity.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\SakuraHarunoEntity.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\TentenEntity.java`: reports the new timer, restock path, and per-entity tick call sites.
  - `.\gradlew.bat '-Dnet.minecraftforge.gradle.check.certs=false' --no-daemon compileJava`: passes.

### M6 Legacy Merchant Ninja Leap AI Slice

- Restored old ninja leap behavior for Iruka Sensei, Sakura Haruno, and Tenten:
  - Old `EntityNinjaMerchant.Base` added `EntityNinjaMob.AILeapAtTarget(this, 1.0F)` at priority 2 for merchant-derived ninja entities.
  - Old leap logic triggered while grounded, with a target between 3 and 12 blocks away, at a 1-in-5 random chance, and set motion toward the target using the old horizontal plus height-difference formula.
  - Added `LegacyNinjaLeapAtTargetGoal` to mirror that old formula in 1.20.1.

### M6 Legacy Merchant Village Home And Door AI Slice

- Restored old village home-restriction and door-navigation behavior for Iruka Sensei, Sakura Haruno, and Tenten:
  - Old `EntityNinjaMerchant.Base` enabled ground navigation through doors, registered `EntityAIOpenDoor(this, true)`, and registered `EntityAIMoveTowardsRestriction(this, 0.8D)`.
  - Old `updateAITasks()` refreshed village home data every 70-119 ticks, found the nearest village within 48 blocks, detached home when no village was found, or set home to the village center with radius `villageRadius * 0.6`.
  - Added `LegacyMerchantVillageBehavior`, which configures `GroundPathNavigation` to open/pass doors and refreshes a 1.20.1 `restrictTo(...)` home area from the existing POI-backed legacy merchant village context.
  - Iruka, Sakura, and Tenten now register `OpenDoorGoal` plus `MoveTowardsRestrictionGoal` and tick the shared village-home updater.
- Scope note:
  - 1.20.1 no longer exposes the old `Village` object or door-count radius directly; this slice maps the old center/radius concept to the already-restored POI/villager `VillagePoiHelper.Context`.

### M6 Legacy Merchant Watch Nearby AI Slice

- Restored old non-trading watch behavior for Iruka Sensei, Sakura Haruno, and Tenten:
  - Old `EntityNinjaMerchant.Base` registered `EntityAIWatchClosest(this, EntityPlayer.class, 3)` and `EntityAIWatchClosest(this, EntityLiving.class, 8)` for idle awareness.
  - Old trading-specific `AIWatchCustomer` is already covered by the shared trading upkeep slice, which points look control at the active trading player.
  - Iruka, Sakura, and Tenten now register `LookAtPlayerGoal(this, Player.class, 3.0F)` and `LookAtPlayerGoal(this, LivingEntity.class, 8.0F)` at the same low priorities.

### M6 Legacy Merchant Defend Villagers Zombie Target Slice

- Restored the directly mappable zombie-defense branch from old `AIDefendVillage` for Iruka Sensei, Sakura Haruno, and Tenten:
  - Old `EntityNinjaMerchant.Base` registered `AIDefendVillage` as target priority 1.
  - Old `findNearestVillagerChaser(...)` searched village villagers and selected nearby zombies within each villager's `8 x 3 x 8` inflated box.
  - `LegacyMerchantVillageBehavior.isVillagerChaser(...)` now maps that behavior to the POI-backed legacy merchant village context and checks whether a candidate zombie is close to a living villager inside that village context.
  - Iruka, Sakura, and Tenten now register a high-priority `NearestAttackableTargetGoal` for those villager-chasing zombies; Tenten's hurt-by-target goal is moved to priority 2 to match the old merchant-base ordering.
- Scope note:
  - The old `Village#findNearestVillageAggressor(...)` and `Village#getNearestTargetPlayer(...)` parts depend on removed 1.12 village reputation/aggressor internals. This slice restores the concrete zombie-near-villager branch first and leaves the fuzzier village-aggressor table mapping for a separate pass.

### M6 Legacy Merchant Village Aggressor Target Slice

### M6 Sakura Legacy Heal Home Search Slice

- Restored Sakura Haruno's old home-centered healing target search:
  - Old `AIHeal` only searched for a new healing target when Sakura had a home and passed the 1-in-40 chance check.
  - Old target search used an AABB around `getHomePosition()` expanded by `getMaximumHomeDistance()` horizontally and 8 blocks vertically, while the actual valid-target range still used Sakura's follow range.
  - Current `HealNearbyGoal` now maps that to 1.20.1 restriction state: it requires `hasRestriction()`, searches around `getRestrictCenter()` with `getRestrictRadius()` and 8 vertical blocks, and keeps follow-range gating through the `FOLLOW_RANGE` attribute.

### M6 Sakura Legacy Heal Repath Slice

- Restored Sakura Haruno's old healing-target look and navigation throttling:
  - Old `AIHeal#updateTask()` looked at the target eye position offset downward by 0.3 blocks.
  - Old path recalculation only happened after the short delay when the target had moved at least 1 block from the last path point, or with a 5% random refresh chance.
  - Old failed path attempts added 15 ticks to the next navigation delay.
  - Current `HealNearbyGoal` now tracks the last target position, uses the same 4-10 tick delay window, same 1-block movement threshold, same 5% random refresh, same close-range 40% movement speed, and same failed-path extra delay.
- Scope note:
  - The old `healTargetEntity()` active-hand flow is not reconnected in this slice because the current `IryoJutsuItem` use/on-use path is player-specific. Sakura's existing direct NPC healing remains in place while the movement/look behavior is brought closer to old AI.
- Verification after the Sakura legacy heal repath slice:
  - `rg -n "delayCounter|lastVec|getPositionEyes\\(1f\\)\\.subtract\\(0d, 0\\.3d, 0d\\)|getDistanceSq\\(this\\.lastVec|nextFloat\\(\\) < 0\\.05F|tryMoveToEntityLiving|delayCounter \\+= 15|healTargetEntity\\(\\)" 1.12.2\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\EntitySakuraHaruno.java`: reports the old look point, path refresh gate, failure delay, and active-hand heal call.
  - `rg -n "navigationDelay|lastTargetPosition|getEyePosition\\(\\)\\.subtract\\(0\\.0D, 0\\.3D, 0\\.0D\\)|position\\(\\)\\.distanceToSqr\\(this\\.lastTargetPosition\\)|nextFloat\\(\\) < 0\\.05F|getNavigation\\(\\)\\.moveTo|navigationDelay \\+= 15|target\\.heal\\(HEAL_AMOUNT\\)" 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\SakuraHarunoEntity.java`: reports the restored navigation throttling and the still-direct NPC heal path.
  - `.\gradlew.bat '-Dnet.minecraftforge.gradle.check.certs=false' --no-daemon compileJava`: passes.
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0.

### M6 Sakura Legacy Healing Item Hand Sync Slice

- Restored Sakura Haruno's old healing-item hand visibility:
  - Old Sakura stored the configured Iryo Jutsu stack in the shared ninja inventory slot and swapped it into the main hand only while a heal target was active.
  - Old `setHealTarget(...)` reset active hand use and swapped the main hand with storage when entering or leaving healing.
  - Current Sakura now keeps the main hand empty while idle, creates the configured HEALING-enabled Iryo Jutsu stack when `healingTarget` becomes non-null, and clears it when `healingTarget` is cleared.
- Scope note:

### Resource Legacy Item Texture Atlas And Player Variable Crash Guard Slice

- Fixed the 1.20.1 item/block texture atlas load path:
  - Client logs showed the real issue was not missing files: models referenced legacy `narutomod:items/...` and `narutomod:blocks/...`, while the 1.20 block atlas only stitched singular `item/` and `block/` paths by default.
  - Added `assets/minecraft/atlases/blocks.json` directory sources for `textures/items` and `textures/blocks`, preserving the existing legacy model paths instead of rewriting hundreds of JSON files.
  - Extended `tools/validate_port_resources.py` so model texture references now fail validation if the PNG exists but is not included in the 1.20 block atlas.
- Cleaned remaining resource load warnings:
  - Simplified `amaterasublock`, `explosive_tag`, and `water_still` blockstates to match their current plain-block registrations, removing stale `north/east/south/west/up`, `facing`, and `level` variants.
- Fixed the crash path seen during client testing:
  - Crash report `crash-2026-06-14_12.39.57-server.txt` was `Missing Narutomod player variables` while ticking `EternalMangekyoHelmetItem`.
  - Registered `NarutomodModVariables.ForgeEvents` explicitly on Forge's event bus.
  - Added a UUID-keyed fallback `PlayerVariables` mirror and made client variable sync write through `NarutomodModVariables.get(...)`, preventing HUD/armor code from crashing or spamming errors when a player instance lacks the attached capability.
- Verification after the legacy texture atlas and crash guard slice:
  - Short actual client load: log scan for `Missing textures`, model/blockstate exceptions, `Missing Narutomod player variables`, `ERROR`, `FATAL`, and unexpected exceptions returns 0 matches; no new crash report generated.
  - `.\gradlew.bat '-Dnet.minecraftforge.gradle.check.certs=false' --no-daemon compileJava`: passes.
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0.
  - `python tools\validate_dedicated_server_safety.py`: 268 non-client Java files scanned, 0 client-reference issues.
  - `.\gradlew.bat '-Dnet.minecraftforge.gradle.check.certs=false' --no-daemon build`: passes.

- Restored the old Special Jutsu 2 fixed dynamic-key semantics:
  - Added `NarutomodModVariables.CTRL_PRESSED = "CTRL_pressed"`.
  - `SpecialJutsuKeyMessage` now updates `JutsuKey2Pressed` at the key-2 network entry before skill routing, so this legacy player state is not dependent on Byakugan/Rinnegan/Biju handler matches.
  - After key-2 routing, `SpecialJutsuKeyMessage` resets `CTRL_pressed` to false through `ProcedureSync.EntityNBTTag.setAndSync(...)`, matching the old `ProcedureSpecialJutsu2OnKeyPressed` tail write.
  - This addresses the concrete `CTRL_pressed` unresolved-row meaning from the §6.5 persistent-data audit. The old audit CSV still contains the source-side dynamic local variable row by design; do not treat that CSV row alone as proof the 1.20 runtime is still missing the behavior.

  - Old Haku rendered through `EntityNinjaMob.RenderBase`, whose pose setup switched non-empty main/off hands into `ModelBiped.ArmPose.ITEM` before rendering the slim biped model.

### M5 Replacement Clone Legacy Player Decoy Renderer Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the visual slice:
  - Latest client log scan for missing textures/models/blockstates, `Missing Narutomod player variables`, `ERROR`, `FATAL`, exceptions, and crashes returned 0 matches.
  - Latest crash reports remained the two older `Missing Narutomod player variables` reports from `2026-06-14 12:03:35` and `2026-06-14 12:39:57`, which were already addressed by the player-variable crash guard slice.
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0 before code changes.
- Restored Replacement clone's old player-decoy visual path:
  - Old `EntityClone._Base(EntityLivingBase summonerIn)` copied the summoner id, custom name, handedness, all equipment slots, yaw/head rotations, and location.
  - Old `RenderClone` rendered that clone with the player's skin when the summoner was an `AbstractClientPlayer`, selected the slim model for slim skins, added armor/held-item/custom-head/elytra/inventory-item layers, and applied the old `0.9375F` player-owner scale.
  - `ReplacementCloneEntity` is now a short-lived `PathfinderMob` instead of a plain `Entity`, with synced owner id, owner UUID fallback, copied owner equipment, copied name/handedness/rotation, no drops, no AI, no gravity, and the existing 40-tick poof/falling-log expiry behavior.
  - `ReplacementCloneRenderer` now uses `ClonePlayerModel`, owner skin/slim-model selection, `CloneArmPoses`, `CloneRenderScales`, `HumanoidArmorLayer`, `ItemInHandLayer`, `CustomHeadLayer`, `ElytraLayer`, and `OwnerInventoryItemLayer` instead of rendering an oak log during the decoy lifetime.
  - The oak log visual is now limited to the already-restored expiry burst/falling-block path, matching the old flow more closely: player clone first, wood log on replacement poof.
  - `NarutomodMod` now registers `ReplacementCloneEntity.createAttributes()` so the living replacement clone has a valid 1.20 entity attribute table at startup.
- Scope note:
  - This is code-level parity for the old decoy renderer path using the existing 1.20 clone-render layer stack. Live integrated-client screenshot comparison is still needed for exact pose/scale validation under attacks.

  - Current 1.20.1 sand bullet, sand bind, and sand shield already drive their visible feedback through server-authoritative colored suspended particles / sand trails, but their entity renderer registrations still reused `ChidoriRenderer`, a Chidori-specific renderer that also owns hand-position sync and weapon-spark logic.

### M5 Final Chidori Renderer Cleanup Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the visual slice:
  - Latest runtime log scan only matched Forge language-provider `mods.toml` warnings; no Narutomod missing texture/model/blockstate, player variable, `ERROR`, `FATAL`, exception, or crash entries were found.
  - Latest crash reports remained the two older `Missing Narutomod player variables` reports from `2026-06-14 12:03:35` and `2026-06-14 12:39:57`, which were already addressed by the player-variable crash guard slice.
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0 before code changes.
- Removed the last non-Chidori `ChidoriRenderer` registrations:
  - Old `ItemFoldingFan` registered `entitybulletfolding_fan` with a snowball-style projectile renderer, not the Chidori hand/spark renderer. Current `FoldingFanProjectileEntity` is a `ThrowableItemProjectile` and already sets its item stack to `ModItems.FOLDING_FAN`, so it now uses the standard `ThrownItemRenderer::new` path used by the other thrown projectiles.
  - Old `ItemKaton.EntityFireStream` registered `katonfirestream` without a client renderer; visible feedback came from flame particles and flamethrower sounds. Current `KatonFireStreamEntity` preserves the flame particle, fire, damage, and block ignition paths, so `KATONFIRESTREAM` now uses `NoopEntityRenderer::new`.
  - Old `EntityLavaChakraMode` had a custom lava-cloak renderer over the player model, not Chidori sparks. Current 1.20.1 already provides that visible cloak through `LavaChakraModeLayer` with `textures/lavacloak1.png` and the synced `LavaChakraModeActive` player variable, while `LavaChakraModeEntity` handles lava particles, buffs, sound, chakra drain, and nearby burn; the entity registration now uses `NoopEntityRenderer::new`.
  - Old `ItemRaiton.EntityChakraMode` registered `raitonchakramode` without a client renderer; visible feedback came from LightningArc particles/entities and smoke particles. Current `RaitonChakraModeEntity` preserves LightningArc, smoke, sound, buff, chakra-drain, and cooldown paths, so `RAITONCHAKRAMODE` now uses `NoopEntityRenderer::new`.
  - `ChidoriRenderer::new` now remains only on `CHIDORI` and `CHIDORI_SPEAR`, matching the renderer's client hand-position sync and Chidori weapon-spark behavior.
- Verification after the final Chidori renderer cleanup slice:

### M5 Snake Summon Legacy Model Renderer Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the visual slice:

  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0 before code changes.
- Replaced the box-textured snake summon renderers with the old legacy snake model path:
  - Old `EntitySnake.RenderSnake` rendered `ModelSnake` with old summon scale translation and 21 manually positioned body segments.
  - Old `ItemSummoningContract.EntityGenericSnake` reused `EntitySnake.RenderSnake`, selecting `textures/snake_white.png` for small snakes and `textures/snake_blue.png` for scale above 4.
  - Old `EntityManda` reused `EntitySnake.RenderSnake` with `textures/snake_purple.png` and fixed scale 18.
  - Current 1.20.1 `MANDA` and `SNAKE_SUMMON` still used the generic `SummonAnimalRenderer`, which mapped the snake textures onto cuboids instead of rendering the old snake head/body geometry.
  - `ModelSnake` is skipped by the generated layer pipeline because its `segment[i]` tail taper uses a dynamic `CubeDeformation` expression, so this slice adds a hand-written `SnakeModel` layer with the old 32x32 head geometry and 21 tapered segment parts.
  - Added a light 1.20 approximation of the old movement-offset chain: head/neck look response, attack-target jaw opening, scale-adjusted body wave, old summon scale translation, hidden pre-spawn render state, and scaled shadow.
  - Added `SnakeSummonRenderer`, switched `MANDA` to `textures/snake_purple.png`, switched `SNAKE_SUMMON` to the old white/blue scale texture rule, and registered `SnakeModel.LAYER_LOCATION`.

### M5 Buddha 1000 Pose Visibility Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the visual slice:
  - Latest runtime log scan only matched the existing Mixin output cleanup and Netty/JDK reflective-access debug warnings; no Narutomod missing texture/model/blockstate, player variable, `ERROR`, `FATAL`, exception, or crash entries were found.

  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0 before code changes.
- Restored old Buddha 1000 model visibility and sitting pose behavior:
  - Old `EntityBuddha1000.RenderCustom` set all model parts visible, showed `armStand` only while `entity.isSitting()`, and hid `bipedHead` plus `bipedHeadwear` when the controlling passenger was the local first-person camera.
  - Old `ModelBudha1000.setRotationAngles` applied a sitting pose to the main arms/forearms and legs/calves when the entity was sitting, then returned those limbs to the standing base pose otherwise.
  - Current 1.20.1 `Buddha1000Model` already used the generated legacy geometry, but `setupAnim` was empty, so `armStand` stayed visible in all states and the sitting limb pose / first-person head hide were not restored.
  - Added explicit model fields for the generated `head`, `hat`, main limbs, calves, forearms, and `armStand`; `setupAnim` now resets pose/visibility, applies `armStand.visible = entity.isSitting()`, hides head/hat in first-person rider view, and restores the old sitting limb pose.
  - Updated `Buddha1000DebugRenderer` to pass first-person rider camera state into the model before rendering, keeping the existing world transform unchanged to avoid shifting the already-visible large model without a runtime screenshot check.

### M5 Suiton Water Shark Legacy Animation/Ride Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the visual/behavior slice:

- Restored the old Water Shark render animation inputs and ride window:
  - Old `EntitySuitonShark.EC` synced previous/current limb swing amount, accumulated limb swing, and mouth-open amount, then `RenderCustom` fed those values into `ModelShark` for tail and jaw animation.
  - Current 1.20.1 `WaterSharkRenderer` already had the legacy model geometry, but it guessed swim and mouth animation from client-side velocity, so mouth opening did not follow the old 20-tick launch ramp and limb swing was not authoritative.
  - Added synced `PREV_LIMB_SWING_AMOUNT`, `LIMB_SWING_AMOUNT`, `LIMB_SWING`, and `MOUTH_OPEN_AMOUNT` data to `WaterSharkEntity`, saved them with `FlightTicks`, and updated them server-side with the old `sqrt(delta) * 4`, cap-at-1, `0.4F` smoothing formula.

  - Restored the old large-shark player ride interaction window: players can right-click ride when `FullScale >= 2.0F` and the shark has been in flight for at most 10 ticks; passenger positioning is scaled to the shark size, and non-player forced passengers are rejected.
  - Damage, targeting, water placement, particles, explosion radius, and lifetime logic remain unchanged.

### M6 Purple Dragon Legacy Model Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the visual slice:
  - Latest runtime log scan only matched normal dedicated-server-gate registry/debug lines; no Narutomod missing texture/model/blockstate, player variable, `ERROR`, `FATAL`, exception, or crash entries were found.

- Replaced Purple Dragon's first-pass box renderer with the old dragon-head model path:

  - Current 1.20.1 `PurpleDragonRenderer` still used a hand-written segmented box silhouette, so the old model geometry and movement-following spine were not visible even though the runtime chase/kill behavior was already restored.
  - Added `PurpleDragonEntity.SPINE_SEGMENTS = 20`, a local `partRotations` visual history, and the old movement/yaw/pitch delta segment update formula so client rendering can curve the spine without changing target tracking, hit, owner, save-data, or lifetime behavior.

  - Replaced `PurpleDragonRenderer` with a model renderer using `textures/dragon_purple.png`, the old transform, full-bright translucent no-cull rendering, always-render culling behavior, and wait-progress alpha fade.

### M6 Sealing Chains Repeated Link Renderer Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the visual slice:
  - Latest runtime log scan only matched normal dedicated-server-gate registry/debug lines; no Narutomod missing texture/model/blockstate, player variable, `ERROR`, `FATAL`, exception, or crash entries were found.
  - Latest crash reports remained the two older `Missing Narutomod player variables` server reports from `2026-06-14 12:03:35` and `2026-06-14 12:39:57`, which were already addressed by the player-variable crash guard slice.
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0 before code changes.
- Refined the Sealing Chains body from a smooth tube into old-style repeated chain links:
  - Old `EntitySealingChains.ModelChainLink` rendered `textures/chainlink_gold.png` as repeated link boxes, with count `MathHelper.ceil(len * 6.4f) - 1`, spacing `i * 2.5F` model units, twist `i * PI * 0.4722222D`, and a separate crossed-plane tip at the far end.
  - Current 1.20.1 `SealingChainsRenderer` already had the old gold texture, extension/retraction timing, and a legacy-style tip, but the body was still a continuous textured tube, so it lacked the old chain-link silhouette.

  - Kept `renderChainTip(...)`, target attach resolution, owner interpolation, sound/control/chakra behavior, and server entity logic unchanged.

  - Left animated textures, GUI/hand-sign images, large entity textures, and other non-model art untouched.

### M6 Asakujaku SmallFireball Mechanics Parity Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the mechanics slice:
  - Latest runtime log after the previous server gate only matched normal Forge language-provider `mods.toml` warnings, Netty reflective-access debug noise, Narutomod initialization, and server startup lines; no Narutomod missing texture/model/blockstate, player variable, `ERROR`, `FATAL`, exception, or crash entries were found.

- Restored Asakujaku fireball movement closer to the old `EntitySmallFireball` release mechanism:
  - Old `ItemEightGates.attackAsakujaku` spawned 10 `EntitySmallFireball` instances from the attacker's eye position using the same look vector, kept them alive for 12 ticks, dealt `modifiedAttackDamage * 0.5F`, set hit entities on fire for 10 seconds, created strength-2 explosions, and used `getMotionFactor() = 1.1F`.
  - Current 1.20.1 `AsakujakuFireballEntity` had the correct burst count, lifetime, damage multiplier, fire duration, and explosion strength, but it added artificial ring/random spread and constant velocity, so the release pattern and projectile acceleration did not match the old small-fireball behavior.

  - Added a saved acceleration vector using old small-fireball scale `0.1D`, and applied `motion = (motion + acceleration) * 1.1F` each server tick before collision/movement, preserving the old overridden motion factor rather than using a fixed speed.
  - Kept server-side owner tracking, damage, fire, explosion, lifetime, particle rendering, and Eight Gates gate-6 left-click routing unchanged outside this mechanics correction.

### M6 Hirudora / Night Guy Damage Hurt-Resistance Parity Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the mechanics slice:
  - Latest runtime logs were `1.20.1/run/logs/debug.log` and `latest.log` from the previous dedicated-server gate at `2026-06-14 16:23:33`; the only matched warnings/errors were normal Forge language-provider `mods.toml` warnings and Netty reflective-access debug noise, with Narutomod initialization succeeding and no Narutomod missing texture/model/blockstate, player variable, `ERROR`, `FATAL`, exception, or crash entries.
  - Latest crash reports remained the two older `Missing Narutomod player variables` server reports from `2026-06-14 12:03:35` and `2026-06-14 12:39:57`, already covered by the player-variable crash guard slice.
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0 before code changes.
- Restored old hurt-resistance behavior for Hirudora and Night Guy damage:
  - Old `ItemEightGates.EntityHirudora` used `ProcedureAoeCommand.set(...).exclude(shootingEntity).damageEntities(...)` without chaining `resetHurtResistanceTime()`.
  - Old `ItemEightGates.EntityNGDragon` dealt the direct `f * 64F` hit and then `ProcedureAoeCommand.set(...).exclude(shootingEntity).damageEntities(ds, f * 16F)`, also without resetting `hurtResistantTime`.
  - Current 1.20.1 code was explicitly setting each target's `invulnerableTime = 0` before Hirudora AOE, Night Guy direct hit, and Night Guy AOE, which made hits bypass the vanilla hurt-cooldown layer that the old code left intact. In particular, Night Guy direct targets could take the direct damage and the same-tick AOE damage more reliably than in 1.12.2.
  - Removed those target-side `invulnerableTime` resets while keeping the old caster-side `InvulnerableTime = 40` behavior, the existing custom bypass-armor damage types, damage multipliers, explosions, particles, scale logic, owner dragging, and persistence unchanged.
  - Confirmed `hirudora_damage` and `night_guy_damage` remain listed in `data/minecraft/tags/damage_type/bypasses_armor.json`, preserving the old `setDamageBypassesArmor()` semantics separately from hurt resistance.
- Verification after the Hirudora / Night Guy damage hurt-resistance parity slice:
  - `rg -n "invulnerableTime|INVULNERABLE_TIME|resetHurtResistanceTime|hurtResistantTime" 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\HirudoraEntity.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\NightGuyDragonEntity.java`: reports only the intended caster-side `NarutomodModVariables.INVULNERABLE_TIME` writes and no target-side hurt-cooldown reset.
  - `rg -n "resetHurtResistanceTime|hurtResistantTime|setDamageBypassesArmor|EntityHirudora|EntityNGDragon|damageEntities\(" 1.12.2\src\main\java\net\narutomod\item\ItemEightGates.java 1.12.2\src\main\java\net\narutomod\procedure\ProcedureAoeCommand.java`: confirms the old Eight Gates damage calls did not use `resetHurtResistanceTime()` while `ProcedureAoeCommand` only resets hurt resistance when that method is explicitly chained.
  - `.\gradlew.bat '-Dnet.minecraftforge.gradle.check.certs=false' --no-daemon compileJava`: passes.

### M6 Hirudora / Night Guy Legacy AOE Target Filter Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the mechanics slice:
  - Latest runtime logs were `1.20.1/run/logs/debug.log` and `latest.log` from the previous dedicated-server gate at `2026-06-14 16:27:35`; the only matched warnings/errors were normal Forge language-provider `mods.toml` warnings and Netty reflective-access debug noise, with Narutomod initialization succeeding and no Narutomod missing texture/model/blockstate, player variable, `ERROR`, `FATAL`, exception, or crash entries.
  - Latest crash reports remained the two older `Missing Narutomod player variables` server reports from `2026-06-14 12:03:35` and `2026-06-14 12:39:57`, already covered by the player-variable crash guard slice.
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0 before code changes.
- Restored old `ProcedureAoeCommand.set(entity, 0, range)` target filtering for Hirudora and Night Guy AOE damage:
  - Old `ProcedureAoeCommand.set(Entity entity, double minR, double maxR)` used the projectile's current `getEntityBoundingBox().grow(maxR)` and `World#getEntitiesWithinAABB(Entity.class, ..., MIN_DISTANCE)`, where `MIN_DISTANCE` only enforced alive, not spectating, not `kamui_intangible`, and min-distance constraints.
  - Hirudora old AOE used `ProcedureAoeCommand.set(this, 0.0D, 0.5D * getEntityScale()).exclude(shootingEntity).damageEntities(...)`.
  - Night Guy old AOE used `ProcedureAoeCommand.set(this, 0.0D, 5.0D).exclude(shootingEntity).damageEntities(ds, f * 16F)`.
  - Current 1.20.1 AOE loops used a sphere around the hit point, only iterated `LivingEntity`, required `distanceToSqr(point) <= radius^2`, and reused the collision predicate that also excluded same-root-vehicle targets and same projectile class. That made the AOE narrower and not equivalent to the old command helper.
  - Updated both AOE damage loops to collect `Entity` targets from `getBoundingBox().inflate(radius)`, filter alive/non-spectator/not owner/not `kamui_intangible`, and call `hurt(...)` directly, matching the old command helper's damage-list semantics while leaving ray collision, direct Night Guy hit, explosions, damage values, hurt-resistance behavior, and visuals unchanged.
- Verification after the Hirudora / Night Guy legacy AOE target filter slice:
  - `rg -n "canLegacyAoeDamage|getBoundingBox\(\)\.inflate|distanceToSqr\(point\)|getEntitiesOfClass\(LivingEntity|new AABB\(point" 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\HirudoraEntity.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\NightGuyDragonEntity.java`: reports the restored legacy AOE target helpers and no remaining hit-point sphere damage loops for these two entities.
  - `Get-Content 1.12.2\src\main\java\net\narutomod\procedure\ProcedureAoeCommand.java`: confirms old AOE target collection used the projectile/entity AABB grown by `maxR`, alive/non-spectator/not-`kamui_intangible` filtering, and `exclude(shootingEntity)` before damage.
  - `.\gradlew.bat '-Dnet.minecraftforge.gradle.check.certs=false' --no-daemon compileJava`: passes.
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0.

  - Updated `NightGuyDragonRenderer` to bake and render `NightGuyDragonModel`, preserving the old texture, scale translation, yaw/pitch transform, entity scale, additive full-bright path, and shadow size behavior.

- Restored Hirudora's old white-tiger render-pass split:
  - Old `ItemEightGates.ModelWhiteTiger` rendered the tiger head, jaw, ears, and muzzle with ordinary alpha blending, then called `OpenGlHelper.setLightmapTextureCoords(..., 240.0F, 240.0F)` immediately before rendering the separate `eyes` part.
  - Current 1.20.1 `HirudoraRenderer` sent the entire `HirudoraTigerModel` through one full-bright emissive no-cull pass, making the whole tiger glow instead of only the eye plane.

  - Updated `HirudoraRenderer` to render the head with `RenderType.entityTranslucent(TEXTURE)` and the incoming packed light, then render only the eyes with `NarutoRenderTypes.translucentEmissiveNoCull(TEXTURE)` and `LightTexture.FULL_BRIGHT`, preserving the old texture, transform, scale, yaw/pitch alignment, and shadow behavior.
  - Server mechanics, target aiming, damage, AOE, explosion, smoke particles, persistence, and the white-tiger geometry were left unchanged.

- Restored Night Guy Dragon's old full-additive blending:
  - Old `ItemEightGates.ModelNightguyDragon#render` used `GlStateManager.blendFunc(GlStateManager.SourceFactor.ONE, GlStateManager.DestFactor.ONE)` with full-bright lightmap coordinates before rendering the model.
  - After the legacy model restoration, current `NightGuyDragonRenderer` still used the shared `NarutoRenderTypes.energyAdditive(TEXTURE)` path, whose transparency state is `SRC_ALPHA, ONE`. That is useful for several modernized effects, but it is not the old Night Guy Dragon render state.
  - Added `NarutoRenderTypes.energyFullAdditive(...)`, backed by a dedicated `ONE, ONE` additive transparency state and the existing emissive entity shader/no-cull setup.
  - Updated only `NightGuyDragonRenderer` to use `energyFullAdditive(TEXTURE)`, leaving other users of `energyAdditive(...)` unchanged.
  - Geometry, animation, texture, scale transform, damage, AOE, owner dragging, explosion, particles, persistence, and server behavior were left unchanged.
- Verification after the Night Guy Dragon legacy full-additive blend slice:
  - `rg -n "ONE_ONE_ADDITIVE_TRANSPARENCY|energyFullAdditive|naruto_energy_full_additive|SourceFactor\.ONE, GlStateManager\.DestFactor\.ONE|energyAdditive\(TEXTURE\)|energyFullAdditive\(TEXTURE\)" 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\client\render\NarutoRenderTypes.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\client\renderer\NightGuyDragonRenderer.java`: reports the new `ONE, ONE` render type and Night Guy Dragon's use of it.
  - `rg -n "blendFunc\(GlStateManager\.SourceFactor\.ONE, GlStateManager\.DestFactor\.ONE\)|ModelNightguyDragon|OpenGlHelper\.setLightmapTextureCoords" 1.12.2\src\main\java\net\narutomod\item\ItemEightGates.java`: confirms the old model render used `ONE, ONE` additive blending and full-bright lightmap coordinates.

### M6 Katon/Magma Legacy Explosion Flags Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the mechanics/explosion slice:
  - Latest runtime log was `1.20.1/run/logs/latest.log` from the previous dedicated-server gate at `2026-06-14 17:45:46`; it contained normal Forge startup lines, Narutomod initialization, world preparation, and server ready output. No Narutomod missing texture/model/blockstate, player variable, `ERROR`, `FATAL`, exception, or crash entries were found.
  - Latest crash reports remained the two older `Missing Narutomod player variables` server reports from `2026-06-14 12:03:35` and `2026-06-14 12:39:57`, already covered by the player-variable crash guard slice.
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0 before code changes.
- Restored old fire/explosion terrain flags for two fire projectiles:
  - Old `ItemKaton.EntityBigFireball#onImpact` computed Forge mob-griefing into `flag` and called `world.newExplosion(shootingEntity, ..., explosionSize, flag, false)`, meaning the explosion could create fire when mob griefing allowed it, but it did not damage terrain.
  - Current 1.20.1 `KatonFireballEntity#impact` used `serverLevel.explode(owner, ..., explosionSize, Level.ExplosionInteraction.MOB)`, which did not pass the old fire flag and could damage terrain under mob griefing.
  - Updated Katon fireball to call `ForgeEventFactory.getMobGriefingEvent(serverLevel, owner)` and pass that as the fire flag while using `Level.ExplosionInteraction.NONE`, matching old `flag, false`.
  - Old `ItemYooton.EntityMagmaBall#onImpact` called `world.newExplosion(shootingEntity, ..., explosionSize, flag, flag)`, so fire creation and terrain damage were both controlled by mob griefing.
  - Updated `MagmaBallEntity` to pass the same Forge mob-griefing fire flag while keeping `Level.ExplosionInteraction.MOB` for terrain damage, matching old `flag, flag`.
  - Damage source mapping was deliberately left unchanged in this slice because old `causeJutsuDamage(...).setFireDamage()` needs a separate fire-ninjutsu damage type/tag decision; projectile spawn, growth, same-type collision filtering, damage amount, target fire duration, particles, sounds, persistence, and renderers were left unchanged.
- Verification after the Katon/Magma legacy explosion-flags slice:
  - `rg -n "newExplosion\(this\.shootingEntity, this\.posX, this\.posY, this\.posZ, this\.explosionSize, flag, false\)|newExplosion\(this\.shootingEntity, this\.posX, this\.posY, this\.posZ, this\.explosionSize, flag, flag\)|getMobGriefingEvent\(serverLevel, owner\)|ExplosionInteraction\.(NONE|MOB)|explode\(owner, point\.x\(\), point\.y\(\), point\.z\(\), explosionSize, mobGriefing" 1.12.2\src\main\java\net\narutomod\item\ItemKaton.java 1.12.2\src\main\java\net\narutomod\item\ItemYooton.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\KatonFireballEntity.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\MagmaBallEntity.java`: reports the old `flag,false`/`flag,flag` explosion calls and the current restored Forge mob-griefing fire flag plus `NONE`/`MOB` interactions.
  - `.\gradlew.bat "-Dnet.minecraftforge.gradle.check.certs=false" --no-daemon compileJava`: passes.

- Restored non-armor-piercing fire-ninjutsu damage semantics for direct fire projectiles:
  - Old `ItemKaton.EntityBigFireball#onImpact` damaged direct-hit targets with `ItemJutsu.causeJutsuDamage(...).setFireDamage()`: same `ninjutsu_damage` type, fire flag, and no armor-bypass flag.

- Restored Amaterasu block's old fire-like collision semantics:
  - Old `BlockAmaterasuBlock.BlockCustom` extended `BlockFire`, was unbreakable, emitted full light, and used the old fire block shape/collision behavior rather than acting as a solid generic block.
  - Old `onEntityWalk` and `onEntityCollidedWithBlock` both called `ProcedureAmaterasuEntityCollidesInTheBlock`.
  - Old `ProcedureAmaterasuEntityCollidesInTheBlock` applied `PotionAmaterasuFlame` for 10000 ticks at amplifier 0 to living entities and called `entity.setFire(10000)` for any colliding entity.
  - Current 1.20.1 `AMATERASUBLOCK` was registered through the generic block helper, so it had no collision hook and was solid despite already using fire-shaped blockstate/model resources.
  - Added a dedicated `registerAmaterasuBlock` path with `MapColor.FIRE`, full light, no loot table, no occlusion, no collision, and old-style unbreakable strength.
  - Added an `AmaterasuBlock#entityInside` hook that applies `ModEffects.AMATERASUFLAME` for 10000 ticks at amplifier 0 to `LivingEntity` instances and calls `setSecondsOnFire(10000)` for every entity entering the block.
  - Blockstate/model/item resources, Amaterasu effect tick damage, Kagutsuchi projectile AOE semantics, and unrelated block registrations were left unchanged.

- Restored old Kagutsuchi fireball same-type collision filtering:
  - Old `EntityBlackFireball#onImpact` ignored its shooter and `result.entityHit instanceof EntityBlackFireball`; it did not ignore `EntityBigBlackFireball`.

### M6 Kamui Shuriken Legacy Scale Hitbox Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the Kamui Shuriken scale-hitbox slice:
  - Latest runtime log was `1.20.1/run/logs/latest.log` from the previous dedicated-server gate at `2026-06-14 18:19:44`; it contained normal Forge startup lines, Narutomod initialization, world preparation, and server ready output. No Narutomod missing texture/model/blockstate, player variable, `ERROR`, `FATAL`, exception, or crash entries were found.
  - Latest crash reports remained the two older `Missing Narutomod player variables` server reports from `2026-06-14 12:03:35` and `2026-06-14 12:39:57`, already covered by the player-variable crash guard slice.
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0 before code changes.
- Restored Kamui Shuriken scale-driven projectile dimensions:
  - Old `EntityKamuiShuriken` used `ogWidth = 0.4F`, initialized with `setSize(ogWidth, ogWidth)`, and `setScale(float)` immediately called `setSize(ogWidth * scale, ogWidth * scale)`.
  - Old `onUpdate` also re-applied the scaled size when `width < getScale() * ogWidth`, keeping large Winged Susanoo shuriken collision dimensions aligned with the synced scale.
  - Current 1.20.1 `KamuiShurikenEntity` synced/saved `Scale` and the renderer consumed it, but the entity dimensions remained the fixed registered `0.4F x 0.4F` size.
  - Added `BASE_SIZE = 0.4F`, dynamic `getDimensions(Pose)` returning `BASE_SIZE * getScale()`, and `refreshDimensions()` when scale changes or sync data updates.

### M6 Shakuton Scorch Kill Legacy Queue Rotation Slice

No Narutomod missing texture/model/blockstate, player variable, `ERROR`, `FATAL`, exception, or crash entries were found.

  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0 before code changes.
- Restored Scorch Kill's old scorch-orb queue rotation order:
  - Old `ItemShakuton.SetOrbTarget#createJutsu` first called `get1stBallAndPutLast(entity.world, stack)`, rotating the first active scorch orb to the back of the stack queue, and only then performed the 30-block `ProcedureUtils.objectEntityLookingAt(...)` target lookup.
  - Current 1.20.1 `AdvancedNatureJutsuItem#activateScorchKill` performed the target lookup first and returned early when no target was in sight, so an active scorch orb was not rotated in the old no-target case.
  - Reordered the 1.20.1 path to fetch/rotate the first active `ScorchOrbEntity` before the target lookup, matching the old queue semantics while keeping the same no-orb/no-target messages and success path.
  - Chakra cost, advancement gate, target range, target assignment, XP award, active-orb filtering, queue writeback, Scorch Blast queue clearing, and Scorch Orb runtime behavior were left unchanged.

### M6 Scorch Orb Legacy Target Return Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the Scorch Orb target-return slice:
  - Latest runtime log from the previous dedicated-server gate contained only normal Forge startup warnings for language jars missing `mods.toml`; no Narutomod missing texture/model/blockstate, player variable, `ERROR`, `FATAL`, exception, or crash entries were found.
  - Latest crash reports remained the two older `Missing Narutomod player variables` server reports from `2026-06-14 12:03:35` and `2026-06-14 12:39:57`, already covered by the player-variable crash guard slice.
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0 before code changes.
- Restored Scorch Orb's old target-return and target-clear timing:
  - Old `ItemShakuton.EntityScorchBall#onUpdate` set `targetTime = 0` when a tracked target died, then routed through `setNextPosition(getIdlePosition())`.
  - Old `setNextPosition(...)` kept the capped `0.4D` return speed while `targetTime >= 0` and only called `setTarget(null)` after the orb reached its idle orbit position.
  - Current 1.20.1 followed live targets correctly, but a dead target with positive `TargetTime` could keep stale target state indefinitely, and a zero `TargetTime` cleared the target before returning to idle, changing the old return movement.
  - Added `returnToIdlePosition(...)` so expired or missing targets keep the old capped return path until arrival, and dead targets first set `TARGET_TIME` to `0` before returning.
  - Scorch Orb spawning, queue rotation, target assignment range, launch/growth behavior, touch damage, impact AOE, spherical explosion, particles, persistence, and renderer behavior were left unchanged.
- Verification after the Scorch Orb legacy target-return slice:
  - `rg -n "this\.target != null && this\.targetTime > 0|this\.target\.isEntityAlive\(\)|this\.targetTime = 0|setNextPosition\(this\.getIdlePosition\(\)\)|vec\.equals\(this\.getIdlePosition\(\)\)|this\.setTarget\(null\)" 1.12.2\src\main\java\net\narutomod\item\ItemShakuton.java`: reports the old live-target follow, dead-target `targetTime = 0`, idle return, and clear-on-arrival behavior.
  - `rg -n "returnToIdlePosition|targetTime > 0|target\.isAlive\(\)|TARGET_TIME, 0|clearTargetOnArrival|setTarget\(null\)" 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\ScorchOrbEntity.java`: reports the restored dead-target transition and return-to-idle clear timing.
  - `.\gradlew.bat "-Dnet.minecraftforge.gradle.check.certs=false" --no-daemon compileJava`: passes.
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0.
  - `python tools\validate_dedicated_server_safety.py`: 268 non-client Java files scanned, 0 client-reference issues.
  - `.\gradlew.bat "-Dnet.minecraftforge.gradle.check.certs=false" --no-daemon build`: passes.
  - `tools\run_dedicated_server_gate.ps1`: ready `true`, fatal issue count 0, latest ready line `Done (11.534s)`.

  - Updated `PuppetScrollRenderer` to bake a dedicated Karasu or Sanshouo model layer, render at old `2.0F` scale, and keep the old yaw/pitch transform plus texture selection.
  - Registered separate layer definitions for Karasu and Sanshouo scroll projectiles; projectile spawn, stored puppet health, scroll item behavior, poof effects, and puppet creation were left unchanged.

- Restored the old Buddha1000 renderer behavior on the current 1.20.1 legacy model path:
  - Old `EntityBuddha1000.RenderCustom` registered `ModelBudha1000`, texture `textures/budha1000.png`, shadow size `0.5F * MODELSCALE`, `MODELSCALE = 20.0F`, first-person rider head/hat hiding, and rider attack-swing copying.
  - Old `ModelBudha1000.render(...)` raised the summon out of the ground with `(entity.isSitting() ? 1.0F : 1.5F) * MODELSCALE * getGrowth(...)` while keeping the full legacy model scale.
  - Current 1.20.1 `Buddha1000DebugRenderer` already used the old texture/model scale, but did not use the entity's `getRenderGrowth(...)`, so the summon appeared fully raised immediately; the class also still carried the debug renderer name.
  - Renamed the renderer path to `Buddha1000Renderer`, kept the old full-bright `textures/budha1000.png` cutout render, old `10.0F` shadow radius, old first-person rider head/hat hiding, and old 20x model scale.
  - Added the old growth lift to the renderer by translating the model downward by the remaining sitting/standing lift until `getRenderGrowth(partialTick)` reaches 1.0.
  - Extended `Buddha1000Model` with the old biped head/hat copy, standing arm/leg walk pose, idle arm sway, attack swing math, sitting pose, and `armStand` visibility behavior. The renderer feeds attack progress from the controlling passenger and a speed-based limb swing amount for the non-LivingEntity port.
  - Switched only `BUDDHA_1000` to the renamed renderer. Entity spawn, chakra burn, riding, input handling, Buddha arm projectile logic, texture assets, and generated legacy layer definitions were left unchanged.

### M6 Transformation Jutsu Player-Target Renderer Layer Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the Transformation Jutsu renderer slice:
  - Latest runtime log remained `1.20.1/run/logs/latest.log` from the previous dedicated-server gate, last written `2026-06-14 19:27:25`; it contained normal Forge startup output and no Narutomod missing texture/model/blockstate, player variable, `ERROR`, `FATAL`, exception, or crash entries.
  - Latest crash reports remained the two older `Missing Narutomod player variables` server reports from `2026-06-14 12:03:35` and `2026-06-14 12:39:57`, already covered by the player-variable crash guard slice.
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0 before code changes.
- Restored the missing player-model renderer pieces for the current 1.20.1 Transformation Jutsu visual path:
  - Old player-target Transformation Jutsu changed the caster's player texture through `PlayerRender.setSkinCloneTarget(..., false)`, preserving the target player skin/model family while the transformation control entity handled lifecycle/input.
  - Current 1.20.1 `TransformationJutsuRenderer` already used the target player's skin texture when available, but it always rendered the wide Steve player model and had no armor, held-item, custom-head, or elytra layers, so player-target transformations could show the wrong body type and dropped copied visible equipment.
  - Updated `TransformationJutsuRenderer` to use the shared clone player model path, keep both wide and slim baked player models, select the slim model from the disguise target's client skin metadata, apply copied held-item arm poses, apply the same player scale correction used by clone renderers, and attach humanoid armor, held-item, custom-head, and elytra layers.
  - Entity spawning, owner/target sync, copied equipment data, chakra upkeep, health transfer, poof effects, and attack interception were left unchanged.
  - The deeper old non-player target behavior remains pending: the 1.12 path spawned a real target-class clone, forwarded input, and moved the camera to that clone; this slice only fixes the current player-model renderer layer gap.
- Verification after the Transformation Jutsu renderer layer slice:
  - `rg -n "setSkinCloneTarget\(\(EntityPlayer\)this\.user, \(EntityPlayer\)this\.target, false\)|TransformationJutsuRenderer|ClonePlayerModel|HumanoidArmorLayer|PLAYER_SLIM|TRANSFORMATION_JUTSU" 1.12.2\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\EntityTransformationJutsu.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\client\renderer\TransformationJutsuRenderer.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\client\ClientModEvents.java`: reports the old player skin-clone path and the current renderer registration/model/layer updates.
  - `.\gradlew.bat "-Dnet.minecraftforge.gradle.check.certs=false" --no-daemon compileJava`: passes.
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0.
  - `python tools\validate_dedicated_server_safety.py`: 268 non-client Java files scanned, 0 client-reference issues.

### M6 Transformation Jutsu Non-Player Disguise Renderer Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the non-player Transformation Jutsu renderer slice:
  - Latest runtime log remained `1.20.1/run/logs/latest.log`, last written `2026-06-14 19:27:25`; it was searched for Narutomod missing texture/model/blockstate, player variable, `ERROR`, `FATAL`, exception, crash, registry, and load failure patterns, with no matches.
  - Latest crash reports remained the two older `Missing Narutomod player variables` server reports from `2026-06-14 12:03:35` and `2026-06-14 12:39:57`, already covered by the player-variable crash guard slice.
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0 before code changes.
- Restored a closer client visual path for non-player Transformation Jutsu disguises:
  - Old non-player Transformation Jutsu created a real target-class clone through `EntityList.newEntity(this.target.getClass(), this.world)`, copied the caster's health/location/angles into it, spawned it, forwarded player input to it, and spectated it while the jutsu was active.
  - Current 1.20.1 `TransformationJutsuEntity` keeps target/owner state and copied equipment, but the renderer still used the player-model fallback for non-player targets, so animal/mob disguises could render with the wrong model and texture.
  - Updated `TransformationJutsuRenderer` so non-player targets delegate to the target entity's registered renderer instead of the player renderer. During the delegated render it temporarily aligns target yaw, pitch, head yaw, and body yaw to the transformation entity, renders at the transformation entity's origin, and restores all target rotations afterward.
  - The player-target path from the previous slice remains unchanged, including slim/wide model selection and player equipment layers.
  - The full old non-player clone/input-forward/camera mechanism remains pending; this slice is scoped to recovering the correct non-player client model and texture when the disguise target is client-visible.
- Verification after the non-player Transformation Jutsu renderer slice:
  - `rg -n "EntityList\.newEntity|this\.clone|setSkinCloneTarget|setRenderViewEntity|movementInput|TransformationJutsuRenderer|renderNonPlayerTarget|EntityRenderDispatcher|dispatcher\.render|instanceof Player" 1.12.2\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\EntityTransformationJutsu.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\client\renderer\TransformationJutsuRenderer.java`: reports the old clone/input/spectate evidence and the new renderer delegation path.

  - `python tools\validate_dedicated_server_safety.py`: 268 non-client Java files scanned, 0 client-reference issues.

### M6 Lava Chakra Mode Legacy Cloak Renderer Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the Lava Chakra Mode cloak renderer slice:
  - Latest runtime log was searched for Narutomod missing texture/model/blockstate, player variable, `ERROR`, `FATAL`, exception, crash, registry, and load failure patterns; no matches were found.
  - Latest crash reports remained the two older `Missing Narutomod player variables` server reports from `2026-06-14 12:03:35` and `2026-06-14 12:39:57`, already covered by the player-variable crash guard slice.
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0 before code changes.
- Restored the old entity-rendered Lava Chakra Mode cloak path for non-local visibility:
  - Old `EntityLavaChakraMode.RenderCustom` rendered the user's current living model again with `textures/lavacloak1.png`, alpha `0.6F`, full-bright lighting, and a scrolling texture matrix offset of `(ticks + partialTicks) * 0.01F` on both axes, while skipping first-person owner rendering.
  - Current 1.20.1 had a `LavaChakraModeLayer` attached to player renderers, but the `LAVA_CHAKRA_MODE` entity itself was still registered with `NoopEntityRenderer`. Since the active player-variable tag is written/synced through the owner player path, other clients could miss the old entity-rendered cloak even though the entity already syncs its owner id.
  - Added `LavaChakraModeRenderer`, which resolves the synced owner, skips first-person owner rendering, avoids duplicate rendering when the existing player layer already handles that player, and otherwise re-renders the owner's current living model with `textures/lavacloak1.png`, full-bright translucent scrolling render type, alpha `0.6F`, owner interpolation, body/head/pitch animation, walk animation, baby/riding flags, and the 1.20 living-model pose transform.
  - Changed the client entity renderer registration for `LAVA_CHAKRA_MODE` from `NoopEntityRenderer` to `LavaChakraModeRenderer`.
  - Existing chakra upkeep, lava particles, ambient lava sound, nearby burn damage, owner active tag sync, and the existing local player `LavaChakraModeLayer` were left unchanged.
- Verification after the Lava Chakra Mode cloak renderer slice:
  - `rg -n "RenderCustom|lavacloak1\.png|LavaChakraModeLayer|LavaChakraModeRenderer|LAVA_CHAKRA_MODE|NoopEntityRenderer::new|isLavaChakraModeActive|scrollingTranslucent\(TEXTURE, 0\.01F, 0\.01F\)|getOwner\(\)" 1.12.2\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\EntityLavaChakraMode.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\client\renderer\LavaChakraModeLayer.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\client\renderer\LavaChakraModeRenderer.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\client\ClientModEvents.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\LavaChakraModeEntity.java`: reports the old cloak renderer/texture evidence, the retained player layer, the new entity renderer, owner lookup, and renderer registration.

### M6 Sakura Legacy Healing Held-Item Renderer Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the Sakura renderer-layer slice:
  - Latest runtime log was searched for Narutomod missing texture/model/blockstate, player variable, `ERROR`, `FATAL`, exception, crash, registry, and load failure patterns; no Narutomod issue was found.
  - Latest crash reports remained the two older `Missing Narutomod player variables` server reports from `2026-06-14 12:03:35` and `2026-06-14 12:39:57`, already covered by the player-variable crash guard slice.
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0 before code changes.
- Restored Sakura's old visible healing-jutsu main-hand path:
  - Old `EntitySakuraHaruno` created an enabled `ItemIryoJutsu` healing stack in inventory slot 0, swapped it into `EntityEquipmentSlot.MAINHAND` while a heal target was active, set the main hand active at close range, and rendered through `EntityNinjaMob.RenderBase` with the old held-item pose setup.
  - Old Sakura used the slim 64x64 biped model, `textures/sakura_slim.png`, and old scale `0.0625F * 14`.
  - Current 1.20.1 `SakuraHarunoEntity` already preserves the healing-target-driven main-hand `ModItems.IRYO_JUTSU` stack with the healing jutsu enabled, and the renderer already preserves the old texture and scale, but the renderer lacked `ItemInHandLayer` and `SakuraHarunoModel` did not implement `ArmedModel`, so the synced healing item could not render in hand.

### M6 Kage Bunshin Legacy Activated Held Jutsu Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the Kage Bunshin activated-held-jutsu slice:
  - Latest runtime log was `1.20.1/run/logs/latest.log`, last written `2026-06-14 19:44:32`; it was searched for Narutomod missing texture/model/blockstate, player variable, `ERROR`, `FATAL`, exception, crash, registry, and load failure patterns; no matches were found.
  - Latest crash reports remained the two older `Missing Narutomod player variables` server reports from `2026-06-14 12:03:35` and `2026-06-14 12:39:57`, already covered by the player-variable crash guard slice.
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0 before code changes.
- Restored the old Kage Bunshin one-shot activated held-jutsu hook:
  - Old `EntityKageBunshin.EC#onUpdate()` checked the clone main hand on server tick 1; when the stack was an `ItemJutsu.Base`, it iterated `getActivatedJutsus(stack)` and directly called `je.jutsu.createJutsu(stack, this, je.jutsu.getPower(stack))`.
  - Old `ItemJutsu.Base#getActivatedJutsus(...)` only returned jutsu callbacks whose own `isActivated(stack)` was true, so this was not the normal selected-jutsu use path. For Ninjutsu, the practical active clone case is the stored `RasenganSize` state; replacement and hiding-camouflage active flags are toggled off by their old callbacks.
  - Added a default `JutsuItem#activateCloneHeldJutsus(...)` hook, called once by `KageBunshinEntity` on server tick 1 after owner validation.
  - Implemented `NinjutsuItem#activateCloneHeldJutsus(...)` to mirror the old active-state behavior: clear active replacement, clear active hiding camouflage, and spawn a `RasenganEntity` attached to the clone when the copied stack carries a stored `RasenganSize` at or above the current Rasengan minimum power.
  - The clone-triggered Rasengan path does not consume chakra or award XP, matching the old direct `createJutsu(...)` call rather than the normal player release path. Kage Bunshin spawning, clone ownership/equipment copy, clone XP transfer, combat/follow behavior, and the larger controlled-clone state machine were left unchanged.

- Restored several old Chidori runtime effects that were missing or simplified in 1.20:
  - Added `LightSourceBlock`, a 1.20 equivalent of old `BlockLightSource`: full light, no collision, no loot, empty shape, age-based scheduled ticking, and automatic removal when it is no longer refreshed.
  - Switched `ModBlocks.LIGHT_SOURCE` from a plain placeholder block to the new light-source block implementation.
  - Updated `assets/narutomod/blockstates/light_source.json` with `age=0`, `age=1`, and `age=2` variants so the new block state does not produce missing-model blockstate logs.
  - Updated `ChidoriEntity` to refresh the legacy light source after half growth time, matching old `ProcedureLightSourceSetBlock` usage after `growTime / 2`.

  - Changed Chidori melee damage back onto `LightningArcEntity.onStruck(...)`, restoring the lightning strike side effects, paralysis effect, and old damage-source path instead of direct damage plus generic pushback.
  - Kept the current 1.20 swing throttle around `owner.swinging` because there is no direct `swingProgressInt == 1` equivalent; this prevents one 1.20 swing animation from applying multiple Chidori hits.
  - Deferred the old non-Sharingan temporary FOV bump from `ProcedureRenderView.setFOV(...)` because 1.20 currently has only specialized Byakugan/Kamui FOV channels, not a generic legacy FOV message/state yet.

  - Compared old `EntityRasenshuriken.EC` with the current 1.20 `RasenshurikenEntity`. The old projectile continued to call `shoot(...)` toward the caster's current 50-block look target while it remained within 48 blocks of the caster, so its path could keep being guided after the initial release.
  - Reworked 1.20 flight timing so an already-launched Rasenshuriken first travels with the previous tick's motion, then refreshes its acceleration from the owner's current look target when still within the old 48-block guidance range.

  - Adjusted the wind loop cadence from `tickCount % 80 == 0` to the old `ticksAlive % 80 == 79` timing.

  - Old `EntitySuitonShark.EC` inherited `EntityScalableProjectile.Base#shoot(...)`, so its post-wait tracking path did not directly set a flat velocity. Each tick first moved/collided using the previous motion in the base projectile update, then the shark's own `onUpdate()` called `shoot(...)` toward the existing target or initial look target, storing a normalized `0.1` acceleration and the old water/dry speed as `motionFactor`.
  - The 1.20 `WaterSharkEntity` already restored wait-position attachment, scale growth, target selection, water/dry speed choices, mouth animation, limb swing, ride interaction, ninjutsu absorption, scaled dimensions, impact damage/explosion, and temporary water footprint, but it still directly set velocity before moving in the same tick.
  - Added `acceleration` and `motionFactor` state, saved/restored them in NBT, changed launch tracking to seed old acceleration/factor instead of direct velocity, and reordered the launched tick path so existing launched sharks travel with the previous tick's motion before updating the next tick's `shoot(...)` state.
  - Cleared acceleration/factor on impact, matching the old halted projectile behavior.
- Verification after the Water Shark projectile-acceleration slice:
  - `rg -n "acceleration|motionFactor|MotionFactor|AccelerationX|updateLegacyNoGravityMotion|direction\.normalize\(\)\.scale\(speed\)|setDeltaMovement\(motion\)|updateLaunchMotion|travelAndImpact" 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\WaterSharkEntity.java`: reports the persisted acceleration/factor path, launch/travel ordering, and confirms the direct `direction.normalize().scale(speed)` launch path is gone.

- Restored Tail Beast Ball's old scalable-projectile launch semantics:
  - Old `EntityTailedBeast.EntityTailBeastBall` inherited `EntityScalableProjectile.Base`, used `setOGSize(0.25F, 0.25F)`, grew with `setEntityScale(...)`, froze non-disabled `EntityLiving` shooters during buildup, launched Mob shots toward the current attack target, launched player/disabled-AI shots along the shooter's look vector, called `shoot(..., 1.05F, 0.0F)`, and used water slowdown `0.98F` instead of disappearing in water.
  - The 1.20 `TailBeastBallEntity` already had the old buildup, sounds, damage/explosion, square-ball renderer, and persisted owner/scale state, but it still launched with a direct flat velocity, used a simplified buildup offset, kept fixed entity dimensions on the client sync path, and discarded immediately in water.

  - Added persisted `acceleration`, `MotionFactor`, and acceleration-vector NBT fields, changed launch to seed old no-gravity acceleration (`direction.normalize() * 0.1`) with `motionFactor=1.05`, and changed flight to move with previous tick motion before updating next tick motion as `(motion + acceleration) * factor`.
  - Restored buildup placement to the old head-yaw eye-vector mouth position without the 1.20-only active-radius offset.
  - Restored the old Mob shooter branch by freezing non-disabled Mob AI during buildup, restoring it at launch/removal, and targeting `mob.getTarget()` for AI shots; player and already-disabled Mob shots still use the shooter's look direction.
  - Replaced the 1.20 water discard with the old water slowdown path (`motionFactor * 0.98F`) and bubble particles while submerged.

  - Added persisted `MotionFactor` and acceleration-vector fields to NBT, changed launch to zero the accumulated vertical wait motion and store normalized `0.1` acceleration plus `motionFactor=1.2`, and changed launched travel to move/raycast with the previous tick motion before updating next tick motion as `(motion + acceleration) * factor`.
  - Restored the old waiting order by moving with the existing motion first and only then adding the next `-0.03D` vertical acceleration during the first half of the wait.
  - Added the old default scalable-projectile water slowdown path (`motionFactor * 0.8F`) with bubble particles while submerged.

- Restored a focused part of Chibaku Tensei's old launch and living-entity pull behavior:

The 1.20 entity added and damped the new vertical motion before moving, causing the ball to move too early and too far during buildup.

The current 1.20 path still uses a much smaller one-shot block capture (`CAPTURE_RADIUS=7`, `MAX_CAPTURED_BLOCKS=384`) and should be handled as a dedicated block-capture/satellite parity slice.

  - The 1.20 `IceSpearEntity` previously set no-gravity in the constructor for every spear, assigned direct `direction * speed` velocity, discarded on block impact, used render-only scale, and had no shattered-shard entity path for Ice Dome breaking.
  - Added real scaled dimensions via `getDimensions(Pose)`, synced scale refresh, and `setEntityScale(...)`; jutsu-created spears now explicitly use `JUTSU_SCALE=0.5F`, while shattered shards get the old random `0.05F..0.55F` scale.
  - Added persisted `MotionFactor`, acceleration, in-air/in-ground timers, no-gravity state, and random yaw/pitch fields. Launch now stores normalized `0.1D` acceleration and moves with the previous tick's velocity before calculating the next tick's velocity as `(motion + acceleration) * factor`.
  - Restored old landing behavior: tip collision/block impact sticks the spear into the block and clears launch motion instead of immediately discarding it; in-ground spears expire after 1200 ticks.
  - Restored old shattered-shard behavior and wired Ice Dome break feedback back to `100 + random(50)` falling Ice Spear shard entities with random horizontal motion, replacing the simplified block-particle-only break feedback.
- Verification after the Ice Spear launch/scale/dome-shatter slice:
  - `rg -n "JUTSU_SCALE|RAND_YAW|RAND_PITCH|spawnShatteredShard|MotionFactor|AccelerationX|travelLegacy|direction\.scale\(0\.1D\)|WATER_SLOWDOWN|MAX_IN_GROUND_TIME|setEntityScale\(JUTSU_SCALE\)|getDimensions\(Pose|spawnIceTrail\(\)|IceSpearEntity\.spawnShatteredShard" 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\IceSpearEntity.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\IceDomeEntity.java`: reports the restored jutsu scale, shard random rotation fields, persisted acceleration state, legacy travel path, water slowdown, 1200 tick in-ground timeout, real dimension scaling, and Ice Dome shard spawning path.

- Restored Finger Bone's old fired-projectile orientation semantics:

  - The 1.20 `FingerBoneEntity` already preserved scale `0.4F`, no-gravity acceleration, water slowdown, bonecrack/impact sounds, block dust, and 100 tick lifetime, but `travelAndImpact()` recalculated facing every tick from the current motion vector and `faceMotion(...)` used a positive yaw sign.
  - Removed the per-tick `faceMotion(motion)` call from travel so launch inaccuracy owns the model direction like old `shoot(...)`.
  - Changed yaw back to old `-atan2(x,z)` and wrapped pitch after applying the old `+90F` model offset.

- Restored Night Guy Dragon's old suspended launch, scalable-projectile motion, and impact behavior:

  - Restored old `ticksInAir > 30` cleanup timing, entity-position no-fire explosion, old post-impact `motion *= 0.4` continuation on entity hits, old negative yaw, and launched smoke-only particles.
