### M6 Rasenshuriken Legacy Flight Lifetime And Raycast Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the Rasenshuriken flight slice:
  - Latest runtime log was `1.20.1/run/logs/latest.log`, last written `2026-06-14 23:38:00`; it was searched for `ERROR`, `FATAL`, exception, crash, missing texture/model, load failure, and resource-location patterns; no problematic matches were found.
  - Latest crash reports remained unchanged: only the older `2026-06-14 12:03:35` and `2026-06-14 12:39:57` server reports were present.
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0 before code changes.
- Restored focused Rasenshuriken projectile semantics from old `EntityRasenshuriken.EC` / `EntityScalableProjectile.Base`:
  - Old Rasenshuriken used `ticksInAir > 200` for launched-flight cleanup, so held/charging time did not consume the projectile lifetime. The 1.20 entity used total `tickCount > 220`, which could expire a not-yet-flown Rasenshuriken.
  - Old growth ran while `ticksAlive < growTime` and used `fullScale * (ticksAlive + 1) / growTime`; the first post-grow tick only called `shoot(...)`, storing normalized `0.1D` acceleration and `motionFactor=0.95F`.
  - Added persisted `TicksInAir` and explicit `MotionX/Y/Z` fields alongside existing `MotionFactor` and acceleration fields.
  - Changed cleanup to use launched `ticksInAir > 200`, restored the old growth condition and scale formula, and kept the launch tick separated from movement.
  - Changed flight raycast to originate from the scaled entity center like old `forwardsRaycast(...)`, restored the old fixed `1.0D` broad-phase grow, and kept movement on the entity feet position after center-line raycast.
  - Removed the per-flight `faceMotion(motion)` update so no-gravity flight keeps the `shoot(...)` direction like old scalable projectiles, and corrected launch yaw to old `-atan2(x,z)`.
  - Added old water slowdown behavior (`motionFactor * 0.8F`) with bubble particles, and made impact start immediately run the first impact update in the same tick like the old base/subclass update ordering.
  - Preserved the existing impact damage/terrain implementation for this slice; broader Rasenshuriken impact-size parity remains separate from the flight-lifetime/raycast correction.
- Verification after the Rasenshuriken flight slice:
  - `rg -n "MAX_AIR_TICKS|TicksInAir|MotionX|MotionY|MotionZ|centerPosition|ParticleTypes\.BUBBLE|WATER_SLOWDOWN|tickCount < GROW_TIME|faceMotion\(motion\)|setYRot\(\(float\)\(-Mth\.atan2|LightSourceBlock|tickImpact\(\);" 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\RasenshurikenEntity.java`: reports the old launched-flight lifetime, motion persistence, center raycast, water slowdown, old grow condition, immediate impact update, and negative yaw; no `faceMotion(motion)` travel update remains.

### M6 Rasenshuriken Legacy Impact Radius Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the Rasenshuriken impact-radius slice:
  - Latest runtime log was `1.20.1/run/logs/latest.log`, last written `2026-06-14 23:43:32`; it was searched for `ERROR`, `FATAL`, exception, crash, missing texture/model, load failure, resource-location, and missing player-variable patterns; no problematic matches were found.

- Restored the old Rasenshuriken impact size and radius model:
  - Old `EntityRasenshuriken.EC` used original size `2.5F x 0.5F` while flying, then during the first impact updates called `setOGSize(0.5F, 0.5F)` before growing the impact sphere. Damage and terrain radius then used the live entity `width / 2`.
  - The 1.20 entity already had real scale-backed dimensions, but its impact radius was `max(0.75D, currentScale * 0.25D)` and terrain radius was clamped to 6, which made low-scale impacts too large and high-scale expanded impacts far smaller than old code.
  - Added explicit legacy base dimensions for flying (`2.5F x 0.5F`) and impact (`0.5F x 0.5F`) states, with `IMPACT_TICKS` synced-data updates refreshing client/server dimensions.
  - Changed active and impact radius helpers to use the actual current `getBbWidth() * 0.5D`, matching old `this.width / 2`.
  - Removed the 6 block terrain-radius cap and restored old `ceil(width / 2) + 1` terrain radius.
  - Restored old impact growth Y adjustment: as the impact entity grows, its rendered entity position moves down by half the height delta while damage/terrain center remains at `impactVec`.
  - Restored the old AOE command's zero-motion effect by clearing damaged targets' delta movement after each impact tick.
- Verification after the Rasenshuriken impact-radius slice:
  - `rg -n "BASE_WIDTH|BASE_HEIGHT|IMPACT_BASE_SIZE|impactDimensions|getDimensions\(Pose|setImpactTicks|growImpactScale|oldHeight|getBbHeight\(\) - oldHeight|target\.setDeltaMovement\(Vec3\.ZERO\)|ceil\(impactRadius\(\)\) \+ 1|activeRadius\(\)|impactRadius\(\)|getBbWidth\(\) \* 0\.5D|Mth\.clamp\(\(int\)Math\.ceil\(impactRadius" 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\RasenshurikenEntity.java`: reports the flight/impact base dimensions, impact dimension switch, old width-based radii, vertical growth offset, target motion clearing, and unclamped terrain radius; no old clamped terrain-radius pattern remains.

- Restored Rasenshuriken block collision toward old `EntityScalableProjectile.forwardsRaycast(...)`:
  - Old scalable projectiles traced from the entity center, searched the motion-expanded entity AABB with a fixed `grow(1.0D)`, then checked each block collision box grown by `projectile.width / 2`, `projectile.height / 2`, and `projectile.width / 2`.
  - The 1.20 Rasenshuriken flight path had already moved to center-origin raycast and fixed broad-phase grow, but block collision was still a vanilla center-line `level.clip(...)`, so large/scaled Rasenshuriken could miss grazing block collisions that old code would catch.
  - Added `findLegacyBlockImpact(...)` for Rasenshuriken that iterates block collision shapes inside the expanded swept AABB, grows each shape by the current entity half-width/height, and selects the nearest intercept along the center sweep.
  - Replaced the Rasenshuriken block hit path with this swept collision helper while leaving entity hit selection and light-source impact ignore behavior intact.

- Restored focused Tail Beast Ball behavior from old `EntityTailedBeast.EntityTailBeastBall` / `EntityScalableProjectile.Base`:
  - Old Tail Beast Ball used original size `0.25F x 0.25F`, real scale from `0.01F` up to max scale over `buildupTime=100`, `setWaterSlowdown(0.98F)`, then launched on the tick after buildup with `shoot(..., 1.05F, 0.0F)` no-gravity acceleration.
  - The 1.20 `TailBeastBallEntity` already preserved scale-backed dimensions, AI freeze/restore, sounds, damage, and render scale, but launched at the end of tick 100 instead of the following tick, used vanilla `level.clip(...)`, searched entities with radius-scale broadphase, recalculated facing from flight motion, centered impact at the hit vector, and lacked explicit motion-vector persistence.

  - Changed buildup so tick 100 only reaches full scale, and launch happens on the following tick like the old subclass update ordering.
  - Replaced the invented total flight lifetime with the old loaded-chunk gate, so launched balls are discarded when their current chunk is no longer loaded instead of expiring after a fixed 1.20-only timer.
  - Replaced vanilla block clipping with old-style swept block collision boxes grown by current scaled half-width/height, and changed entity search to fixed `1.0D` broad-phase plus scaled target-box inflation.
  - Removed the per-flight `faceMotion(motion)` call so no-gravity flight keeps the initial old `shoot(...)` orientation, and corrected launch yaw to the old negative `atan2(x,z)` sign.
  - Restored impact explosion/AOE center to the Tail Beast Ball entity position instead of the hit-vector position.
  - Restored buildup particles to the available legacy `HOMING_ORB` particle path with old-style count/timing (`ticks <= buildupTime - 40`) and max-scale-based arguments.

### M6 Sand Bullet Legacy ProjectileHelper Raycast Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the Sand Bullet raycast slice:
  - Latest runtime log was `1.20.1/run/logs/latest.log`, last written `2026-06-15 00:35:42`; it was searched for `ERROR`, `FATAL`, exception, crash, missing texture/model, load failure, resource-location, and missing player-variable patterns; no problematic matches were found.
  - Latest crash reports remained unchanged: only the older `2026-06-14 12:03:35` and `2026-06-14 12:39:57` server reports were present.
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0 before code changes.
- Restored focused Sand Bullet behavior from old `EntitySandBullet.EC` / `EntityScalableProjectile.Base`:
  - Old Sand Bullet used original size `0.2F x 0.2F`, spawned at the shooter's eye height, launched with `shoot(..., 1.2F, 0F)`, played the sand-place sound on spawn, rendered suspended colored trail particles, and discarded only after base projectile travel when `ticksAlive > 80`.
  - Old base projectile collision used center-origin raycast, old swept block collision, fixed `1.0D` broad-phase grow, target boxes grown by projectile half-width/height, and ignored the shooter only while `ticksInAir < 25` because the Sand Bullet subclass did not add its own permanent owner guard.
  - The 1.20 `SandBulletEntity` already preserved spawn sound, color, damage, impact sound, push force, and particle type, but used vanilla `level.clip(...)`, fixed `0.2D` target inflation, feet-position raycast start, lifetime discard before travel, unpickable projectile state, permanent owner exclusion, and incomplete saved motion compatibility.

  - Restored the old owner-dead and unloaded-chunk discard gate from the base projectile update.
  - Changed update ordering so travel and collision happen before the `tickCount > 80` lifetime discard, matching the old subclass calling `super.onUpdate()` first.
  - Changed travel raycast to originate from the current scaled entity center instead of the feet position.
  - Replaced vanilla block clipping with old-style swept block collision boxes grown by current projectile half-width/height.
  - Changed entity search to fixed `1.0D` broad-phase grow plus scaled target-box inflation, made the projectile pickable while alive, and restored the old post-25-tick owner self-hit eligibility.
  - Corrected launch yaw to the old negative `atan2(x,z)` sign and kept flight from recalculating facing every tick.

### M6 Finger Bone Legacy ProjectileHelper Raycast Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the Finger Bone raycast slice:
  - Latest runtime log was `1.20.1/run/logs/latest.log`, last written `2026-06-15 00:40:21`; it was searched for `ERROR`, `FATAL`, exception, crash, missing texture/model, load failure, resource-location, and missing player-variable patterns; no problematic matches were found.
  - Latest crash reports remained unchanged: only the older `2026-06-14 12:03:35` and `2026-06-14 12:39:57` server reports were present.
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0 before code changes.
- Restored focused Finger Bone behavior from old `EntityFingerBone.EC` / `EntityScalableProjectile.Base`:
  - Old Finger Bone used original size `0.2F x 0.2F`, entity scale `0.4F`, spawned at `user.position + look + (0, 1.4, 0)`, seeded motion with `look * 0.1`, launched with `shoot(..., 0.95F, 0.05F)`, and added `+90F` pitch during launch so the legacy model orientation renders correctly.
  - Old base projectile collision used center-origin raycast, old swept block collision, fixed `1.0D` broad-phase grow, target boxes grown by projectile half-width/height, and ignored the shooter only while `ticksInAir < 25`.
  - Old Finger Bone additionally ignored same-owner Finger Bone projectiles in raycast, and treated any Finger Bone or the shooter as pass-through in `onImpact` instead of damaging or discarding.
  - The 1.20 `FingerBoneEntity` already preserved spawn sound, damage, bone-block dust, impact sound, battle-exp hook, renderer scale, launch pitch offset, and model spin, but used vanilla `level.clip(...)`, fixed search/target inflation, feet-position raycast start, lifetime discard before travel, permanent owner exclusion, and incomplete saved motion compatibility.
  - Added `FlightTicks`/`TicksInAir` and explicit `MotionX/Y/Z` saved-state compatibility alongside the existing acceleration and motion-factor fields.
  - Restored the old owner-dead and unloaded-chunk discard gate from the base projectile update.
  - Changed update ordering so travel and collision happen before the `tickCount > 100` lifetime discard, matching the old subclass calling `super.onUpdate()` first.
  - Changed travel raycast to originate from the current scaled entity center while keeping entity position advancement as `position + motion`, matching the old center-raycast plus `pos += motion` split.
  - Replaced vanilla block clipping with old-style swept block collision boxes grown by current projectile half-width/height.
  - Changed entity search to fixed `1.0D` broad-phase grow plus scaled target-box inflation, restored the old pre-25-tick shooter ignore, and restored same-owner Finger Bone filtering plus Finger Bone/shooter pass-through impacts.
- Corrected the same center-raycast position advancement issue in the recent Great Fireball, Yooton Magma Ball, Tenseigan Orb, and Sand Bullet slices:
  - Those entities now raycast from their scaled center but advance their entity position by current motion, instead of incorrectly setting entity position to the ray end center point and accumulating a vertical offset.
- Verification after the Finger Bone raycast slice:
  - `rg -n "ClipContext|level\(\)\.clip|findLegacyBlockImpact|FlightTicks|TicksInAir|MotionX|centerPosition|hasChunkAt|target == owner && this\.ticksInAir < 25|isSameOwnerBone|target instanceof FingerBoneEntity|setPos\(getX\(\) \+ motion\.x\(\), getY\(\) \+ motion\.y\(\), getZ\(\) \+ motion\.z\(\)\)|setPos\(end\.x\(\), end\.y\(\), end\.z\(\)\)" 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\FingerBoneEntity.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\KatonFireballEntity.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\MagmaBallEntity.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\TenseiganOrbEntity.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\SandBulletEntity.java`: reports the old chunk/owner gate, persisted flight state, center raycast, swept block collision helper, old Finger Bone pass-through/filtering rules, and corrected `position + motion` advancement; no `ClipContext`, `level().clip`, or center-end `setPos(...)` path remains in the checked files.
  - `.\gradlew.bat "-Dnet.minecraftforge.gradle.check.certs=false" --no-daemon compileJava`: passes, with the existing Forge `ItemBlockRenderTypes.setRenderLayer(...)` deprecation warning from the Water Still render-layer slice.
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0.
  - `python tools\validate_dedicated_server_safety.py`: 275 non-client Java files scanned, 0 client-reference issues.
  - `.\gradlew.bat "-Dnet.minecraftforge.gradle.check.certs=false" --no-daemon build`: passes; latest full build jar is `1.20.1/build/libs/narutomod-0.2.10-beta.1+mc1.20.1.jar`, size 19,970,855 bytes.
  - `tools\run_dedicated_server_gate.ps1`: ready `true`, fatal issue count 0, latest ready line `Done (10.890s)`, checked at `2026-06-15T00:46:16`.

### M6 Asakujaku Legacy Small Fireball Motion Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the Asakujaku slice:
  - Latest runtime log was `1.20.1/run/logs/latest.log`, last written `2026-06-15 00:46:12`; it was searched for `ERROR`, `FATAL`, exception, crash, missing texture/model, load failure, resource-location, and missing player-variable patterns; no problematic matches were found.
  - Latest crash reports remained unchanged: only the older `2026-06-14 12:03:35` and `2026-06-14 12:39:57` server reports were present.
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0 before code changes.
- Restored focused Asakujaku behavior from old `ItemEightGates.RangedItem#attackAsakujaku(...)` and 1.12 `EntitySmallFireball`:
  - Old Asakujaku spawned 10 `EntitySmallFireball` instances at the attacker's eye position, each constructed with the same base direction but with vanilla fireball Gaussian scatter (`rand.nextGaussian() * 0.4`) baked into acceleration.
  - Old `EntityFireball` initialized motion to zero, raycasted/moved before adding acceleration, then applied `getMotionFactor() = 1.1F`; the 1.20 port had been adding acceleration before travel and carrying the owner's current movement into the projectile.
  - Old anonymous Asakujaku `onImpact` ignored the shooter and other small fireballs by returning without killing the projectile, damaged entity hits for `modifiedAttackDamage * 0.5F`, set targets on fire for 10 seconds, exploded at the fireball entity position with strength 2, and discarded after the old superclass update when `ticksExisted > 12`.
  - `AsakujakuFireballEntity` now stores `FlightTicks`/`TicksInAir` and explicit `MotionX/Y/Z` for saved-state compatibility.
  - Restored old owner-dead and unloaded-chunk discard behavior from `EntityFireball`.
  - Restored old Small Fireball launch semantics: per-projectile Gaussian scatter, zero initial motion, travel/raycast before acceleration, `position + motion` advancement, and post-update lifetime discard.
  - Restored old vanilla projectile entity search shape: `1.0D` broad-phase grow, `0.3D` target-box grow, pickable/noPhysics checks, and shooter exclusion only while `ticksInAir < 25`.
  - Restored old pass-through impact behavior for the shooter and other Asakujaku fireballs, while keeping block/entity impacts exploding from the current fireball entity position instead of the hit vector.
  - Restored the old `ProjectileHelper.rotateTowardsMovement(entity, 0.2F)` yaw/pitch formulas for in-flight facing.
  - Kept vanilla-style block raytracing (`ClipContext` / `level().clip`) for this slice intentionally, because old Asakujaku used vanilla `EntitySmallFireball` rather than the custom `EntityScalableProjectile.Base` swept block collision path.
- Verification after the Asakujaku slice:

  - `.\gradlew.bat "-Dnet.minecraftforge.gradle.check.certs=false" --no-daemon compileJava`: passes, with the existing Forge `ItemBlockRenderTypes.setRenderLayer(...)` deprecation warning from the Water Still render-layer slice.

### M6 Kagutsuchi Black Fireball Legacy EntityFireball Motion Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the Kagutsuchi Black Fireball slice:
  - Latest runtime log was `1.20.1/run/logs/latest.log`, last written `2026-06-15 00:52:19`; it was searched for `ERROR`, `FATAL`, exception, crash, missing texture/model, load failure, resource-location, and missing player-variable patterns; no problematic matches were found.
  - Latest crash reports remained unchanged: only the older `2026-06-14 12:03:35` and `2026-06-14 12:39:57` server reports were present.
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0 before code changes.
- Restored focused Kagutsuchi Black Fireball behavior from old `ItemKagutsuchiSwordRanged.EntityBlackFireball` / `EntityBigBlackFireball` and 1.12 `EntityFireball`:
  - Old Kagutsuchi spawned three black fireballs from the player's eye direction fan, using `EntityFireball` constructors that set projectile motion to zero and bake `rand.nextGaussian() * 0.4D` scatter into acceleration.
  - Old `EntityFireball` raycasted and moved using the current motion, then rotated with `ProjectileHelper.rotateTowardsMovement(entity, 0.2F)`, then applied acceleration and `getMotionFactor() = 0.95F`; the previous 1.20 port used direct `direction.normalize().scale(0.95F)` velocity instead.
  - Old Kagutsuchi did not have the 1.20-only `MAX_LIFE = 100` or water discard rule; water only changed the motion factor to `0.8F` and spawned bubbles.
  - Old same-type black fireballs and the shooter were pass-through impacts. Other entity/block impacts placed Amaterasu blocks and applied the Amaterasu AOE from the fireball entity position, not the ray hit location.
  - `KagutsuchiFireballEntity` now persists `AccelerationX/Y/Z`, `FlightTicks`/`TicksInAir`, and `MotionX/Y/Z`.
  - Restored old owner-dead and unloaded-chunk discard behavior from `EntityFireball`.
  - Restored old zero-initial-motion launch, Gaussian acceleration scatter, travel-before-acceleration tick order, `0.95F` / water `0.8F` motion factors, and water bubble path.
  - Restored old vanilla projectile entity search shape: `1.0D` broad-phase grow, `0.3D` target-box grow, pickable/noPhysics checks, and shooter exclusion only while `ticksInAir < 25`.
  - Restored old same-type/shooter pass-through impacts and changed Amaterasu block/area center back to the fireball entity position.
  - Restored old `ProjectileHelper.rotateTowardsMovement(entity, 0.2F)` yaw/pitch formulas for in-flight facing.
  - Kept vanilla-style block raytracing (`ClipContext` / `level().clip`) intentionally, because the old Kagutsuchi projectiles inherited vanilla `EntityFireball`, not `EntityScalableProjectile.Base`.

### M6 Hirudora Legacy Scalable Projectile Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the Hirudora slice:
  - Latest runtime log was `1.20.1/run/logs/latest.log`, last written `2026-06-15 00:57:06`; it was searched for `ERROR`, `FATAL`, exception, crash, missing texture/model, load failure, resource-location, and missing player-variable patterns; no problematic matches were found.
  - Latest crash reports remained unchanged: only the older `2026-06-14 12:03:35` and `2026-06-14 12:39:57` server reports were present.
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0 before code changes.
- Restored focused Hirudora behavior from old `ItemEightGates.RangedItem#attackHirudora(...)`, `EntityHirudora`, and `EntityScalableProjectile.Base`:
  - Old Hirudora waited at `owner.position + (0, 0.5, 0)`, grew from scale 1 to 6 during `NGD_SUSPEND_TIME`, then launched at speed `1.2F` with zero initial motion and acceleration from the owner look or mob attack target.
  - Old base projectile travel raycasted from the scaled entity center but advanced the entity by `position + motion`; the 1.20 port had been raycasting from the feet position and using vanilla block clipping.
  - Old base projectile block collision swept the projectile-size-grown collision boxes, used a fixed `1.0D` broad-phase grow for entity search, inflated target boxes by projectile half-width/height, and ignored the shooter only while `ticksInAir < 25`.
  - Old base projectile discarded when the owner died or the current block was unloaded, and saved motion/flight state through legacy NBT fields.
  - `HirudoraEntity` now persists `FlightTicks`/`TicksInAir`, explicit `MotionX/Y/Z`, acceleration, motion factor, scale, launch state, and owner UUID.
  - Restored old owner-dead and unloaded-chunk discard behavior.
  - Restored center-origin raycast with `position + motion` entity advancement, old swept block collision, fixed `1.0D` entity broad phase, scaled target-box inflation, and old pre-25-tick shooter ignore.
  - Restored same-owner Hirudora filtering for the scalable-projectile raycast path while preserving owner pass-through handling if an owner hit is encountered after the old 25-tick window.
  - Restored Hirudora AOE radius to `0.5D * scale`, matching the old `ProcedureAoeCommand.set(this, 0.0D, 0.5d * getEntityScale())` radius, while keeping the legacy owner invulnerability marker and strength-70 explosion at the entity position.
  - Added the old no-gravity water path: water uses `motionFactor * 0.8F` and spawns bubbles instead of discarding.
- Verification after the Hirudora slice:
  - `rg -n "findLegacyBlockImpact|centerPosition|FlightTicks|TicksInAir|MotionX|hasChunkAt|isPickable\(\)|noPhysics|target == owner && this\.ticksInAir < 25|isSameOwnerHirudora|impact\(HitResult hit\)|0\.5D \* getHirudoraScale\(\)|ParticleTypes\.BUBBLE|setPos\(getX\(\) \+ motion\.x\(\), getY\(\) \+ motion\.y\(\), getZ\(\) \+ motion\.z\(\)\)|ClipContext|level\(\)\.clip|setPos\(end\.x\(\), end\.y\(\), end\.z\(\)\)" 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\HirudoraEntity.java`: reports the old persisted flight state, owner/chunk gate, center raycast, swept block collision helper, old entity search and owner filtering, same-owner Hirudora filtering, old AOE radius, water bubble path, and corrected `position + motion` advancement; no `ClipContext`, `level().clip`, or center-end `setPos(...)` path remains.
  - `.\gradlew.bat "-Dnet.minecraftforge.gradle.check.certs=false" --no-daemon compileJava`: passes, with the existing Forge `ItemBlockRenderTypes.setRenderLayer(...)` deprecation warning from the Water Still render-layer slice.

### M6 Night Guy Dragon Legacy Scalable Projectile Collision Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the Night Guy Dragon slice:
  - Latest runtime log was `1.20.1/run/logs/latest.log`, last written `2026-06-15 01:03:25`; it was searched for `ERROR`, `FATAL`, exception, crash, missing texture/model, load failure, resource-location, and missing player-variable patterns; no problematic matches were found.
  - Latest crash reports remained unchanged: only the older `2026-06-14 12:03:35` and `2026-06-14 12:39:57` server reports were present.
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0 before code changes.
- Restored focused Night Guy Dragon behavior from old `ItemEightGates.RangedItem#attackNightGuy(...)`, `EntityNGDragon`, and `EntityScalableProjectile.Base`:
  - Old Night Guy Dragon used original size `1.0F x 1.0F`, waited on the owner for `NGD_SUSPEND_TIME`, grew to scale 6, then launched with no-gravity `shoot(..., 1.2F, 0F)`.
  - Old scalable projectile travel raycasted from the scaled entity center, used swept block collision boxes grown by projectile half-width/height, searched entities with a fixed `1.0D` broad phase, inflated target boxes by projectile half-width/height, and advanced the entity by `position + motion`.
  - Old base projectile ignored the shooter only before `ticksInAir >= 25`; the Night Guy subclass then treated a shooter hit as pass-through in `onImpact`.
  - Old Night Guy did not globally ignore other Night Guy Dragon entities and did not use a same-root-vehicle exclusion in raycast filtering.
  - Old Night Guy impact damaged direct living hits for `modifiedAttackDamage * 64`, applied a 5-block bypass-armor AOE for `modifiedAttackDamage * 16`, exploded at the dragon entity position, discarded only if a block was on the current swept path, and otherwise multiplied current motion by `0.4F` so the dragon continued through entity hits.
  - `NightGuyDragonEntity` now persists `FlightTicks`/`TicksInAir`/`LaunchedTicks`/`flighttime` plus explicit `MotionX/Y/Z`, acceleration, motion factor, scale, launch state, and owner UUID.
  - Restored the old owner-dead and unloaded-chunk discard gate.
  - Restored center-origin raycast with `position + motion` advancement, old swept block collision, fixed `1.0D` entity broad phase, scaled target-box inflation, old shooter pre-25 filter, shooter pass-through, and old block-only death check after entity impacts.
  - Restored old no-gravity water behavior: water uses `motionFactor * 0.8F` and spawns bubbles instead of using a dry-only motion update.
- Verification after the Night Guy Dragon slice:
  - `rg -n "findLegacyBlockImpact|centerPosition|FlightTicks|flighttime|MotionX|hasChunkAt|isPickable\(\)|target != owner \|\| this\.ticksInAir >= 25|impact\(LivingEntity owner, HitResult hit, Vec3 start, Vec3 end\)|ParticleTypes\.BUBBLE|ClipContext|level\(\)\.clip|setPos\(end\.x\(\), end\.y\(\), end\.z\(\)\)|target instanceof NightGuyDragonEntity|getRootVehicle" 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\NightGuyDragonEntity.java`: reports the old persisted flight state, owner/chunk gate, center raycast, swept block collision helper, old entity search and shooter filtering, block-only death check, water bubble path, and corrected `position + motion` advancement; no `ClipContext`, `level().clip`, center-end `setPos(...)`, all-Night-Guy filter, or same-root vehicle exclusion remains.
  - `.\gradlew.bat "-Dnet.minecraftforge.gradle.check.certs=false" --no-daemon compileJava`: passes, with the existing Forge `ItemBlockRenderTypes.setRenderLayer(...)` deprecation warning from the Water Still render-layer slice.

### M6 Shakuton Scorch Orb Legacy Scalable Projectile Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the Scorch Orb slice:
  - Latest runtime log was `1.20.1/run/logs/latest.log`, last written `2026-06-15 01:08:54`; it was searched for `ERROR`, `FATAL`, exception, crash, missing texture/model, load failure, resource-location, and missing player-variable patterns; no problematic matches were found.
  - Latest crash reports remained unchanged: only the older `2026-06-14 12:03:35` and `2026-06-14 12:39:57` server reports were present.
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0 before code changes.
- Restored focused Shakuton Scorch Orb behavior from old `ItemShakuton.EntityScorchBall` and `EntityScalableProjectile.Base`:
  - Old Scorch Orb used original size `1.0F x 1.0F`, initial scale `0.5F`, orbited the owner at eye height using `Vec3d.fromPitchYaw(0, ticksExisted * 9)`, moved to target eyes for 60 ticks, and killed itself if the owner stopped holding the Shakuton item or the orb entered water.
  - The 1.20 implementation already preserved the active-orb limit, item-held lifetime gate, target routing, idle orbit, target-time behavior, Scorch Blast first-orb scaling, billboard renderer, `fireball2.png` texture, and idle scorch-touch damage.
  - Old scalable projectile travel for launched orbs raycasted from the scaled entity center, used swept block collision boxes grown by projectile half-width/height, searched entities with a fixed `1.0D` broad phase, inflated target boxes by projectile half-width/height, and advanced the entity by `position + motion`.
  - Old base projectile ignored the shooter only before `ticksInAir >= 25`; the Scorch Orb subclass then treated a shooter hit as pass-through in `onImpact`.
  - `ScorchOrbEntity` now persists `FlightTicks`/`TicksInAir`/`flighttime` plus explicit `MotionX/Y/Z`, acceleration, motion factor, scale, max scale, target time, launch state, owner UUID, and target UUID.
  - Restored old owner-dead and unloaded-chunk discard behavior.
  - Restored launched-orb center raycast with `position + motion` advancement, old swept block collision, fixed `1.0D` entity broad phase, scaled target-box inflation, old shooter pre-25 filter, and shooter pass-through.
  - Restored the old Scorch Blast grow semantics by letting `scale * 1.03F` overshoot `maxScale` instead of clamping exactly to max before launch.
  - Restored the old negative-yaw `shoot(...)` rotation and made the orb pickable/collidable like old `canBeCollidedWith()`.
  - Restored impact center to the orb entity position for spherical explosion, AOE damage, and scorch effects instead of using the ray hit vector.

### M6 Melting Jutsu Legacy Scalable Projectile Collision Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the Melting Jutsu slice:
  - Latest runtime log was `1.20.1/run/logs/latest.log`, last written `2026-06-15 01:12:59`; it was searched for `ERROR`, `FATAL`, exception, crash, missing texture/model, load failure, resource-location, and missing player-variable patterns; no problematic matches were found.
  - Latest crash reports remained unchanged: only the older `2026-06-14 12:03:35` and `2026-06-14 12:39:57` server reports were present.
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0 before code changes.
- Restored focused Melting Jutsu behavior from old `EntityMeltingJutsu.EC` and `EntityScalableProjectile.Base`:
  - Old Melting Jutsu used original size `0.25F x 0.25F`, initial scale `0.5F`, an emitter at the user's eye/look offset, 10 droplets per emission tick, Gaussian launch inaccuracy `0.1F`, launch speed `0.85F`, no in-flight rotation updates, growth from scale `0.5F` to `4.0F` over the first 20 flight ticks, and a 100 tick lifetime after the old base update path.
  - The 1.20 implementation already preserved the emitter, lava placement, lava solidification, death pull-down effect, legacy texture/model, Gaussian inaccuracy, launch-only facing, and old non-no-gravity drag/gravity constants, but still used vanilla `ClipContext`, fixed target inflation, feet-position raycast, tick-count growth, and incomplete flight-state persistence.
  - `MeltingJutsuEntity` now persists `FlightTicks`/`TicksInAir`/`flighttime`, `MotionFactor`, and explicit `MotionX/Y/Z` alongside owner, scale, emission, death, and drip state.
  - Restored the old owner-dead and unloaded-chunk discard gate before active flight/emission while preserving the old death-update path after impact.
  - Restored launched-droplet travel to use center-origin raycast, old swept block collision boxes grown by projectile half-width/height, fixed `1.0D` entity broad phase, scaled target-box inflation, old pre-25-tick shooter filtering, and same-owner Melting Jutsu filtering from the scalable-projectile base path.
  - Restored `position + motion` entity advancement instead of setting entity position to the center ray end.
  - Restored droplet growth and movement sound timing to use `ticksInAir` after the old base-style travel step, with the old movement sound volume/pitch range.
  - Restored same-type Melting Jutsu impacts as pass-through and kept lava placement/death startup at the old block/entity impact position logic.

### M6 Kirin Legacy Scalable Projectile Collision Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the Kirin slice:
  - Latest runtime log was `1.20.1/run/logs/latest.log`, last written `2026-06-15 01:18:23`; it was searched for `ERROR`, `FATAL`, exception, crash, missing texture/model, load failure, resource-location, and missing player-variable patterns; no problematic matches were found.
  - Latest crash reports remained unchanged: only the older `2026-06-14 12:03:35` and `2026-06-14 12:39:57` server reports were present.
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0 before code changes.
- Restored focused Kirin behavior from old `EntityKirin.EC` and `EntityScalableProjectile.Base`:
  - Old Kirin used original size `1.0F x 1.0F`, entity scale `10.0F`, spawned 100 blocks above the caster, waited 60 ticks while orienting toward the target/look vector, then launched with no-gravity `shoot(..., 1.2F, 0F)`.
  - The 1.20 implementation already preserved the high spawn, wait/launch timing, dragon roar and lightning shoot/impact sounds, lightning segment rendering, lightning arc, scale-10 explosion, and 10-block AOE fire/damage behavior.
  - Old scalable projectile travel raycasted from the scaled entity center, used swept block collision boxes grown by projectile half-width/height, searched entities with a fixed `1.0D` broad phase, inflated target boxes by projectile half-width/height, and advanced the entity by `position + motion`.
  - Old base projectile ignored the shooter only before `ticksInAir >= 25`; the Kirin subclass then treated direct shooter hits as pass-through in `onImpact`.
  - `KirinEntity` now persists `FlightTicks`/`TicksInAir`/`flighttime` plus explicit `MotionX/Y/Z`, acceleration, motion factor, scale, launch state, wait countdown, owner UUID, and lightning segment state.
  - Restored the old owner-dead and unloaded-chunk discard gate.
  - Restored launched Kirin center raycast with `position + motion` advancement, old swept block collision, fixed `1.0D` entity broad phase, scaled target-box inflation, old shooter pre-25 filter, shooter pass-through, and same-owner Kirin filtering from the scalable-projectile base path.
  - Removed the per-tick in-flight facing update after launch so no-gravity Kirin keeps the launch rotation like the old base projectile path.
  - Made Kirin pickable/collidable while alive, matching old `canBeCollidedWith()`.

### M6 Lightning Beast Legacy Destination Cadence Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the Lightning Beast slice:
  - Latest runtime log was `1.20.1/run/logs/latest.log`, last written `2026-06-15 01:22:54`; it was searched for `ERROR`, `FATAL`, exception, crash, missing texture/model, load failure, resource-location, and missing player-variable patterns; no problematic matches were found.

  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0 before code changes.
- Restored focused Lightning Beast behavior from old `EntityLightningBeast.EC`:
  - Old player-spawned Lightning Beast used `ProcedureUtils.raytraceBlocks(player, 4.0D)` and always spawned at that returned block X/Z plus `0.5`, rather than falling back to an arbitrary look-vector position on a miss.
  - Old `updateAITasks()` recomputed the owner's current 50-block look destination every AI update, but only ran the high-jump and navigator `tryMoveToXYZ(...)` block every 10 ticks.
  - The 1.20 implementation now mirrors that cadence: each server tick refreshes `destination` when the owner's legacy-style block/liquid look ray finds a valid top-solid or fluid block, while `moveTowardDestination()` is gated by `tickCount % 10 == 0`.
  - Old `EC(World)` set `isImmuneToFire = true`, but the player constructor set `isImmuneToFire = false`; the 1.20 entity now tracks `PlayerSummoned` and returns `fireImmune() == false` for normal jutsu-spawned beasts, while preserving fire immunity for non-player/base-spawned instances.
  - Added `PlayerSummoned` saved-state compatibility and defaults older owned saved beasts to player-summoned semantics when that tag is absent.
- Verification after the Lightning Beast slice:
  - `rg -n "summonedByPlayer|PlayerSummoned|fireImmune\(\)|findDestination\(|tickCount % 10|moveTowardDestination\(|raytraceBlocks\(owner, 4\.0D\)|getBlockPos\(\)\.getX\(\) \+ 0\.5D|ClipContext\.Fluid\.ANY|ClipContext\.Fluid\.NONE|updateDestination\(" 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\LightningBeastEntity.java`: reports the old player-spawn flag, saved `PlayerSummoned` tag, fire-immunity override, legacy 4-block spawn X/Z, per-tick destination finder, 10-tick movement gate, and fluid-aware 50-block owner-look ray; no stale `updateDestination(...)` method remains.

- Restored focused False Darkness behavior from old `EntityFalseDarkness.EC`:
  - Old False Darkness used `setSize(0.01F, 0.01F)`; the 1.20 entity type now registers `narutomod:false_darkness` at `0.01F x 0.01F` instead of the wider temporary `0.1F x 0.1F` size.
  - Old `onUpdate()` only discarded when `user == null`; it did not check `user.isEntityAlive()` before charging or firing.
  - Old firing only required `target != null`; it did not require the target to still be alive before spawning the 40-tick black lightning arc.
  - `FalseDarknessEntity` now matches those gates: the owner must resolve, but no owner-alive predicate is applied, and the final shot fires whenever the stored target still resolves.
  - Existing old behavior remains preserved: buildup duration `power * 20`, mouth/eye position follow at `eyeY - 0.2`, increasing black charge arcs, high-pitch electricity buildup sound, loud fire cue, target lightning-impact sound, black lightning color `0x000000FF`, and damage `30 * power`.

  - Old runtime position followed the user at exactly `(user.posX, user.posY, user.posZ)`; `ShadowImitationEntity#moveToOwner(...)` now uses the owner's base Y instead of the previous `+0.05D` offset.

### M6 Inton Raiha Legacy Power/Arc Timing Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the Inton Raiha power/timing slice:
  - Latest runtime log was `1.20.1/run/logs/latest.log`, last written `2026-06-15 01:34:28`; it was searched for `ERROR`, `FATAL`, exception, crash, missing texture/model, load failure, resource-location, and missing player-variable patterns; no problematic matches were found.

- Restored focused Inton Raiha numeric/runtime parity from old `EntityIntonRaiha.EC`:
  - Confirmed `narutomod:inton_raiha` already preserves the old tiny `0.01F x 0.01F` controller entity size and no-op renderer boundary.
  - Old spawn initialized the controller with yaw/pitch `0.0F, 0.0F`; `IntonRaihaEntity#configure(...)` now does the same instead of copying the caster's current body/head rotation.

  - Existing restored behavior remains unchanged: 50 tick charge wait for `power >= 4`, bow-pose sync/clear, caster-follow position, purple randomized cone arcs, electricity/intonraiha sounds, senjutsu damage, and cleanup at `waitTime + power * 10`.

### M6 Chibaku Tensei Legacy Progressive Block Capture Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the Chibaku Tensei block-capture slice:
  - Latest runtime log was `1.20.1/run/logs/latest.log`, last written `2026-06-15 01:39:18`; it was searched for `ERROR`, `FATAL`, exception, crash, missing texture/model, load failure, resource-location, and missing player-variable patterns; no problematic matches were found.
  - Latest crash reports remained unchanged: only the older `2026-06-14 12:03:35` and `2026-06-14 12:39:57` server reports were present.
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0 before code changes.
- Restored the focused old Chibaku Tensei block-capture flow from `EntityChibakuTenseiBall.EntityCustom`:
  - Old Chibaku built a `getBoundingBox().grow(100D)` non-air/collidable block candidate list after the 100 tick launch phase; the 1.20 ball now builds the same 100 block candidate list instead of doing one small `CAPTURE_RADIUS = 7` capture.
  - Removed the temporary 1.20 `MAX_LIFE_TICKS = 220` blackhole cutoff so the ball can continue until the old max-size/drop condition is reached.
  - Old block capture randomly attempted a group every 10 ticks, dislodging `6 + rand.nextInt(4)` blocks via `EntityEarthBlocks`; the 1.20 ball now spawns moving `EarthBlocksEntity` groups with the same 6..9 group size and pulls them toward the core.
  - `EarthBlocksEntity` now has an opt-in movement mode used by Chibaku groups, plus block-state copy/clear helpers; the default debug/static EarthBlocks behavior remains unchanged.
  - Old Chibaku filled nearest air blocks around the full-scale core and marked max size when the nearest remaining air was outside the core radius; the 1.20 ball now fills core air positions from incoming EarthBlocks and starts the old 30 tick drop timer on the same radius condition.
  - Old final satellite came from non-air blocks in `getBoundingBox().grow(1)` after max size; the 1.20 ball now captures those core blocks on drop and spawns `ChibakuSatelliteEntity` then cleans up the active flag.
  - Raised EarthBlocks/Satellite block sync capacity to 8192 and Chibaku satellite packing radius to 10 so the restored core is not truncated to the previous 512-block / radius-8 temporary limit.
  - Kept the already-restored 100 tick launch motion order, full scale 80, 128 block living-target pull radius, 2 tick Mining Fatigue III collision penalty, ModelBall renderer, owner active-tag cleanup, and satellite impact damage/explosion paths.
- Verification after the Chibaku Tensei progressive block-capture slice:
  - `rg -n "maxScale = 80f|launchTime = 100|grow\(100d\)|rand\.nextInt\(10\)|6 \+ this\.rand\.nextInt\(4\)|maxSizeReached|dropTime = 30|new Satellite\(this\.shootingEntity, list\)|getNonAirBlocks\(this\.world, this\.getEntityBoundingBox\(\)\.grow\(1\)" 1.12.2\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\EntityChibakuTenseiBall.java`: reports the old max scale, launch time, 100 block capture list, 1-in-10 cadence, 6..9 dislodge size, max-size/drop timer, and local core-block satellite spawn evidence.
  - `rg -n "BLOCK_CAPTURE_RADIUS = 100|EARTH_BLOCK_GROUP_MIN = 6|EARTH_BLOCK_GROUP_VARIANCE = 4|DROP_DELAY_TICKS = 30|createBlockList\(|dislodgeNextBlockGroup\(|collectDislodgeGroup\(|pullTrackedEarthBlocks\(|absorbEarthBlocks\(|ensureAirBlocks\(|maybeMarkMaxSizeReached\(|dropSatellite\(|captureCoreBlocks\(|tickCount > MAX_LIFE_TICKS|CAPTURE_RADIUS = 7|MAX_CAPTURED_BLOCKS = 384" 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\ChibakuTenseiBallEntity.java`: reports the restored constants and progressive capture helpers; no temporary 220 tick cutoff, small capture radius, or 384 block limit remains.
  - `rg -n "movementEnabled|MAX_SYNC_BLOCKS = 8192|copyBlockStates\(|clearBlocksAndDiscard\(|setMovementEnabled\(|move\(MoverType\.SELF, motion\)|setDeltaMovement\(motion\.scale\(0\.98D\)\)|MAX_BLOCKS = 8192|MAX_RADIUS = 10|Math\.cbrt" 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\EarthBlocksEntity.java 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\ChibakuSatelliteEntity.java`: reports the Chibaku EarthBlocks movement/copy helpers and enlarged satellite sync/packing limits.

- Restored a focused, server-verifiable part of old `EntityShadowImitation.EC` PlayerInput behavior:
  - Old Shadow Imitation called `PlayerInput.Hook.haltTargetInput(target, true)` on activation and reverted it on cleanup; non-player living targets now have `Mob#setNoAi(true)` applied on the first active tick, navigation stopped while controlled, and `setNoAi(false)` restored on removal.
  - Replaced the temporary universal paralysis/slowdown/zero-motion placeholder for non-player targets with legacy-style controlled movement: target facing and pitch follow the owner, owner `xxa/zza` input is passed through `moveRelative(0.2F, ...)`, jumping mirrors owner upward motion with the old `0.42D` impulse, and old gravity/friction damping is applied after the forced move.

  - Player targets remain on the existing server-side fallback path: facing follows the owner and short paralysis/Slowness effects keep them pinned. The full old player-to-player client input hook still requires a separate network/client slice because old `PlayerInput` also mirrored raw keyboard/mouse packets and halted target GUI input client-side.
  - Existing restored behavior remains unchanged: initial 30 block target acquisition, sustained old `ItemJutsu.canTarget(...)`-style gate, line-of-sight gate, owner-position follow, shadow sound/particles, chakra upkeep, active-id cleanup, and dimension cleanup.

  - `JintonCubeEntity#tick()` now returns immediately after `anchorAtLookTarget(owner)` on `tickCount == WAIT_TICKS`, preserving the old one-tick split between anchor placement and the following hold/growth phase.
  - Existing restored behavior remains unchanged: 60 tick wait, 30 tick cube growth, 40 tick full-size idle, 10 tick shrink, old full-scale formula, falling-dust feedback, overlap-volume Jinton damage, Jinton Beam 60 tick wait/60 tick active lifetime, 30 block active beam, armor-bypassing Jinton damage, and legacy model/render paths.

- Restored the old Fire Stream block-ignition boundary from `ItemKaton.EntityFireStream.FireStream#processAffectedBlock(...)`:
  - Old Fire Stream overrode `ProcedureAirPunch#processAffectedBlock(...)` and did not call the parent mob-griefing gate for its fire-placement side effect; `KatonFireStreamEntity#igniteBlocks(...)` no longer blocks ignition on a direct `GameRules.RULE_MOBGRIEFING` read.
  - Old Fire Stream only attempted to ignite adjacent air when the affected block was more than 4 blocks away from the caster (`player.getDistanceSq(pos) > 16d`) and the 10 percent random chance passed.
  - The 1.20 Fire Stream now returns immediately when the active range is at or below 4 blocks and samples ignition positions from `4 + random * (range - 4)`, preserving the old beyond-4-block ignition boundary.
  - Existing restored Fire Stream behavior remains unchanged: 50 tick wait, 110 tick max life, owner look-offset follow, post-wait active phase, old quadratic range falloff, randomized fire damage, `setSecondsOnFire(10)`, flame particles, flamethrower loop, and no-op renderer.

### M6 Futton Mist Legacy Temporary Ice Melt Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the Futton Mist temporary ice-melt slice:
  - Latest runtime log was `1.20.1/run/logs/latest.log`, last written `2026-06-15 02:11:11`; it was searched for error, crash, missing texture/model, resource-location, and missing player-variable patterns; no matches were found.
  - Latest crash reports remained unchanged: only the older `2026-06-14 12:03:35` and `2026-06-14 12:39:57` server reports were present.
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0 before code changes.
- Restored the old Futton Mist temporary ice-melt side effect:
  - Old `ItemFutton.EntityBoilingMist.AirPunch#processAffectedBlock(...)` converted ice and packed ice by creating a one-block map with `Blocks.WATER.getDefaultState()` and scheduling `new EventSetBlocks(player.world, map, 0, 100, true, false)`.
  - `FuttonMistEntity#processBlocks(...)` now schedules `SpecialEvent.setBlocksEvent(level, Map.of(pos.immutable(), Blocks.WATER.defaultBlockState()), 0L, 100, true, false)` for ice and packed ice instead of permanently setting water immediately.
  - The old fire branch remains immediate air removal, matching `player.world.setBlockToAir(pos)`, and the prior owner-scoped Forge mob-griefing gate plus Amaterasu-block exclusion remain unchanged.

### M6 Night Guy Legacy Explosion MobGriefing Event Slice

- Preflight texture/crash check before the Night Guy explosion mob-griefing slice:
  - Latest runtime log was `1.20.1/run/logs/latest.log`, last written `2026-06-15 02:44:29`; the Narutomod-focused crash/resource/capability search found no matches.
  - Latest crash reports remained unchanged: only the older `2026-06-14 12:03:35` and `2026-06-14 12:39:57` server reports were present.
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0 before code changes.
- Restored the old Night Guy dragon explosion terrain gate:
  - Old `ItemEightGates.EntityNGDragon#onImpact(...)` used the dragon entity itself as the explosion source, but queried terrain permission with `ForgeEventFactory.getMobGriefingEvent(this.world, this.shootingEntity)`.
  - Old impact then called `world.newExplosion(this, posX, posY, posZ, 10.0F, false, mobGriefing)`, so fire creation was false and terrain damage followed the owner-scoped Forge mob-griefing event result.
  - `NightGuyDragonEntity#impact(...)` now uses `ForgeEventFactory.getMobGriefingEvent(serverLevel, owner)` and maps the result to `Level.ExplosionInteraction.MOB` or `NONE`, while keeping the explosion entity as the dragon and the fire flag explicitly `false`.
  - Existing Night Guy behavior remains unchanged: 20 tick suspend/grow phase, scale 6, 1.2 launch speed, owner ride-along, direct `64 * modified attack damage`, AOE `16 * modified attack damage`, 5 block AOE radius, 10 strength explosion, 1999 resistance reduction, smoke/flame particles, and the intentionally excluded Death Gate death behavior were not changed in this slice.
- Verification after the Night Guy explosion mob-griefing slice:
  - `rg -n "ForgeEventFactory|getMobGriefingEvent\(serverLevel, owner\)|serverLevel\.explode\(this, point\.x\(\), point\.y\(\), point\.z\(\), EXPLOSION_STRENGTH, false|Level\.ExplosionInteraction\.NONE|Level\.ExplosionInteraction\.MOB" 1.20.1\src\main\java\net\narutomod\entity\NightGuyDragonEntity.java`: reports the restored owner-scoped Forge event gate and explicit no-fire dragon-source explosion call.
  - `rg -n "this\.world\.newExplosion\(this, this\.posX, this\.posY, this\.posZ, 10\.0F, false|ForgeEventFactory\.getMobGriefingEvent\(this\.world, this\.shootingEntity\)|result\.entityHit\.attackEntityFrom\(ds, f \* 64f\)|ProcedureAoeCommand\.set\(this, 0\.0D, 5\.0D\)|getExplosionResistance\(Explosion explosionIn" 1.12.2\src\main\java\net\narutomod\item\ItemEightGates.java`: reports the old Night Guy direct damage, AOE damage, explosion mob-griefing, and resistance-reduction evidence.
  - `.\gradlew.bat "-Dnet.minecraftforge.gradle.check.certs=false" --no-daemon compileJava`: passes, with the existing Forge `ItemBlockRenderTypes.setRenderLayer(...)` deprecation warning from the Water Still render-layer slice.

### M7 Commands Restoration + Max-Everything Slice

- Goal: restore the three original 1.12.2 gameplay commands as Brigadier commands and add a new one-click "max everything" command, separate from the `/narutoport` debug tree in `PortingDebugCommands`.
- Original commands audited (1.12.2):
  - `/addninjaxp <target> <integer>` (`command/CommandAddNinjaXp.java:53` + `ProcedureAddNinjaXpCommandExecuted`): perm level 4; added battle XP to a target player only if that player already had the `narutomod:ninjaachievement`; messaged "<name> is not a ninja." / "No player found ...". Used `PlayerTracker.addBattleXp`.
  - `/addxp2jutsu <integer>` (`command/CommandAddXP2Jutsu.java` + `ProcedureAddXP2JutsuCommandExecuted`): operated on the executing player; precedence biju cloak (`EntityBijuManager.cloakLevel>0` -> `addCloakXp`) -> main/off-hand Eight Gates (`ItemEightGates.addBattleXP`) -> main/off-hand `ItemJutsu.Base` (`ItemJutsu.addBattleXP`, current jutsu).
  - `/locateEntity <biju [num] | jinchuriki {list | revoke {all|[num]} | assign [player] [num]}>` (`command/CommandLocateEntity.java` + `ProcedureLocateEntityCommandExecuted`): biju position lookup + jinchuriki management via `EntityBijuManager`.
- New file `command/NarutoCommands.java` (`@Mod.EventBusSubscriber` -> `RegisterCommandsEvent`):
  - `/addninjaxp <targets> <amount>` (perm 4): `EntityArgument.players()`; per target, faithfully gates on `NarutomodModVariables.isNinja` (battle_experience > 0) unless creative, then `addBattleExperience` (capped at 100000).
  - `/addxp2jutsu <amount>`: cloak (`BijuManager.getCloakLevel>0` -> `addCloakXp`) -> Eight Gates (`EightGatesItem.addBattleXP(player, amount)`) -> held `JutsuItem` (`JutsuItem.addXpToCurrentJutsu`). Same precedence as the original.
  - `/locateentity biju <1-10>`: scans loaded `TailedBeastEntity`/`TenTailsEntity` by tail count -> coords + dimension; else reports the sealed jinchuriki (`BijuManager.getJinchurikiUuid/Name`); else not-found.
  - `/locateentity jinchuriki list|revoke all|revoke <tails>|assign <player> <tails>`: maps to `BijuManager.listAssignments/revokeAll/revokeByTail/setPlayerAsJinchuriki`.
  - `/narutomax [targets]` (perm 2, NEW): sets `battle_experience` to 100000 (ninja level = sqrt(xp) ~= 316); sets `ChakraPathwaySystem` to `battle_experience*0.5` (= 50000, the `PlayerPathway` max); iterates `ModItems.all()` and for every `item instanceof JutsuItem` binds owner + enables and fully levels every `JutsuDefinition` via new `JutsuItem.maxOut`; also grants/maxes Eight Gates battle XP; syncs variables.
- Framework additions in `item/JutsuItem.java`:
  - `maxOut(stack, owner)`: `setOwner` + `setAffinity(true)` (so required XP isn't doubled) + per-`JutsuDefinition` `enableJutsu` + `setJutsuXp = getRequiredXp` + `onMaxedOut` hook.
  - `onMaxedOut(stack)` protected hook; `addXpToCurrentJutsu(stack, xp)` public wrapper over the protected `addCurrentJutsuXp`.
  - `item/NinjutsuItem.java`: `onMaxedOut` override sets `RasenganLearned` (inherited by `SenjutsuItem`), matching `JutsuLearning.unlock`.
- Verification:
  - `.\gradlew.bat "-Dnet.minecraftforge.gradle.check.certs=false" --no-daemon compileJava`: BUILD SUCCESSFUL (only the pre-existing `ItemBlockRenderTypes.setRenderLayer` and `IForgeItem.onArmorTick` deprecation warnings).
  - `python tools\validate_dedicated_server_safety.py`: 276 non-client Java files scanned, 0 client-reference issues (NarutoCommands is server-only).
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0.
- Coverage note: `/narutomax` currently maxes all 12 registered `JutsuItem` tomes (NINJUTSU, SENJUTSU, DOTON, FUTON, KATON, RAITON, SUITON, INTON, IRYO_JUTSU, SIX_PATH_SENJUTSU, TENSEIGAN_CHAKRA_MODE, SUMMONING_CONTRACT), all `AdvancedNatureJutsuItem` kekkei tomes (BAKUTON, FUTTON, HYOTON, JINTON, JITON, KEKKEI_MORA, MOKUTON, RANTON, SHAKUTON, SHIKOTSUMYAKU, YOOTON, YOTON) and Eight Gates. As more jutsu tomes gain real behavior, `/narutomax` covers them automatically because it is data-driven over `ModItems.all()`.

### M7 Jutsu Inventory Audit + Fidelity Fix Batch 1

- Ran a `jutsu-inventory` workflow (15 domains, throttled 3-at-a-time after a 16-wide fan-out tripped a transient server rate-limit). It wrote a per-jutsu porting checklist to `audit/jutsu_backlog/<domain>.md` + `INDEX.md`. Headline finding: the port is far more complete than "rough" implies — **159 techniques: 133 DONE (~84%), 20 PARTIAL (verify-level), 1 STUB (Gourd armor), 5 MISSING** (all dojutsu: Amaterasu activation + sneak-extinguish, Shinra Tensei, Ban Sho Ten'in, Hakke Kusho — single root cause = the dojutsu key-dispatch table never fires Rinnegan key1/key3 or Byakugan key1+sneak). So remaining work is a **fix-and-verify** plan, not a from-scratch port.
- Every audit "CONFIRMED" claim was re-verified against current code before acting (the audit is point-in-time and had at least one false positive — see Katon below).

- **FIX (mechanical, high-leverage): `Chakra.getChakraModifier` charge-speed curve.**
  - 1.12.2 truth (`1.12.2/.../Chakra.java:63-65`): `getChakraModifier(entity) = ProcedureUtils.getCDModifier(getLevel(entity))` where `getCDModifier(m) = 1/(0.5 + 0.02*m)` and `getLevel = sqrt(max(amount,max))`. 1.12.2's charge formula (`ItemJutsu.getModifier`/`getPower`, lines 156-162) multiplies the charge divisor by this.
  - Port regression: `getChakraModifier` returned `level<=0 ? 1.0 : max(0.05, 20/(level+20))` — a different curve (e.g. level 10: faithful 1.43 vs port 0.67; level 50: 0.67 vs 0.29), so charge speed diverged for EVERY charged jutsu. The faithful `ProcedureUtils.getCDModifier` already existed (`1.20.1/.../ProcedureUtils.java:520-521`, byte-identical to 1.12.2) but was unused.
  - Fix: `Chakra.getChakraModifier` now returns `ProcedureUtils.getCDModifier(getLevel(entity))` (added the import). No guard/floor (1.12.2 has none; denominator >= 0.5 so it is safe).

- **NO-OP (audit false positive): Katon firestream armor-bypass.**
  - Audit claimed `katon_firestream_damage` lacked the `bypasses_armor` tag. Verified: `data/minecraft/tags/damage_type/bypasses_armor.json` ALREADY lists `narutomod:katon_firestream_damage`, and `is_fire.json` lists it too. The audit only inspected the damage-type JSON, not the tag files. No change made; recorded so it is not re-chased.

- **FIX (mechanical): Futon Rasenshuriken -> Rasengan prerequisite gate.**
  - 1.12.2 truth (`ItemFuton.RangedItem.onUpdate`, lines 124-139): each tick, Rasenshuriken is auto-enabled iff the player can currently use Rasengan from their `ItemNinjutsu` tome (`canUseJutsu(stack, RASENGAN, player)`), else auto-disabled. The port `FutonItem` had no such logic.
  - Fix: added `FutonItem.inventoryTick` override mirroring it — finds the player's NINJUTSU stack via `ProcedureUtils.getMatchingItemStack(player, ModItems.NINJUTSU.get())` and toggles `RASENSHURIKEN` enable state from `ninjutsuItem.canPlayerUseJutsu(stack, NinjutsuItem.RASENGAN, player)`. Confirmed `NinjutsuItem` does not override `canUseJutsu`, so base semantics (creative || owned && enabled) match 1.12.2.
  - Framework: added public `JutsuItem.canPlayerUseJutsu(stack, definition, entity)` exposing the protected `canUseJutsu` for cross-tome gating.

- Verification:
  - `.\gradlew.bat "-Dnet.minecraftforge.gradle.check.certs=false" --no-daemon compileJava`: BUILD SUCCESSFUL (only the pre-existing `setRenderLayer`/`onArmorTick` deprecation warnings).
  - `python tools\validate_dedicated_server_safety.py`: 276 files scanned, 0 client-reference issues.
  - `python tools\validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0.
- Next batches (from `audit/jutsu_backlog/INDEX.md`): Batch 2 = Futon/Raiton verify + build the **Gourd armor STUB** + Jiton sand-`Type` system; Batch 3 (highest leverage) = port shared dojutsu bases (`ProcedureAirPunch`, `ProcedurePullAndHold`/`dislodgeBlocks`, spherical-explosion helper, centralized `ItemDojutsu.Base` Blindness penalty), fix the key-dispatch table, then implement the 5 MISSING dojutsu jutsu (Amaterasu +extinguish, Shinra Tensei, Ban Sho Ten'in, Hakke Kusho).

### M7 Fidelity Fix Batch 2 — Amaterasu Activation (MISSING jutsu #1 of 5)

- From `audit/jutsu_backlog/INDEX.md`: 1 of the 5 MISSING jutsu, all sharing the root cause that the dojutsu key-dispatch table never fired Mangekyo (Sasuke/Eternal) activation.
- 1.12.2 source ported: `ProcedureAmaterasu` (id 78) + `ProcedureAmaterasuPlaceBlock` (227) + `ProcedureAmaterasuExtinguishEntities` (95), originally dispatched on Special Jutsu Key 1.
- New `item/AmaterasuHandler.java`, wired into `network/SpecialJutsuKeyMessage.handle` dispatch chain (inserted after `ObitoKamuiHandler`, before `ByakuganHandler`). Claims Key 1 only when the head slot is `MANGEKYOSHARINGANHELMET` or `MANGEKYOSHARINGANETERNALHELMET` (Obito's helmet keeps Kamui via `ObitoKamuiHandler`; no overlap).
- Faithful behavior reproduced:
  - Key 1 is **edge-triggered** by the client (`ClientSpecialJutsuKeys`: send only on down-state change), unlike charge Key 3. So the original's per-tick "while held" ignite (it polled the key each tick) is reproduced from a server `PlayerTickEvent` gated on the `amaterasu_active` flag (same pattern as `ByakuganHandler.tick`). Press initializes (sound + full chakra + cooldown + active) and ignites once; the tick continues igniting; release ends it. A `LAST_IGNITE_TAG == gameTime` guard prevents a double-ignite on the press tick.
  - Ignite (each tick): `ProcedureUtils.objectEntityLookingAt(player, 30)` → if ENTITY, apply `AMATERASUFLAME` 10000t, amplifier `experienceLevel/30`; if BLOCK, place `AMATERASUBLOCK` at hitPos + face normal when air.
  - Sneak-release: raytrace (50) → clear FIRE (`BaseFireBlock`) + `AMATERASUBLOCK` in a 31×31×31 cube; remove `AMATERASUFLAME` + `clearFire()` on all living in a 15-grown AABB (1.12.2 `ExtinguishEntities`).
  - Normal release: Weakness II + Nausea (CONFUSION) 0 for `(cooldown - gameTime) * 0.5` ticks (self-penalty).
  - Constants from 1.12.2 `ItemMangekyoSharingan`: `AMATERASU_CHAKRA_USAGE = 100` (×2 non-owner), gate `>= usage*1.25`, full `usage` on activation + `usage*0.25` per ignite tick; cooldown `gameTime + cdMod*300`, then `+= cdMod*10` per tick while `cd-now < 2000`. `cdMod = ProcedureUtils.getCooldownModifier`. `sharingan_blinded` helmet-NBT gate preserved.
- Reused existing infra (confirmed, not re-created): `AMATERASUFLAME` effect (DOT — `AmaterasuFlameEffect.applyEffectTick` hurts with ninjutsu-fire), `AMATERASUBLOCK` (its `entityInside` applies `AMATERASUFLAME` + `setSecondsOnFire`, so painted blocks ignite walkers), `SOUND_AMATERASU2`, `ProcedureUtils.objectEntityLookingAt`/`getCooldownModifier`/`isOriginalOwner`.
- Verification:
  - `compileJava`: BUILD SUCCESSFUL (only the pre-existing `setRenderLayer`/`onArmorTick` deprecation warnings).
  - `validate_dedicated_server_safety.py`: 277 files scanned, 0 client-reference issues (AmaterasuHandler is server-only).
  - `validate_port_resources.py`: all issue counts 0.
- MISSING jutsu remaining: 4 — Shinra Tensei + Ban Sho Ten'in (Rinnegan/Tenseigan Key 1/Key 3) and Hakke Kūshō (Byakugan Key 1 + sneak; needs the `ProcedureAirPunch` cone base), plus the Gourd-armor STUB.

### M7 GitHub Repository Publication Slice

- Created the public GitHub repository `quasar2333/narutomod-1.20.1` for the Forge 1.20.1 port:
  - Repository URL: https://github.com/quasar2333/narutomod-1.20.1
  - Initial branch: `main`.
  - Initial source commit: `12a09d3` (`Publish Forge 1.20.1 port workspace`).
  - `1.12.2/` is recorded as a proper Git submodule pointing at `https://github.com/AHZNB/naruto_mod.git`, so the original mod reference stays linked without embedding a broken nested repository.
  - Added root `README.md`, `LICENSE`, `.gitignore`, `.gitattributes`, and `.gitmodules` for GitHub publication. The license notice preserves the original mod rule: source is public, forks/modifications are allowed, and monetization of the mod/forks/releases requires permission.
  - Build output, `.gradle`, runtime logs, crash reports, Python caches, and packaged jars are ignored from source control; the Gradle wrapper jar remains tracked so fresh clones can build.
- Published the first GitHub prerelease:
  - Release URL: https://github.com/quasar2333/narutomod-1.20.1/releases/tag/v0.2.10-beta.1%2Bmc1.20.1
  - Release title: `Naruto Mod 0.2.10-beta.1 for Forge 1.20.1`.
  - Uploaded artifact: `narutomod-0.2.10-beta.1+mc1.20.1.jar`, size 20,000,917 bytes after the release build.
- Verification before publication:

  - `python tools\validate_dedicated_server_safety.py`: 277 non-client Java files scanned, 0 client-reference issues.
  - `.\gradlew.bat "-Dnet.minecraftforge.gradle.check.certs=false" --no-daemon build`: passes.
